companyDescription:
We are bunq, bank of The Free. We’re here to break free from the status quo, and create a bank with our users in mind. Giving them the freedom to live life on their own terms.

We built our own financial system from the ground up, and with the launch of our app in 2015, reinvented banking as you know it. And that’s just the beginning! We work around the clock to create fintech innovations that simply make banking and money management as easy, transparent, and fun as possible. 

Have a business? 
bunq Business was created as the ultimate bank account to save you time. This means no more paperwork, branch visits or bureaucracy ever again. Just the freedom to focus on what’s truly important: running your business. 
Go to bunq.com/business and find out how we can help your business thrive. 

Want to join us in our mission to revolutionise the traditional banking industry? You will become part of an international team of more than 500 bunqers (48 nationalities and counting!) that all have one thing in common: we love what we do. Waking up with brilliant ideas and going to bed knowing that you’ve personally contributed to them. That’s working at bunq. 
If that sounds like your kind of challenge, go to bunq.com/jobs get the ball rolling.
companySlogan: We are bunq, bank of The Free. We're here to create a bank that helps our users save time, money and the environment.
descriptionHtml:
We’re building the bank of The Free – and our mission is to Make Life Easy for our users with fast, secure, and smart banking experiences 🌈 The Internal Audit team powers this mission by ensuring bunq is operating as effectively as possible, solving any invisible threats so we keep on delivering on our promise to our users, at all times.


Take ownership:

As an Internal Auditor for Growth, you’ll play a critical role in ensuring we’re always growing stronger by digging deep into bunq’s growth engine, so that everything runs smoothly for our users and the teams behind the scenes.

  • Identifying weak spots in the way we operate towards growth, before and as they emerge.
  • Turning complex findings into clear, prioritized, actionable recommendations that directly improve bunq’s growth while keeping our user’s trust and bunq’s speed intact.
  • Ensuring that corrective actions (CAP items) actually get implemented on time and that root causes are truly solved - not just patched over.


This challenge is perfect for you if

  • You have proven skills in auditing business growth levers - words like profitability models, customer acquisition costs, or brand metrics speak to you.
  • You have commercial accumen
  • You can turn data into insights, and break down complex financial, brand, or partnership data into actionable intelligence.
  • You understand how to strategize & prioritize between most long-term growth vs. urgent needs.
  • Applying Root Cause Analysis holds no secret to you, especially in high or medium risk scenarios tied to growth areas.
  • You’re able get teams across a company to act fast on recommendations, in our user’s best interest, always.
  • You’re familiar enough with AI oversight frameworks to review the use of AI in marketing, partnership, or product growth areas, spotting compliance or trust risks.


Your space to perform:

🤟 Join forces with great colleagues across the globe to truly make life easy for our users banking

🌟 Make lasting impact by working on complex & exciting challenges

💶 A gross monthly salary between €5,432 and €5,802 (based on a 32h work week) and the opportunity to receive a bonus up to 25% of your monthly salary each quarter, depending on your and bunq's performance.

👩 💻 Hybrid setup: after 3 months in-office, work 2 days remote, 3 days in-office weekly

🧳 Digital Nomad program: 1 week/quarter after 1 year, 3 weeks/quarter after 2 years

✈️ Carousel program: work from other bunq offices based on your performance and tenure

📚 Accelerate your career growth with bunq Academy and €1500 annual learning budget

🏖️ 24 days of paid leave after onboarding additional 3 days of study leave for personal development

🚴 Urban Sports Club discount to stay fit and healthy

🚌 Contribution to travel expenses— whether you walk, bike, take the bus, or drive (we love green choices 🌳)

💻 A MacBook while you're with us so you can Get Shit Done

🥗 Fresh, delicious lunches from our in-house chefs (vegan & vegetarian options too!)

💰 Optional pension plan with monthly contributions from bunq

💸 Monthly contributions to your phone and internet bills

🍻 Friday drinks and other bunq-style celebrations

Check out our open roles to become part of the world of Red Bull. Most jobs take energy, ours give it. Check out our open roles to become part of the world of Red Bull – jobs.redbull.com
companySlogan: Red Bull Gives Wings to People and Ideas.
descriptionHtml:
Wil jij weten welk bedrijf schuilt achter het merk en blikje Red Bull? Ben jij een energiek Finance talent met oog voor detail? Making the impossible, possible" maakt Red Bull zo succesvol. Red Bull Nederland is een jonge, snelgroeiende organisatie waar we vleugels geven aan onze medewerkers. Met een omzet van bijna 270 mio zijn we marktleider in de categorie energiedranken.

De afdeling Finance is verantwoordelijk voor het financiële reilen en zeilen van Red Bull in Nederland. Met elf mensen vervult deze afdeling een ultieme “partner in business” rol in de organisatie.

Binnen Finance is de afdeling Accounting onder meer verantwoordelijk voor het opstellen van de jaarrekening, de cashflow forecasting, balansbeheer en naleving van (fiscale) wet- en regelgeving. Als Financial Accountant speel je hier een belangrijke rol in. Daarnaast werk je veel samen met team Business Control. Je hebt namelijk ook diverse Financial Control verantwoordelijkheden en daarmee ben je de ultieme linking pin binnen de afdeling Finance. Je opereert zowel zelfstandig als ondersteunend en rapporteert rechtstreeks aan de Head of Accounting & Taxes.

Alle verantwoordelijkheden die we jou toevertrouwen:

FINANCIAL ACCOUNTING

Je verzorgt samen met de Head of Accounting & Taxes de Accounting maandafsluiting en jaarafsluiting en ondersteunt team Control bij het technische gedeelte hiervan Je voert regelmatig de betaalrun uit, je zorgt ervoor dat de juiste bedragen op tijd op de juiste rekening worden gestort Je verzorgt samen met de Head of Accounting & Taxes de interim- en eindejaarscontrole en helpt bij het opstellen van de jaarrekening Je voert periodiek de liquiditeitsprognose uit, plant en optimaliseert de werkkapitaalpositie Je verzorgt belastingaangiften en houdt jezelf op de hoogte van wijzigingen in wet- en regelgeving en nieuwe eisen binnen dit vakgebied. Je hebt een adviserende rol op het gebied van belastingen binnen de organisatie Je zorgt maandelijks voor een tijdige en sluitende statiegeldaangifte en leidt de jaarlijkse accountantscontrole voor statiegeld Indien nodig fungeer je als back-up voor je Accounting collega's tijdens hun afwezigheid

FINANCIAL CONTROL

Je bent verantwoordelijk voor verschillende financiële rapportages. Denk hierbij aan het opstellen en interpreteren van rapportages voor RB hoofdkantoor (o.a. Balans & Cashflow rapportages), rapportages voor het CBS en verzekeringen. Daarnaast stel je verschillende rapporten op voor de financiële afdeling en deel je deze binnen het bedrijf. Je verschaft waardevolle inzichten op basis van feiten, cijfers en analyses om verbeteringen en/of efficiency te identificeren, te prioriteren en te implementeren Tijdens de Business Planning en de Planning & Control cyclus (inclusief maand- en jaarafsluiting) vervul je een belangrijke coördinerende taak Diverse verbeterprojecten op het snijvlak van Finance en business. Samen met het Finance team werk je continu aan het verbeteren van bestaande processen in de organisatie. Je ziet mogelijkheden voor het inrichten van nieuwe processen en/of systemen en je helpt bij de implementatie van (Finance) projecten Je zorgt voor een overzicht van en inzicht in de aanwezige voorzieningen (balans) en hun onderbouwing (P&L), inclusief Trade Spends

DATA QUALITY

Binnen Red Bull werken we met CPM, dit staat voor Customer Profitability Management. Dit systeem geeft o.a. inzicht in de spends per klant. In jouw rol ben je verantwoordelijk voor CPM Data Quality, verzorg je rapportages vanuit het systeem, draag je bij aan de volledigheid van de beschikbare data en voer je diverse werkzaamheden uit tijdens de maandafsluiting Je bent verantwoordelijk voor account- en masterdata management. Je zorgt ervoor dat je collega’s toegang hebben tot de juiste systemen en coderingen en zorgt hierbij dat voldaan wordt aan alle richtlijnen van ons hoofdkantoor Je bent verantwoordelijk voor het Contractmanagementsysteem, het beheer en onderhoud van de contracten, waarbij je ook zorgt dat de contracten juist en volledig vertaald worden in de systemen

Jouw kennis- en expertisegebieden wat het belangrijkste is voor deze baan:

  • Je hebt een HBO/WO opleiding afgerond en 3-5 jaar werkervaring in een finance positie
  • Je bent goed in Excel en houdt van cijfers
  • Kennis van een SAP systeem is een pré
  • Je hebt oog voor detail, een sterk ontwikkeld analytisch vermogen en bent een zelfstarter die dit analytisch vermogen aanwendt om op zoek te gaan naar verbeteringen en/of efficientie
  • Je bent communicatief vaardig naar zowel externe partijen als collega’s. Je hebt graag contact met anderen en bent nieuwsgierig naar het grotere plaatje
  • Je bent proactief en in staat om onder druk en in een snelle omgeving gestructureerd en nauwkeurig te blijven werken
  • Je krijgt energie van het behalen van resultaten en het leveren van kwaliteit. Hierbij kijk je voortdurend naar manieren waarop het nog beter kan en geeft daar ook zelf invulling aan

Red Bull Nederland gelooft erin dat het hebben van een diverse pool van talenten een belangrijke bijdrage levert aan ons succes. We nemen daarom aan op ervaring, skills en mindset. We staan open voor sollicitaties van iedereen uit onze maatschappij ongeacht leeftijd, geslacht, handicap, seksuele geaardheid, ras of religie.

companySlogan: REDEFINING QUALITY
descriptionHtml:
Over jouw rol

Als Lead Auditor Safety binnen onze Industry & Facilities divisie voer je zelfstandig of in teamverband audits uit bij onze klanten. Je beoordeelt of klanten voldoen aan verschillende (inter)nationale normen en of ze zich bezighouden met het continue verbeteren van hun processen. Afhankelijk van jouw expertise voer je audits uit op het gebied van ISO 45001, VCA en de Safety Culture Ladder (SCL.) Binnen het team ben je verantwoordelijk voor de praktische organisatie van de audit (zoals de auditplanning, logistiek etc.), de uitvoering en het tijdig indienen van de rapportage.

Kortom, een uitdagende en diverse job, waarbij je jouw kennis kunt delen en veel verschillende mensen ontmoet binnen verschillende bedrijven.

Versterk ons team om samen een wereld van vertrouwen vorm te geven!

Wat ga je doen

  • Je voert audits uit bij verschillende (internationale) key accounts met een diverse klantenportefeulle
  • Voor deze key accounts ben jij verantwoordelijk voor het volledige auditproces, inclusief de verzamelde informatie die wordt aangeleverd door jouw auditteams.
  • Je volgt non-conformiteiten o.b.v. bevindingen die tijdens audits zijn geconstateerd, tot de klant een passende corrigerende maatregel heeft genomen.
  • Je zorgt dat de auditteams werken binnen de geldende accreditatieregels.

Wat breng je mee

  • Hogere veiligheidskundige (HVK)
  • Minstens 10 jaar werkervaring in het vakgebied veiligheid waarvan minimaal 5 jaar in een zware sector zoals Bouw, Chemie of Afval.
  • Ervaring met implementeren en/of (intern) auditeren van ISO 45001, VCA en/of SCL is een pré
  • Sterke communicatieve vaardigheden
  • Je bent zelfstandig ingesteld, stressbestendig en hebt een hands-on mentaliteit|
  • Je bent enthousiast, vlot in de omgang en je staat gekend om je overtuigingskracht
  • Je bent behendig met Office 365 en in het aanleren van computersystemen
  • Je bent in het bezit van een rijbewijs B, bent bereid om te reizen en wekelijks te overnachten

Wat bieden wij jou

  • Een salaris dat past bij je verantwoordelijkheden en ervaring.
  • Een beschikbare leaseauto (gedurende het eerste jaar.)
  • Thuiswerkvergoeding van €2,40 netto per dag en thuiswerkmeubilair.
  • Onkostenvergoeding van € 85,- netto per maand.
  • 24,5 vakantiedagen per jaar en 3 ATV dagen aangevuld met eventuele senioriteitsdagen o.b.v. leeftijd (op basis van een 40-urige werkweek.)
  • Pensioenregeling gebaseerd op een beschikbare premieregeling
  • Collectieve ziektekostenverzekering (optioneel.)
  • Opleidingsmogelijkheden en (persoonlijke) ontwikkeling.

Waarom Werken bij Bureau Veritas

Elk jaar verwelkomen en trainen we meer dan 13.000 toegewijde, getalenteerde en zeer bekwame ingenieurs, technici, inspecteurs, auditors en ondersteunende medewerkers over de hele wereld.

Onze inclusieve, samenwerkingsgerichte, missie gedreven omgeving zorgt ervoor dat jouw unieke talenten bijdragen aan onze missie om een betere wereld vorm te geven.

Deel uitmaken van de BV-familie betekent dat je jouw stempel kunt drukken op het vormgeven van een wereld van vertrouwen.

Wil je deel uitmaken van Bureau Veritas? Kom ons team versterken!

Toegewijd aan een Inclusieve Werkplek

Bureau Veritas zet zich in voor het creëren van een inclusieve werkomgeving die zo divers is als de samenleving waarin we actief zijn. We discrimineren niet op basis van etniciteit, religie, geslacht, achtergrond, sociaaleconomische status, leeftijd, seksuele oriëntatie, burgerlijke staat, medische aandoening, handicap, politieke overtuiging, genderidentiteit of enige andere wettelijk beschermde status.

Wij streven ernaar om een inclusieve cultuur te bevorderen waarin mensen zichzelf kunnen zijn, een gevoel van verbondenheid ervaren bij Bureau Veritas en gedijen, omdat "iedereen een talent kan zijn" op basis van prestaties en gedrag dat in lijn is met onze waarden.

Heb je vragen over de rol? Neem dan contact op met onze Corporate Recruiter Chaouki Touzani via +31 6 42288925 of mail naar chaouki.touzani@bureauveritas.com

companyDescription:
Together We Build

Building Materials Europe (BME) - headquartered at Schiphol -The Netherlands - is one of Europe’s leading and rapidly growing B2B distributors of building materials. BME has leading market positions across the Netherlands, Belgium, Germany, France, Switzerland, Spain, and Portugal. They connect a broad network of suppliers to over 275.000 customers, primarily small to medium-sized (SME) contractors and installers, mainly serving the residential and renovation markets. 

BME offers best-in-class service to its customers and suppliers, enabling them to concentrate on their core operations. Leveraging its scale and expertise, they strive to be a key driver of sustainability in the building materials industry. BME employs over 13,000 employees across more than 940 locations, generating revenues of €5 billion in 2024.

Together We Build – Close Proximity | Trusted Partners | Leading Scale | Sustainably Committed

For more information, please visit: www.bme-group.com
descriptionHtml:
Group Manager Audit & Risk


We’re seeking someone who can design and deliver a modern, risk-based internal audit program across Europe and act as a trusted advisor on internal control maturity and ERM.


On one hand, you’re a hands-on internal auditor: building the annual risk-based plan with the Group Director Audit & Risk, scoping and executing financial and operational audits end-to-end, embedding IT-audit techniques, data analytics, and elements of continuous auditing, and aligning coverage with external auditors.


On the other hand, you’re a controls and ERM advisor: standardising and strengthening key controls and RCMs, running control self-assessments and targeted testing, tracking remediation, coaching Operating Companies on practical control design, and facilitating ERM activities (risk identification, assessment, reporting, and appetite alignment) that help OpCo’s mature.


Fluent in English and at least one of the following: Dutch, German, or French. Proficiency in two or all three is strongly preferred.


Hybrid policy, three days on-site at Schiphol-Oost, two days home-office, with the willingness to travel 20-40% throughout Europe.


Please note that this position is only open to residents already living in the Netherlands. BME does not provide Visa-sponsorship or relocation.

descriptionHtml:
At CoolGames and Keesing Media Group we are looking for a versatile Financial Accountant to join our Finance team(s) in Amsterdam (fulltime: two locations). You will play a key role in maintaining our financial administrations and reporting processes and contribute to the success of a growing and dynamic company.

About CoolGames And Keesing Media Group

At CoolGames, we're proud to be a leading international HTML5 game studio, working with some of the world's biggest ‘Big Tech' game platforms, such as Netflix, Discord, Facebook and Microsoft Play. We also work with famous games IP such as Battleship, Angry Birds and Monopoly.

CoolGames is part of Keesing, the world's largest Braintainment company. Keesing is a financially strong publisher of engaging sudokus, crosswords and many other puzzles & games with a presence in over 20 countries headquartered in The Netherlands.

At Keesing, it's all about smart fun—from puzzles to games. With 16 offices across 13 countries, we're constantly evolving in both print and digital content.

CoolGames is the instant games division, where we're building the future of gaming: available anytime, anywhere, with no downloads required.

With the CoolGames HQ in Amsterdam, supported by our global remote teams, we offer market-competitive salaries, the chance to contribute to successful casual games that are played by millions and work directly with some of the biggest brands in the world. We believe in creativity, collaboration and delivering world-class gaming experiences, while giving you the choice to either join our Amsterdam studio or become part of our remote teams.

If you want to grow your career in a profitable, internationally recognized company, work closely with world-famous partners and be part of a highly professional international team - CoolGames is the right place for you.

About The Role

  • As Financial Accountant, you'll be responsible for a wide range of finance and accounting tasks, including:
  • Managing the General Ledger, Accounts Payable, Accounts Receivable, and Revenue Statements
  • Preparing and filing VAT returns and maintaining fixed asset records
  • Supporting monthly and quarterly closings, including balance sheet reconciliations
  • Creating and maintaining process documentation and ensuring accuracy of source records
  • Contributing to finance-related projects, analyses, and other assignments to support the department's goals

Requirements

About you

  • 3+ years of experience in a similar financial/accounting role
  • A MBO+ or HBO/Bachelor's degree in Accounting, Finance, or Business Administration
  • Fluent in English (written and spoken) — a must, Dutch is a great plus
  • Solid knowledge of Dutch GAAP; IFRS experience is a plus
  • Experience with Twinfield, Business Central and Basecone is a bonus
  • Strong Excel skills (pivot tables, formulas, reporting)
  • A hands-on, proactive attitude and a genuine sense of ownership
  • Detail-oriented, structured, and quick to learn
  • A collaborative team player — but also confident working independently
  • And most importantly: you bring positive energy to the workplace and enjoy what you do!

Benefits

What we can offer you* (for studio HQ roles)

  • At CoolGames we are an international team of 50+ professionals from almost 20 different countries and at Keesing Amsterdam we work with 150 professionals;
  • Work closely with some of the biggest (upcoming) game platforms in the world (Netflix, Facebook, Microsoft, Samsung, discord and more) on high-quality casual games that are played by millions of gamers;
  • Competitive remuneration, including health care insurance, travel costs compensation, a company pension plan and a sport allowance;
  • Market salary;
  • A flexible remote working environment for parts of the week;
  • A very open, informal and international company culture where everyone can make a difference.

* EU candidates only *

companyDescription:
JOINSON&SPICE is the first in the Netherlands to specialise in auditing and accounting of tech companies. Why? Because that’s where our interest lies, which makes for a natural curiosity towards our customers’ businesses. Which is essential, if you ask us, for a good accountant.

Highly educated
Based on the thought that we are level with our customers, form the first day you are seated with experienced and well-educated accountants. We truly want to add value. This value is not simply found in printing reports or typing in some numbers.

Innovation and attention
Therefore we bring together knowledge and experience through the cloud. This way, with proven, innovative means you have control over the financial management of your business. We stay on alert for new opportunities and arising threats. After all, that’s what you get an accountant for.

Award-winning company
The success of our focus on innovation is highlighted by the awards we received. Recently we won the Exact Innovation Award 2018/2019, were we finalists in the international Though Leadership Initiative of the year and with a spot on the FD Gazellen 2017 list, named one of the fastest-growing small businesses. And in 2014 we won the Exact Cloud Accountant Award, with which Exact called us the ‘most innovative accountant of 2014’. 

Interested in how we can spice up your business? Take a look at https://joinsonandspice.nl or contact one of our employees for a meeting and a cup of coffee.
companySlogan: Kom ook werken bij het innovatiefste kantoor van NL! De audit scale-up in Tech. Het #accountantskantoorvandetoekomst
descriptionHtml:
At JOINSON&SPICE, we work with leading tech companies and strongly believe in a data-driven approach, using our in-house developed software to deliver outstanding results. Innovation really does literally flow through our veins. Our team is growing (we aim to triple in size by 2028), and we work with fast-growing scale-up clients in Tech, both national and international who need more than just compliance — they need Audit Staff who think with them.

🧩 Your Day

Your day kicks off with a tasteful cup of tea or coffee. And then the team sync, where you review audit planning for a fast-growing international client. You're working on revenue recognition this week — starting to learn the client’s accounting principles and then start digging into ledgers, preparing tests of controls, and making sure the numbers add up as you’d expect. You collaborate closely with your colleagues and learn to ask the right questions early, because curiosity is how we add value as auditors.

Mid-morning, you help prep for a client meeting. Together with a senior team member, you walk through the agenda, the audit timeline, flag open items, and clarify key issues. The client appreciates your structured thinking and sharp eye for detail. Off course you draft a smashing summary of the call and set out the follow-up.

After lunch, you finalise documentation, you check the ‘definition of done’ and send it off for review. You get thoughtful feedback — and you’re encouraged to challenge inspiring ideas, not just follow checklists. Before the day ends, you support a junior team member who’s stuck on a reconciliation — and you realise how much you’ve already grown in your role.

You close your laptop feeling energised. You’re not just verifying numbers — you’re building your judgment, confidence, and professional voice, one audit at a time. Ready to go home with a smile on your face.

✅ What You’ll Do

  • Support audit engagements from planning through wrap-up
  • Perform audit procedures: control testing, analytical reviews, and substantive testing
  • Document findings and help ensure compliance with relevant standards
  • Communicate clearly with clients and internal teams
  • Spot risks, ask smart questions, write draft management letter points to show our added value to clients. And, yes, think beyond the checklist
  • Contribute to knowledge-sharing, peer coaching, and improving internal processes


🧠 What You Bring

  • A completed or ongoing HBO or WO degree in Accountancy, Finance, or a related field
  • 2+ years of experience in audit (we're hiring across levels, from junior to senior)
  • A sharp analytical mindset and structured way of working
  • Good communication skills in English (Dutch is a plus)
  • Proven curiosity, initiative, and a drive to continuously improve
  • A collaborative spirit — you like being part of a team and supporting others


🌱 What We Offer

  • 27 vacation days to truly unwind—with the option to buy more if you need extra time to recharge
  • Premium-free pension — fully covered by us, with a personalised consultation to help you understand and tailor your plan to fit your needs (and your family’s)
  • Education & Training – an excellent study program plus a personal annual training budget to support your growth
  • Flexibility: hybrid working, a great office in Amsterdam, and a healthy work-life balance
  • An open, diverse team with real collaboration
  • And yes, we do what we promise: you’ll stick to a healthy workweek within your contractual hours.

companyDescription:
Since our founding in 1935, TAPI has been at the forefront of advancing health through innovation and dedication. Today we continue to build on this strong legacy, driving progress in the pharmaceutical industry. Our commitment to health is unwavering, from developing high-quality generics to pioneering cutting-edge solutions.

With our longstanding experience and worldclass experts, we provide one of the industry's most comprehensive API portfolios. We also offer customized CDMO services, utilizing our expertise in a wide range of technologies to meet the diverse needs of our partners, ensuring flexibility and excellence in every project.

Every ingredient we create contributes to a healthier life, and every customer we supply fulfills their mission to cure. Join us in our journey to improve global health, one ingredient at a time.
companySlogan: Technology & API Services
descriptionHtml:
Company Description

At TAPI, we’re not just a company—we’re a community committed to advancing health from the core. As the world’s leading supplier of active pharmaceutical ingredients (APIs), we partner with 80% of the top 50 global pharmaceutical companies. With a legacy spanning over 80 years and a portfolio of more than 350 products, as well as custom CDMO services, we’re shaping the future of health worldwide.

Our strength lies in our people—a team of over 4,200 professionals across 13 state-of-the-art facilities in Italy, Hungary, the Czech Republic, Croatia, Israel, Mexico, and India. Together, we innovate, solve problems, and deliver excellence. Join us and be part of a mission that transforms lives.

Job Description

We are seeking a highly skilled and detail-oriented Senior Financial Controller to manage the financial operations of two Dutch legal entities within our global group structure. Based in the Netherlands, this role is critical to ensuring accurate and timely financial reporting, compliance with local regulations, and alignment with global finance standards. The ideal candidate brings strong technical expertise, sound judgment, and the ability to operate independently while collaborating with international and local stakeholders. We offer a flexible hybrid work setup, with 2–3 days per week spent on-site in our Amsterdam office.

Key Responsibilities

Financial Reporting & Compliance

  • Oversee the month-end, quarter-end, and year-end close processes for both Dutch legal entities in accordance with Dutch GAAP and IFRS.
  • Prepare and file statutory financial statements and coordinate with external auditors to ensure timely and accurate audit completion.
  • Maintain accurate and complete financial records and ensure consistency across both entities.

Local Tax and Regulatory Compliance

  • Ensure timely and accurate filing of all required tax returns (including VAT, CIT, and other statutory filings) for both companies.
  • Monitor regulatory developments and assess their impact on both legal entities.

Internal Controls & Risk Management

  • Implement and maintain effective internal controls and ensure compliance with group policies, including SOX (if applicable).
  • Identify and mitigate financial risks while ensuring proper documentation and audit trails.
  • Support both internal and external audit processes across the entities.

Financial Planning & Analysis

  • Provide financial insights and analysis to support business decision-making for both entities.
  • Prepare budgets, forecasts, and variance analyses.
  • Partner with local operational teams and global finance on key initiatives.

Cash Management & Treasury

  • Monitor and manage liquidity for both entities, including bank reconciliations and cash flow forecasting.
  • Coordinate with group treasury to ensure alignment on funding and foreign exchange matters.

Stakeholder Engagement

  • Serve as the primary finance contact for local management, external service providers, tax advisors, and auditors.
  • Collaborate closely with global finance functions, including consolidation, tax, and legal teams.

Qualifications

  • Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Finance/ Accounting
  • Recognized accounting certification (RA, CPA, ACCA, or equivalent)
  • 4+ years of relevant experience in a multinational environment, including multi-entity management
  • Strong knowledge of Dutch GAAP, IFRS, and Dutch tax regulations
  • Experience working with ERP systems (Priority advantage); advanced Excel skills
  • Fluent in English, both written and spoken (Dutch advantage)

Key Competencies

  • Ability to manage complexity across multiple legal entities
  • Managerial skills
  • Excellent attention to detail, with strong analytical and problem-solving abilities
  • Proactive, hands-on approach with a high sense of accountability
  • Strong interpersonal skills and ability to communicate effectively across levels and cultures
  • Organized, self-driven, and capable of working independently in a fast-paced environment

Additional Information

Make Your Mark with TAPI

Your journey with TAPI is more than a job—it’s an opportunity to make a lasting impact on global health. If you’re ready to lead, innovate, and inspire, we’re excited to welcome you to our team. Together, let’s shape the future of pharmaceuticals. Apply on top of this page and our talent acquisition team will be in touch soon!

companyDescription:
Renaix is an international recruitment and executive search consultancy specialising in placing senior management, finance, and audit professionals.

We place talented finance managers, corporate auditors, financial analysts, financial controllers and senior management at Fortune 500 companies, top European multinationals, and significant financial services institutions. With a network spanning Europe, the Americas, Asia, and MEA, our assignments range from introducing a newly qualified auditor to placing a country or regional CFO.

Celebrating our 25th anniversary in 2022, we’ve built a deep understanding of the international finance industry and nurtured an unrivalled network of clients and candidates around the globe. Our global footprint means our consultants have extensive knowledge of local laws and regulations and an intuitive grasp of the cultural differences that influence role requirements.

descriptionHtml:
We are recruiting for an Internal Auditor for our client, a global leading manufacturing multinational. The role sits within an audit function with responsibility for Operational, Financial and Compliance Audits, based in the Maastricht region – The Netherlands.


Sitting within the Corporate Audit Group, the audit assignments will involve planning, fieldwork, documentation, presentation and reporting phases. You will continually build partnerships between Corporate Audit and the various functional areas and business units throughout the Company.


Internal Auditor Job Responsibilities:

  • Scope and plan audits and projects based on a thorough risk assessment for the location
  • Meet with representatives from Corporate Finance, Tax, and Legal to identify any additional areas that should be considered for inclusion in the scope of the audit
  • Tailor the overall audit programme and detailed steps for the audit to correspond with the results of the risk assessment and functional area partnering
  • Conduct process walk-through meetings and data gathering activities to ensure a sound understanding of business processes and potential measurements to aid in the identification of testing approaches and key risk areas
  • Collect, analyse, test, and interpret information to assess and conclude each control testing requirement. For controls failing to test, assess the financial risk and impact to the organisation
  • Communicate audit results to management in a clear and concise manner, including any issues and recommendations for corrective action or improvement where applicable


Profile:

  • Ideally, you will have a Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance or Business
  • Preferably you will have come from a Big4 background with Audit and Data Analytics experience
  • Ideally, you will be qualified with 3-5 years of work experience
  • You will have an excellent command of English and additional European language skills preferred (Italian, Spanish, French, Dutch, German or Czech would be useful)
  • Happy traveling up to 30% of the time


If you are interested, please send a copy of your up-to-date CV to Gareth Mortimer – gareth@renaix.com (Vacancy ref: 28005).

For controls failing to test, assess the financial risk and impact to the organisationCommunicate audit results to management in a clear and concise manner, including any issues and recommendations for corrective action or improvement where applicableProfile:Ideally, you will have a Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance or BusinessPreferably you will have come from a Big4 background with Audit and Data Analytics experienceIdeally, you will be qualified with 3-5 years of work experienceYou will have an excellent command of English and additional European language skills preferred (Italian, Spanish, French, Dutch, German or Czech would be useful)Happy traveling up to 30% of the timeIf you are interested, please send a copy of your up-to-date CV to Gareth Mortimer – gareth@renaix.com (Vacancy ref: 28005).

A Notified Body in the Netherlands are rapidly expanding their Audit teams as a result of a large project acquisition. This Notified Body has numerous subsidiaries across Europe and offers world renowned, fully funded training programs to attain all of the necessary Certificates.

·Fluent in English and professional knowledge of Dutch

·Excellent verbal and communication skills


Benefits


·Work for a Leading Notified Body

·Excellent fully funded training programs

.Regular salary reviews once onboarded

companyDescription:
At Crocs, Inc., we believe life’s too short for uncomfortable shoes. As the global force behind two iconic brands — Crocs and HEYDUDE — we’re redefining casual footwear for people everywhere. Whether it’s the unmistakable Classic Clog or the laid-back ease of a HEYDUDE slip-on, our products are designed to move with you, wherever life takes you.

We’re more than just shoes. We’re about comfort without compromise, style with personality, and making a positive impact while we’re at it. Our brands are for all seasons, all ages, and all walks of life — and our commitment to personalization and self-expression keeps us connected to our consumer, one pair at a time.
descriptionHtml:
At Crocs, Inc., every career offers a chance to make a real impact. No two journeys look the same. And that's exactly how we like it. Whether you’re welcoming customers into our stores, collaborating with global teams at our headquarters, or keeping operations moving at our distribution centers, your impact is real and valued. At Crocs, Inc. you’re not expected to fit a mold. You’re encouraged to break it and create something better.

Overview

The Accountant of Crocs, Inc., is responsible for accounting and statutory compliance of Crocs and HEYDUDE entities. This includes maintenance of an accurate and timely system of accounting which includes a comprehensive set of internal controls, preparing periodic financial reports, working with auditors on the financial statements, and supporting the business by providing reliable data. You will be part of the of the Global Accounting team in Hoofddorp, the Netherlands – closely collaborating with accounting and other stakeholders within the global Crocs, Inc. organization.

What You'll Do

  • Monthly, quarterly, and year-end closing procedures.
  • Journal entry preparation and review.
  • Maintain ledgers based on US GAAP.
  • Preparation of monthly variance analysis including correlation to results and interaction with operational business units.
  • Preparation of audit documentation, account analysis, annual financial statements and related statutory reports for both US GAAP and local statutory requirement to support timely completion of audits.
  • Collaborate with shared service partners and other third-party business support partners.

What You'll Bring to the Table

  • HBO or University degree in Accounting or related fields.
  • Minimum 3 years of experience in related fields.
  • Solid understanding of Accounting, Internal Control and Reporting.
  • Background knowledge in ERP systems, preferably SAP.
  • Strong Excel skillset a must, experience in BI is preferred.
  • Ability to communicate effectively in English, both verbal and writing.
  • Experience within a US environment, including SOx is preferred.

The Company is an Equal Opportunity Employer committed to a diverse and inclusive work environment.

All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, or disability, or any other protected classification.

Job Category: Corporate

descriptionHtml:
Internal Auditor – GGZ inGeest – Amsterdam

(Dutch speaking required)

Salaris: max €90.000 obv 36 uur


GGZ inGeest biedt psychische gezondheidszorg vanuit 12 locaties in Amsterdam, Amstelveen, Bennebroek, Haarlem en Hoofddorp. Met ruim 1900 medewerkers werk je in een dynamische organisatie. Als Internal Auditor maak je deel uit van de afdeling Financiën, waar je samenwerkt met ongeveer 100 collega’s uit verschillende teams, zoals financiële administratie, business intelligence & datascience, zorgverkoop, zorgadministratie, logistieke ondersteuning en business control.


Als Internal Auditor ben je een sleutelfiguur in het waarborgen van een solide financieel beleid en een goed beheerde organisatie. Je ontwikkelt, onderhoudt en bewaakt de kaders en hulpmiddelen voor risicomanagement, interne beheersing en audits, en zorgt ervoor dat alle processen voldoen aan geldende wet- en regelgeving. Je hebt een scherp oog voor risico’s binnen de bedrijfsvoering. Door grondige analyses en heldere rapportages geef je waardevolle inzichten aan de Raad van Bestuur, directie en management. Als onafhankelijk adviseur signaleer je niet alleen verbeterkansen, maar draag je ook actief bij aan structurele optimalisaties.


Naast het opstellen en vormgeven van beleid zorg je voor een soepele implementatie en draagvlak binnen de organisatie. In jouw rol als adviseur en sparringpartner breng je risico’s in kaart en help je effectieve beheersmaatregelen te ontwikkelen. Je bent de aanjager van compliance en risicomanagement en weet dit breed te verankeren binnen de organisatie. Jaarlijks stel je het Interne Controleplan op en bewaaak je de uitvoering en stuur je bij waar nodig zodat de organisatie grip houdt op haar processen. Daarnaast ben je een belangrijke schakel in de samenwerking met externe partijen, zoals accountants en toezichthouders. Door actief deel te nemen aan overleggen zorg je ervoor dat de organisatie goed voorbereid is op toekomstige ontwikkelingen.


In het kort:

  • Beleid & compliance: je ontwikkelt, implementeert en bewaakt financieel beleid, risicomanagement en interne beheersing;
  • Risicoanalyse & advies: je signaleert en analyseert risico’s en adviseert Raad van Bestuur, directie en management;
  • Implementatie & draagvlak: je begeleidt de uitvoering van beleid en stimuleert risicobewustzijn binnen de organisatie;
  • Controle & naleving: je voert jaarlijks een risicoanalyse uit, stelt het Interne Controleplan op en bewaakt de voortgang;
  • Samenwerking & toezicht: je onderhoudt contacten met accountants, toezichthouders en neemt deel aan relevante overleggen.


Wie ben jij?

  • Je hebt een afgeronde WO opleiding richting bedrijfseconomie of accountancy, bij voorkeur aangevuld met een afgeronde audit-opleiding (RO, RE auditor);
  • Je hebt minimaal 7 jaar ervaring in een functie als auditor of op het gebied van risicomanagement, bij voorkeur in de zorg;
  • Je hebt een scherp oog voor risico’s, kunt complexe financiële en compliance-vraagstukken doorgronden en weet deze helder te vertalen naar concrete adviezen;
  • Je bent een onafhankelijke sparringpartner die zowel gevraagd als ongevraagd advies geeft en stakeholders op alle niveaus weet te overtuigen van verbeteringen en risico-aanpak.


Wat krijg je daarvoor terug?

  • Je krijgt direct een vast dienstverband;
  • De functie is ingeschaald in FWG 60/65 cao ggz. Deze loopt tot max 6504 obv 36 uur;
  • Een vaste 13e maand;
  • Verdere voorwaarden die van toepassing zijn binnen de CAO.


Enthousiast geworden? Neem voor meer informatie contact op met Maud Olislagers via 06-14460693 of via maud.olislagers@greenbergnielsen.nl.

companyDescription:
As a specialized company dedicated to ophthalmology, Santen carries out research, development, marketing, and sales of pharmaceuticals, over-the-counter products, and medical devices. Santen is the market leader for prescription ophthalmic pharmaceuticals in Japan and its products now reach patients in over 60 countries and regions. With scientific knowledge and organizational capabilities nurtured over a 130-year history, Santen provides products and services to contribute to the well-being of patients, their loved ones and consequently to society.

Santen Pharmaceutical Official Social Media Global Terms of Use​

descriptionHtml:
Company Description

Santen is a specialized life sciences company with a proud 130-year heritage focused exclusively on ophthalmology. As a Japan-originated, global company with our footprint in over 60 countries, we are dedicated to delivering innovative treatments and digital solutions for eye health and addressing vision-related social issues for people around the world.

Job Description

Are you passionate about driving excellence in pharmaceutical quality management? Join Santen, a global leader in ophthalmology, and take ownership of quality oversight for our strategic suppliers across the EMEA regions. We are looking for a Director, Supplier Quality to join our team in Amsterdam or Paris.(Hybrid 2x per week in the office)

In this pivotal role, you will be the go-to expert for all quality-related matters and you will ensure compliance with internal standards and external regulations (GxP, ISO, etc.), whilst building strong partnerships with suppliers to uphold the highest quality standards.

What You Will Do

  • Act as the primary Quality point of contact within the Global Quality organization for all strategic materials (APIs, excipients, intermediates, packaging).
  • Ensure the Quality function is equipped with the right capabilities, structure, and expertise to support business needs.
  • Foster a culture of continuous improvement and quality excellence across the organization.
  • Lead and drive the quality management of all allocated strategic suppliers, during their entire lifecycle, ensuring:
    • The development of a robust supplier quality strategy and its translation into tactical plans, including optimal and standardized operational models between Santen and its network of suppliers
    • An updated, consolidated and approved list of strategic suppliers, along with their characteristics and risk profile, is available at all times
    • Effective and efficient quality oversight of the global supplier network
    • Timely and successful resolution of any quality event to prevent recurrence
    • Appropriate and timely quality decisions are made and communicated to relevant stakeholders
    • Development and implementation of state-of-the start and standardized governance, procedures, processes, tools and ways of working, including quality reviews
    • Identification and effective implementation of continuous quality improvement plans
    • Effective follow-up on audit CAPAs, quality support and guidance
    • Appropriate metrics to monitor performance are defined, implemented and communicated
    • Appropriate identification, assessment, prevention and mitigation of risks
    • Development and implementation of quality agreements or other quality-related contractual documents
    • Proper selection, qualification or disqualification of suppliers
    • Effective quality management of supplier-related projects
  • Actively contribute to the EMEA Quality Leadership Team and lead cross-functional initiatives.
  • Collaborate with other QLT members to support strategic quality goals.
  • Lead, coach, and develop your team, fostering a mindset of accountability, agility, and excellence.
Qualifications

What you will bring to the role

  • M.Sc or B.Sc in biology, pharmacy, chemistry, engineering or relevant area
  • 15-20 years of experience within the healthcare industry, in manufacturing and quality operational positions of growing responsibility
  • Significant experience in managing external operations / suppliers, across multiple countries and for a wide range of products
  • Minimum of 5 years of team leadership experience in global, multicultural environments
  • Significant knowledge and understanding of API manufacturing operations

Ready to make a difference?

Apply today and help us shape the future of eye health.

Additional Information

Grow your career at Santen

A career at Santen is an opportunity to make a difference. Through our long-term vision outlined in Santen 2030, we are committed to be a Social Innovator - addressing the social and economic needs of people with visual impairments. We have team members around the world using their diverse talents to unlock new modalities and drive innovations for patient outcomes, education and treatment. At Santen, we believe in empowering all our team members with flexible ways of working and a highly inclusive work environment.

The Santen Group is an Equal Opportunity Employer. We are committed to building diverse teams and ensuring a safe and inclusive physical and virtual workplace for every one of our team members. All employment decisions are based on business needs, role requirements and individual qualifications regardless of race, color, ethnicity, national origin/ancestry, religion, sexual orientation, gender, gender identity/ expression, age, disability, medical condition, marital status, veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law.

If you require any kind of accommodation during our recruitment process, please let the recruiter from our team know.

companyDescription:
Sympower accelerates Europe’s energy transition towards a climate-neutral future by providing flexibility services to support smarter, renewable energy systems. Our end-to-end services encompass BESS multi-market optimisation, demand response services, and optimisation for renewable energy providers. By managing over 2.5GW of energy assets across Europe, we’re enabling our customers and partners to generate long-term revenue.

For over a decade, we have optimised hundreds of energy assets for dozens of industries on multiple European energy markets, providing customised solutions tailored to the needs of our customers and partners.

As a route-to-market provider and optimiser, we prioritise the success of our customers and guarantee the biggest impact towards building a truly sustainable energy system.
companySlogan: Powering Change Together
descriptionHtml:
The Financial Controller is responsible for accounting and control of our entities in Europe. This includes timely closure of the accounting period, internal controls, tax reporting, accounts filling and collaboration on various initiatives. Join our growing international team and help us with the energy transition!


What is in it for you

We are committed to creating an inclusive and values-based culture where everyone feels that they belong and where everyone has the opportunity to do meaningful work.

We offer a market competitive compensation package, including but not limited to:

  • 30 Days Paid Holiday Leave
  • 1 Day Paid Wellness Leave
  • 1 Day Paid Birthday Leave
  • Paid Maternity and Partner Leave
  • Pawternity Leave
  • Mental Health and Wellbeing Support
  • Remote Office Budget
  • Internet Allowance
  • Development Plan & Budget
  • Stock Appreciation Rights
  • 2 Days Paid Volunteer Leave

Learn about all of our benefits on our careers page.


What you will do

  • Manage financial month end close processes including preparation and posting of journal entries, accruals and account reconciliations.
  • Manage monthly payroll checks and posting in multiple jurisdictions.
  • Take ownership of the quality of the accounting data, internal controls and financial compliance in several European subsidiaries.
  • Prepare information and coordinate filling of the VAT returns.
  • Coordinate annual income tax return preparation and filling.
  • Coordinate statutory financial statement preparation.
  • Manage subsidiaries’ liquidity.
  • Support the year-end audit for the Group.
  • Collaborate with the local teams and wider organisations on projects with any financial insights needed both for financial planning/analytics.
  • Manage relationships with local accounting and tax advisors.
  • Take initiatives in process improvements and support the implementations where needed.
  • Support Accounting & Reporting Manager and the larger team as needed and more!


What you will need

  • Bachelor’s degree or higher in Finance/Accounting and/or professional accounting qualification
  • 5+ Accounting/Controlling experience in an international environment.
  • Strong technical skills (systems as well as excel/google sheets).
  • NetSuite experience is an advantage.
  • Strong communication skills and willingness to work as a team.
  • Detailed orientated self-starter with strong focus on timely and quality deliverables.
  • Fluent in English. Other languages are nice to have.


Who we are

Sympower is accelerating the global transition towards ‘net-zero’ by helping to build smarter, cleaner renewable energy systems. Using our proprietary software platform, we help balance the supply and demand of electricity across international energy networks.

We help businesses, grid operators, asset owners and other energy stakeholders around the world reduce their carbon emissions, integrate more distributed renewable energy resources, and generate new revenue streams by participating in demand-side response services. Learn more about us in this video.

In 2022, Sympower became a certified B Corp, which shows the company is meeting high standards of verified performance, accountability, and transparency across 5 impact areas: governance, workers, community, environment, and customers.


Sympower is an equal opportunity employer. We encourage a diverse workforce and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees.

Your personal data will be processed in accordance with our Privacy Policy.

descriptionHtml:
1GLOBAL is a technology-driven global mobile communications provider dedicated to empowering enterprises worldwide to unlock the full growth potential of mobile connectivity. With a best-in-class telecom technology platform, a comprehensive suite of globally viable regulatory licenses, and privileged access to the telecom wholesale market, 1GLOBAL is uniquely positioned to deliver seamless compliance and connectivity solutions. Serving the world's leading banks, corporations, and digital-first businesses—including neo-banks, travel companies, and payment service providers—1GLOBAL connects over 43 million devices globally.

With 2024 full-year revenue exceeding US$100 million and 2025 expected to approach US$200 million, 1GLOBAL is a profitable business generating significant cash flows to fund its ongoing investments in infrastructure, transformation, and growth. 2024 saw major client wins and marked 1GLOBAL's evolution from a multi-market telecommunication provider to a global technology-driven mobile connectivity powerhouse. In 2025 we have launched our mobile virtual network operator enabler (MVNE) business enabling major consumer brands to offer full domestic connectivity to the customers.

Established in 2022 by experienced tech founders and entrepreneurs Hakan Koç and Pyrros Koussios, 1GLOBAL is a European technology leader driving digital transformation in the global telecommunications market. It operates as a fully regulated Mobile Virtual Network Operator ("MVNO") in nine countries and as a regulated telecommunications operator in an additional 31 countries. Headquartered in the Netherlands, with world-class R&D hubs in Lisbon, Berlin, and São Paulo, 1GLOBAL employs almost 450 experts across 13 countries.

Position Overview

We are seeking a credible and experienced Head of Internal Audit to establish and lead our internal audit function. Reporting to the Audit Committee of the Board and for day-to-day matters to the Finance Director, this role will design and execute a risk-based audit programme covering key business areas including finance, legal, HR, and operations. The Head of Internal Audit will also be responsible for overseeing remediation workstreams and managing relationships with senior stakeholders across the organisation.

The role will suit someone who brings technical depth across core financial and operational processes—such as order-to-cash, procure-to-pay, and tax—paired with the influencing skills to drive meaningful change. You'll also have the opportunity to shape and mentor a growing team, starting with one direct report (Internal Audit Analyst).


Key Responsibilities

Internal Audit Framework & Execution

  • Design and implement the internal audit function, including policies, planning cycles, and reporting structures
  • Develop a risk-based annual audit plan, with coverage across financial reporting, revenue assurance, billing, tax, payroll, HR processes, and contract compliance
  • Lead the execution of internal audits from end-to-end: scoping, risk and control assessment, testing design, fieldwork, and reporting
  • Establish documentation standards and quality assurance processes for audit activities.

Ad-Hoc Audits & Reporting

  • Be the go-to person for ad-hoc queries by senior management and deliver timely and independently from day-to-day management views on the operational efficiency of the business
  • Prepare analysis of key performance indicators for the Audit Committee and the Board and report to them when requested to do so.

Stakeholder Engagement & Reporting

  • Build trusted relationships with senior stakeholders across finance, legal, HR, operations, and executive leadership
  • Present audit findings clearly and constructively, ensuring recommendations are pragmatic and solution oriented
  • Provide regular reporting to the Finance Director and senior leadership team on audit progress, findings, and remediation
  • Serve as an advisor to the business on internal controls and risk management best practice.

Remediation Oversight

  • Drive the resolution of audit findings by coordinating remediation plans and tracking progress through to completion
  • Hold business owners accountable for timely and effective action
  • Contribute to the development of policies and internal control improvements based on audit outcomes.

Team Leadership & Development

  • Line manage and develop the Internal Audit Analyst, providing coaching, technical guidance, and performance management. Overtime the internal audit team may be expanded further to include additional headcount
  • Champion a culture of integrity, accountability, and continuous improvement


Requirements

Must Have

  • Professional qualification in audit/accounting (e.g. CIA, ACA, ACCA, or equivalent)
  • Strong internal audit experience gained in practice (e.g. Big 4 or top 10) and/or industry
  • Solid knowledge of business processes including order-to-cash, procure-to-pay, taxation, HR, and operational workflows
  • Proven ability to challenge and influence at a senior/executive level
  • Experience running audits from planning to reporting, including risk assessment and test execution
  • Demonstrated project management and stakeholder coordination capabilities
  • Experience managing and developing junior team members


Nice to Have

  • Experience in high-growth, multi-national or listed businesses.
  • Experience within the Telecoms sector
  • Familiarity with GRC tools, audit workflow software, or data analytics in audit


Benefits

Why 1GLOBAL?

  • Growth Opportunities: Advance your career in one of the fastest growing telecommunications companies, expanding over 40% year-on-year under the leadership of successful tech entrepreneurs
  • Major Transaction Exposure: Be in the driver's seat for transactions that will have an impact on the future telco industry
  • Work with a Talented Team: From the Board and the Founders to the Senior Management Team, you will collaborate daily with the most capable and renowned external advisors, and constantly being exposed to talented and driven individuals
  • Dynamic Work Environment: Thrive in a collaborative, fast-paced workplace where innovation is encouraged, and every contribution counts
  • Professional Development: Work alongside industry experts to enhance your skills and knowledge in a cutting-edge field
  • International Experience: Gain opportunities to work in different 1GLOBAL offices around the world as you grow within the company
  • Open Communication Culture: Join a team where your ideas are heard, and open dialogue is encouraged, fostering a supportive and transparent work environment
  • Get Things Done Attitude: Be part of a results-driven team that values efficiency, creativity, and the drive to make a tangible impact in the industry


1GLOBAL is an equal opportunity employer, we value your character as much as your talent. Diversity drives our innovation, and we offer a collaborative, dynamic, and international work environment. We are excited for you to join our mission to revolutionise connectivity globally.

descriptionHtml:
Job Description

About the Job:

We are looking for a forward thinking Internal Audit Associate (EU) with demonstrated experience in leading and delivering audit engagements. The successful candidate will play a key role in strengthening our Internal Audit function, driving risk-based assurance, and supporting the organization’s commitment to sound governance and compliance across the EU.

In this role, you will contribute to shaping the internal audit agenda, provide insights that influence decision-making at senior levels, and help foster a culture of transparency, accountability and continuous improvement.

Candidates must be based in the Netherlands.

Key Responsibilities:

  • Lead and execute end-to-end internal audit engagements, from planning and risk assessment through fieldwork, reporting, and follow-up.
  • Provide practical, value-adding recommendations to management and support implementation of remediation actions.
  • Assist the Director - Internal Audit to ensure that policies, procedures, and regulations are being followed consistently throughout the company.
  • Evaluate the effectiveness of internal controls, risk management, and governance processes in line with the IIA Standards and EU regulations.
  • Contribute to the continuous improvement of audit methodologies and tools.
  • Support the Internal Audit function by performing spot checks and ad hoc reviews.
  • Keep abreast of developments in EU legislation, GDPR, ESG reporting frameworks, and sector-specific regulations that impact audit scope
  • Engage with external auditors on annual / bi-annual financial statement audits, compliance audits, SOC 1 & SOC 2, etc.

Requirements:

  • 5 - 7 years of experience in internal or external audit, preferably with a Big 4 firm within the EU
  • Prior experience working in financial services firms, fintechs, and/or crypto companies
  • Bachelor's degree or equivalent practical experience; master’s degree a plus
  • General knowledge of MiCAR and related frameworks, such as the Dutch Implementation of AMLD, including delegated acts thereafter and relevant guidance issued by ESMA, EBA and competent authorities
  • Proven ability to manage complex audits independently and deliver high-quality results under deadlines
  • Exceptional written and verbal communication
  • Strong interpersonal skills and the ability to build and maintain relationships with diverse stakeholders, both internally and externally
  • The ability to work independently in a fast-paced environment
  • Receptive to feedback and implements changes immediately with a positive attitude
  • Experience in one or more of the following:
    • Anti-money laundering, financial crimes, and money transmission
    • Financial risk management
    • Cryptocurrency settlement, custody, and markets
    • Cybersecurity
    • Data privacy
Preferred:

  • Certified Internal Auditor (CIA) or equivalent
  • Highly self-motivated, proactive, and able to work effectively in a fast-paced, dynamic environment
  • Self-confident, resilient and goal focused
  • Proactive, forward-thinking, and ability to anticipate emerging risks
  • International mindset and ability to work effectively in a multicultural, cross-boarder environment

Benefits:

We believe a happy, motivated, and healthy team is the best way to succeed. We offer the following benefits:

  • Chance to earn equity
  • Maternity & Paternity leave
  • WeWork Membership
  • WFH Yearly Stipend
  • L&D Stipend (after 6 months)

About Zero Hash

Zero Hash's full stack financial infrastructure seamlessly connects fiat, crypto and stablecoins, enabling a better way to move and transfer money and value globally. Zero Hash provides the complete technical infrastructure (delivered through API and SDK) as well as the global regulatory stack to easily and compliantly send, receive, store, and convert fiat, crypto, and stablecoins, in one platform.

Start-ups, enterprises and Fortune 500 companies, including Stripe, Interactive Brokers, Shift4, Franklin Templeton, and MoonPay embed our infrastructure to power a diverse range of use cases: cross-border payments, commerce, trading, remittance, payroll, tokenization, wallets and on and off-ramps.

Backed by Interactive Brokers, Point72 Ventures, NYCA, Bain Capital, and tastytrade.

The Zero Hash Culture

All Zero Hash employees are guided by the following characteristics and core principles:

  • Independence/Ownership - An ability to work autonomously. Join Zero Hash, pitch ideas, and shape the work you do.
  • Passion - We are innovating quickly and challenging the status quo. We want you to think big, be creative and make a difference every day.
  • Collaborative - A good attitude and respect for others. We’re teammates, not co-workers. Everything we do is a shared success and equally a shared failure - we talk in terms of “we” not “me”.
  • Initiative - An ability and passion for learning and asking questions. We will champion you, challenge you and push you to achieve your best - and we expect you to do the same.
  • Empathy - An ability to listen, respect, and understand your co-workers, customers, and everyone you interact with no matter how different they are to you.
  • Adaptability - An ability to respond quickly. We are in a fast-paced industry and so we expect you to be creative when solving a new problem and comfortable under pressure.
  • Transparency - We believe that transparency is critical to empowering everyone to make the best decisions, both the company to its people and vice versa.
  • Integrity - Integrity creates trust. As both an organization collectively and as individuals, it is our most valuable asset.

Follow us

Twitter

LinkedIn

Youtube

Blog

For candidates based in Colorado, please contact colorado-wages @zerohash.com to request compensation and benefits information regarding a particular role(s). Please include with your email the city you reside (or intend to reside in Colorado) and the title/link to the roles you're interested in.

descriptionHtml:
About Seedify

Seedify is a global Web3 incubator and launchpad, dedicated to empowering innovators and builders in the blockchain space. Together with Seedworld, we are building a thriving ecosystem for innovation, growth, and long-term impact. To support our continued expansion, we are seeking a highly skilled Accountant to establish strong financial processes, ensure compliance, and drive financial clarity across our entities.

Role Overview

The Accountant will play a critical role in managing and organizing financial operations for Seedify and Seedworld. This position requires a professional who can implement proper accounting structures, ensure compliance with jurisdictional requirements, and provide clear financial insights to support strategic decision-making.

Key Responsibilities

Entity & Fund Management

  • Establish and maintain clear separation of finances across Seedify, Seedworld, and executive holdings
  • Set up and oversee wallets and bank accounts to ensure transparent entity-level accounting


Accounting & Compliance

  • Develop and maintain accurate accounting records for annual compliance requirements
  • Manage tax and annual return obligations for Seedworld and Seedify in coordination with jurisdiction-specific contractors
  • Translate digital asset activity into accounting records and tax-ready documentation


Financial Planning & Processes

  • Design and implement robust financial processes to strengthen operational stability
  • Monitor and analyze monthly expenses, payroll, service pricing, and cash flows
  • Support the preparation of budgets, forecasts, and financial projections


Reporting & Advisory

  • Deliver regular financial reports to management and stakeholders
  • Provide actionable insights to optimize costs and improve financial decision-making


Qualifications

  • Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance, or related discipline (CPA/CA or equivalent certification preferred)
  • Minimum 3–5 years of accounting experience, with exposure to multi-entity structures
  • Knowledge of international compliance, taxation, and reporting standards
  • Experience with blockchain, digital assets, or Web3 accounting highly advantageous
  • Proficiency with accounting software and financial reporting tools
  • Strong analytical, organizational, and communication skills


What We Offer

  • Opportunity to work with a leading Web3 incubator and launchpad
  • Dynamic, innovative, and global work environment
  • Remote flexibility with a collaborative international team
  • Competitive compensation aligned with experience


What We’re Looking For

  • Someone proactive who can figure out “where all the money is and what we’re doing with it.”
  • A detail-oriented professional who thrives in environments that require building systems from the ground up
  • A trusted financial advisor who can help us move from “running on feel” to operating on sound financial principles and projections
descriptionText: About SeedifySeedify is a global Web3 incubator and launchpad, dedicated to empowering innovators and builders in the blockchain space. Together with Seedworld, we are building a thriving ecosystem for innovation, growth, and long-term impact. To support our continued expansion, we are seeking a highly skilled Accountant to establish strong financial processes, ensure compliance, and drive financial clarity across our entities.Role OverviewThe Accountant will play a critical role in managing and organizing financial operations for Seedify and Seedworld. This position requires a professional who can implement proper accounting structures, ensure compliance with jurisdictional requirements, and provide clear financial insights to support strategic decision-making.Key ResponsibilitiesEntity & Fund Management Establish and maintain clear separation of finances across Seedify, Seedworld, and executive holdings Set up and oversee wallets and bank accounts to ensure transparent entity-level accountingAccounting & Compliance Develop and maintain accurate accounting records for annual compliance requirements Manage tax and annual return obligations for Seedworld and Seedify in coordination with jurisdiction-specific contractors Translate digital asset activity into accounting records and tax-ready documentationFinancial Planning & Processes Design and implement robust financial processes to strengthen operational stability Monitor and analyze monthly expenses, payroll, service pricing, and cash flows Support the preparation of budgets, forecasts, and financial projectionsReporting & Advisory Deliver regular financial reports to management and stakeholders Provide actionable insights to optimize costs and improve financial decision-makingQualifications Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance, or related discipline (CPA/CA or equivalent certification preferred) Minimum 3–5 years of accounting experience, with exposure to multi-entity structures Knowledge of international compliance, taxation, and reporting standards Experience with blockchain, digital assets, or Web3 accounting highly advantageous Proficiency with accounting software and financial reporting tools Strong analytical, organizational, and communication skillsWhat We Offer Opportunity to work with a leading Web3 incubator and launchpad Dynamic, innovative, and global work environment Remote flexibility with a collaborative international team Competitive compensation aligned with experienceWhat We’re Looking For Someone proactive who can figure out “where all the money is and what we’re doing with it.” A detail-oriented professional who thrives in environments that require building systems from the ground up A trusted financial advisor who can help us move from “running on feel” to operating on sound financial principles and projections

descriptionHtml:
Job title: Senior Internal Auditor

Location: Rotterdam, Netherlands

Hybrid working model


Audit & Risk Recruitment is partnering with a leading industrial player with a strong European footprint and a commitment to innovation, sustainability, and operational excellence. With facilities across multiple countries and a diverse portfolio, the company drives impact in the manufacturing sector and beyond. The Rotterdam office serves as a key hub for operations, strategy, and governance.


This role is ideal for an experienced audit professional who is passionate about both financial and operational audits, thrives in a collaborative environment, and wants to make a tangible impact across multiple business units.

The Senior Internal Auditor will take a hands-on approach to planning, executing, and reporting audits, identifying control weaknesses, and recommending improvements. The successful candidate will be involved in audits across various European operations, offering both variety and visibility within the organisation.


Key Responsibilities:

  • Lead and conduct internal audits (financial and operational) across the company’s European entities
  • Assess effectiveness of internal controls, risk management, and compliance with internal policies and external regulations
  • Collaborate with business stakeholders to understand processes and identify areas for improvement
  • Develop audit plans, work programs, and risk-based methodologies
  • Prepare clear, concise, and well-structured audit reports for senior management
  • Support the Head of Internal Audit with ad-hoc investigations and special projects
  • Monitor the implementation of audit recommendations and corrective actions
  • Promote a culture of integrity, compliance, and continuous improvement


Who They’re Looking For:

  • Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Accounting, Finance, Business Administration, or a related field
  • Minimum 6 years of experience in internal or external audit, preferably within an industrial or manufacturing environment
  • Strong knowledge of internal control frameworks and risk management practices
  • Excellent analytical, communication, and interpersonal skills
  • Fluency in English (Dutch and/or other EU languages are a plus)
  • Willingness to travel across Europe (up to 10%)
  • EU passport holder (requirement for travel and legal employment in the Netherlands)

RSG is een Interim-, Advies- en Recruitmentbureau op het gebied van Finance & Control. 
Ons credo “Human Key Solutions” staat garant voor gemotiveerde kennisspecialisten binnen de domeinen Finance & Control en Audit, Risk & Compliance. Wij bemiddelen mensen vanaf HBO-niveau, in functies vanaf assistent-controller tot aan de CFO. RSG Finance is actief binnen zowel de profit als de publieke sector.

RSG onderscheidt zich door het unieke businessmodel: het Flexibel Vennootmodel. We streven ernaar om met onze flexibele vennoten een arbeidscontract voor onbepaalde tijd aan te gaan. Dit geeft hen zekerheid en dit stelt ons in staat om onze opdrachtgevers op een continue basis te voorzien van ondernemende specialisten met een hoogwaardig kennisniveau.

descriptionHtml:
Als Senior Internal Auditor bij DELA ben je verantwoordelijk voor audits die ertoe doen! Je werkt op het hoofdkantoor in Eindhoven samen met vijf collega-auditors en een manager in teamverband aan het uitvoeren van verschillende soorten audits en onderzoeken. In deze rol kun je jezelf in de breedte ontwikkelen, aan de slag gaan met gedragsaspecten en deze inzetten bij de uitvoering van audits.


Wat je als Senior Internal Auditor bij DELA doet om een waardevolle bijdrage te leveren aan onze organisatie. Onze innovatieve manier van auditen is kenmerkend: scherp, gefocust op leren en altijd vanuit de verbinding. Je focust niet enkel op de audits die per se moeten, maar juist ook op de audits die aansluiten bij wat de business nodig heeft.


Daarbij kijk je niet alleen naar de mogelijke verbetering, maar ook naar de grondoorzaken. Ook hebben we oog voor het grotere plaatje: door afstand te nemen lukt het om de bevindingen uit onze onderzoeken in de juist context te plaatsen. Op deze manier werk je niet jaarlijkse checklists af, maar creëer je écht toegevoegde waarde voor de organisatie.


Samen met je collega’s op het hoofdkantoor in Eindhoven geef je daarnaast invulling aan een visie op internal audit in 2030 en de verdere ontwikkeling van internal audit binnen DELA. Agile werken en samen leren zijn hierbij belangrijke uitgangspunten.


De diversiteit aan audits en de vrijheid die je krijgt maken dit een mooie volgende stap in jouw carrière. En dat alles binnen een lerende omgeving van een maatschappelijk betrokken organisatie.

Wat we van jou als Senior Internal Auditor wensen om de menskant van onze dienstverlening optimaal te vertegenwoordigen.


Functie-eisen:

  • Je hebt een WO-opleiding afgerond, bij voorkeur aangevuld met een postmaster RO, RA of RE;
  • Je hebt >6 jaar ervaring met (interne of externe) auditwerkzaamheden;
  • Je hebt ervaring in de beoordeling van het beheersen van operationele risico’s;
  • Je hebt ervaring in de financiële sector;
  • Je hebt bij voorkeur ervaring met het auditen van cultuur en gedrag en/of data analyse.


Daarnaast herken je jezelf in het onderstaande:

  • Je bent in verbinding met het management en komt zo tot gedragen adviezen;
  • Je bent sterk in mooie ideeën omzetten naar concrete, impactvolle acties;
  • Je werkt graag in teamverband om zo tot nog betere resultaten te komen;
  • Je bent reflectief op je eigen handelen en wilt het steeds een beetje beter doen;
  • Je hebt een flinke dosis nieuwsgierigheid en zelf-startend vermogen;
  • Je combineert in jouw audits harde zaken (data, cijfers, processen) met zachte aspecten (gedrag en emotie)
  • om zo tot waardevolle inzichten te komen;
  • Je bent een stevige gesprekspartner binnen alle lagen van de organisatie.


Wat je als Senior Internal Auditor bij DELA krijgt om voldoening en betekenis uit jouw werk te halen. DELA is een coöperatie die, net als jij, midden in het leven staat. Kenmerkend voor DELA is een open communicatie, een informele sfeer, een goede interne samenwerking en betekenis geven aan je werk. We geven je de mogelijkheid om de arbeidsvoorwaarden af te stemmen op jouw persoonlijke wensen en behoeften.


Daarom krijg je:

  • Direct een contract voor onbepaalde tijd;
  • Een salaris tussen € 5.267,- en € 7.596,- bruto per maand, o.b.v. 40 uur per week;
  • Bovenop je salaris ontvang je een individueel keuzebudget t.w.v. 20,25% van jouw maandsalaris, dat je naar
  • wens kan laten uitbetalen in geld en/of extra verlofdagen;
  • €750 bruto per jaar om te besteden aan jouw vitaliteit (sportschoenen, sportabonnement, mindfulness,
  • stoppen met roken cursus, etc.);
  • Een goede pensioenregeling, korting op verzekeringsproducten en de mogelijkheid om cursussen te volgen
