descriptionHtml:
Manager internal Audit


Are you ready to take the next step in your audit career within a global and dynamic environment? We are looking for an enthusiastic and ambitious Manager Internal Audit to join our international team based in The Netherlands, region the Hague.


In this role, you will lead the operational activities of our Internal Audit teams in The Netherlands and South Africa, while also actively participating in a variety of (IT) audit engagements. You’ll have the opportunity to travel internationally (15–20%) and work across multiple locations worldwide.


We’re looking for a people-oriented professional who builds strong relationships across cultures, demonstrates integrity and professionalism, and values a positive and engaging work environment.

As the designated delegate for the Director of Internal Audit, you will play a key role in driving the department’s success and continuous improvement.


Your Role:

  • Shape and contribute to the department’s strategic direction and lead initiatives that drive innovation and improvement;
  • Manage and execute international audit engagements across business processes, projects, and reporting;
  • Take the lead on IT audits and strategic IT programs, representing Internal Audit in senior management meetings and steering committees;
  • Supervise and support Internal Audit teams in The Netherlands and South Africa, ensuring effective and efficient operations;
  • Ensure timely, high-quality execution of audits, including risk assessments and clear, insightful reporting;
  • Act as a trusted advisor to local, regional, and corporate management;
  • Coordinate operational planning and logistics for the audit team;
  • Identify and implement process improvements to enhance audit efficiency and impact;
  • Expand your professional skills and network to prepare for future leadership opportunities.


Your track record:

  • A Master’s degree in IT, Accounting, Economics, or Business Administration (CISA or RE certification is a plus);
  • 8+ years of relevant experience in internal auditing;
  • Solid knowledge of Internal Audit standards; CIA certification or willingness to obtain it;
  • Excellent written and verbal communication skills in English; speaking Dutch is a plus
  • Willingness and ability to travel internationally (15–20%).


What we offer:

  • A salary up to € 110.000,- a year excluding bonus
  • 29 holidays per year based on a fulltime employment and the possibility to purchase 12 additional days;
  • Extensive career & training opportunities both nationally and internationally;
  • Flexible working hours and the ability to work from home in accordance with your manager and corporate policies;
  • Commuting allowance;
  • Modern pension scheme;
  • Collective health insurance;
  • Possibility to register with our corporate fitness plan;
  • Coaching options through our EAP (Employee Assistance Program).
  • You will receive an automated confirmation-email of the receipt of your application


Application procedure

When we see a match, we will invite you for the first interview within a couple of days. Of course, you will also receive a message if we will not invite you.


After a successful first round, you will be invited for the second round. If we are both still positive after the second interview, we will make you an offer

companyDescription:
McKinsey & Company is a global management consulting firm. We are the trusted advisor to the world's leading businesses, governments, and institutions. 

We work with leading organizations across the private, public and social sectors. Our scale, scope, and knowledge allow us to address problems that no one else can. We have deep functional and industry expertise as well as breadth of geographical reach. We are passionate about taking on immense challenges that matter to our clients and, often, to the world. 

We work with our clients as we do with our colleagues. We build their capabilities and leadership skills at every level and every opportunity. We do this to help build internal support, get to real issues, and reach practical recommendations. We bring out the capabilities of clients to fully participate in the process and lead the ongoing work.
descriptionHtml:
Who You'll Work With

You are someone who thrives in a high-performance environment, bringing a growth mindset and entrepreneurial spirit to tackle meaningful challenges that have a real impact.

In return for your drive, determination, and curiosity, we’ll provide the resources, mentorship, and opportunities to help you quickly broaden your expertise, grow into a well-rounded professional, and contribute to work that truly makes a difference.

When you join us, you will have:

  • Continuous learning: Our learning and apprenticeship culture, backed by structured programs, is all about helping you grow while creating an environment where feedback is clear, actionable, and focused on your development. The real magic happens when you take the input from others to heart and embrace the fast-paced learning experience, owning your journey.
  • A voice that matters: From day one, we value your ideas and contributions. You’ll make a tangible impact by offering innovative ideas and practical solutions. We not only encourage diverse perspectives, but they are critical in driving us toward the best possible outcomes.
  • Global community: With colleagues across 65+ countries and over 100 different nationalities, our firm’s diversity fuels creativity and helps us come up with the best solutions. Plus, you’ll have the opportunity to learn from exceptional colleagues with diverse backgrounds and experiences.
  • Exceptional benefits: On top of a competitive salary (based on your location, experience, and skills), we provide a comprehensive benefits package to enable holistic well-being for you and your family.

Your Impact

You will work in our Amsterdam office as part of our Finance & Accounting team. You will provide general accounting support to the office, ensuring quality of data and compliance with Dutch legislation and Local and Firm Accounting policies.

Financial Reporting

  • Review balance sheet reconciliations for accuracy and completeness and coordinate / collaborate with global accounting team on items requiring action within the appropriate time frame
  • Prepare monthly journal entries
  • Assist in the month-end close process, including review of the monthly trial balance, and validate accuracy of entries processed in accordance with US GAAP
  • Tax Filing and Compliance:
  • Prepare, review and ensure accurate and timely filing of tax-related documents.
  • Prepare year-end audit schedules, gather data as required, and respond to ad-hoc requests in support of global Corporate Tax Team
  • Identify, analyze and report benefit in kind transactions.
  • Explain material positions and mutations taken with regards to corporate tax and conduct difference analysis across financial years
  • Liaise with external VAT service provider and supply documentation to reclaim foreign VAT.
  • Liaise with Dutch tax authorities (Belastingdienst), tax advisors, and other critical stakeholders in order to maintain a high level of compliance with rules and regulations and handle queries/disputes.

Payroll

  • Streamline, coordinate, and prepare transactions for payroll input from various internal and external sources
  • Assist in preparing monthly payroll and be back-up for the payroll accountant
  • Prepare, review and process accounting of payroll from AFAS in SAP - analyze for accuracy and completeness
  • Prepare balance sheet reconciliations for select payroll accounts, e.g., prepaid pension, and devise actions to resolve open items
  • Act as a business partner on payroll-related topics with employees, payroll function, shared service centers and HR
  • Coordinate expense reimbursement of employees on education leave
  • Analytical and Technical Tasks
  • Ensure accurate general ledger and project code usage of purchase invoices
  • Leverage MS Excel and analytical tools for financial analysis and reporting
  • Develop and document business processes and accounting policies to maintain and strengthen internal controls.

Collaboration and Communication

  • Collaborate with cross-functional teams and peers globally
  • Respond to internal and external queries and ad-hoc requests
  • Collaborate with respective shared service centers
  • Support the team by continuously improving accounting, financial, and reporting processes and help drive projects supporting real business needs, e.g., conducting ad-hoc analyses of expense data or revising an internal process for accounting pension

Your Qualifications and Skills

  • University degree (WO) or higher professional education (HBO) with background in accounting and/or tax
  • 2-5 years accounting experience in international corporate environment; Experience in payroll, Dutch tax rules and/or with US GAAP is a plus
  • Strong MS Office skills (i.e., Excel, PowerPoint and Word); Knowledge of SAP, analytical tools, and AFAS are desirable
  • Self-starter that can work collaboratively in a team in an environment of ambiguity
  • Curious with drive for continuous self-development and for learning
  • Ability to communicate complex ideas effectively–both verbally and in writing–in English. Dutch language skills are preferred.

Dutch language skills are preferred.

descriptionHtml:
Risk & Compliance Officer


Help ensure the organization complies with all internal and external rules, regulations, and procedures.

  • Hours: 40
  • Location: Hybrid/Utrecht
  • Experience: Intermediate


Your mission:

Your daily work consists of ensuring the organization complies with all internal and external rules, regulations, and procedures. This means monitoring and assessing the level of compliance risks, developing and implementing controls to protect the organization against these risks, and providing advice on addressing compliance issues. You are also responsible for developing and managing effective action plans in response to audit findings and compliance violations. Finally, you continuously work to improve controls, increase efficiency, and reduce operational risks.


You will also work on the following topics:

  • Audit support: testing and assessing internal controls to effectively manage risks (familiarity with ISAE 3000, ISAE 3402)
  • Operational risk monitoring: maintaining and improving risk frameworks, monitoring risk exposure, and providing input for risk reporting
  • Working practices improvement: collaborating with process owners to increase risk management knowledge, gain greater control over operational processes, and simultaneously reduce workload
  • Compliance monitoring: ensuring compliance with laws, regulations, and internal policies, and advising on regulatory changes.


Your home base at Capgemini:

You'll be part of the risk & compliance team within the pensions division of Capgemini Financial Services. Together, you'll work towards a common goal.


What you can expect from us

  • All the training you need to be successful as a risk & compliance agent
  • A well-stocked suitcase with holiday allowance and enough time (26 days) to spend it
  • Work wherever you want, on a device of your choice, with our connectivity allowance
  • A fast laptop and a SIM card that you can also use for personal purposes
  • Always on the road with your company car or NS Business Card. Or choose reimbursement of your travel expenses
  • And, of course, a competitive salary. We'd love to discuss this with you.


This position is a good fit for you because you:

  • have 1 to 3 years of experience in an audit role, with demonstrable knowledge of audits (internal and external), internal controls, compliance, and risk management.
  • Are familiar with privacy legislation and protected information (GDPR).
  • Can research regulations and company information and quickly grasp new concepts.
  • Can collaborate well with various internal teams and work independently.
  • Have strong oral and written communication skills in English.
  • Are proficient in Microsoft Office (Excel, Word, PowerPoint).
  • Have excellent communication, writing, and analytical skills to translate complex challenges into concrete solutions.
  • Can present findings to relevant internal and external stakeholders.
  • Can resolve complex issues (and separate irrelevant facts) and apply a compliance framework or policy.


Get the future you want

  • That's our promise to you. It starts on day one with a good onboarding process and structured and transparent career paths.
  • But also: plenty of opportunities to deviate from that, to be a pioneer, and to forge your own path.
  • You can count on boundless trust and collegial support from us.
  • Working at Capgemini also means working at the international heart of innovation. The premier league of Technology.
  • An employer with an eye on you and a vision for the future, where sustainability, diversity, and inclusion play a key role

descriptionHtml:
We’re looking for a Senior Specialist Internal Audit and Risk Methodology & Quality at Randstad Global

Diemen - Netherlands, hybrid, fulltime


people at the heart of everything we do

Working at Randstad is unlike working at any organization. Because at Randstad we put people at the heart of everything we do. This goes for our clients, our talent, our employees and society. We combine our passion for people with the power of today’s technologies. This helps us support people and organizations in realizing their true potential. We call this partner for talent.

Learn more about our mission, history and values on our website: www.randstad.com


BR&AF

The business risk and audit function (BR&AF) provides assurance and recommendations to the Executive Board and the Audit Committee on Randstad Group’s (risk) management, control and governance practices. Therefore the department facilitates in setting governance standards. It provides advice on internal control improvements, it conducts audits, special investigations and assignments worldwide on request of the Executive Board and Audit Committee. BR&AF’s responsibility includes the quarterly reporting based on: the risk and internal control self assessments, audit results, discussions on internal control weaknesses and analysis of the key risks reported throughout the company's management structure. This also substantiates the Group’s in-control statements.


The global function consists of team members operating through hubs spread over 4 continents. The team is organized in a matrix consisting of control & audit focus teams on business and functional areas (e.g. digital, talent, client, back office & shared service centers and fraud & forensics) and partners that are the linking pin to operating units (business risk & audit partners) with a global view. A support group is set up for professional practice, learning & development, quality improvement & methods, analytics and PMO.


Representatives of the different groups (Focus teams, BR&A Partners and support group) form the BR&AF core management team that reports to the Managing Director BR&AF.


how you will contribute

As professional practice methods, techniques, and QAIP senior you are going to:

  • design, develop, and enhance methodologies, techniques, and tools for conducting audits and other assurance engagements.
  • assist BR&AF MD to assess the efficiency and effectiveness of the internal audit activity and identify opportunities for improvement.


what you will be doing

  • develop, implement, and maintain a quality assurance and improvement program framework and program to ensure compliance with professional standards, regulatory requirements, and Randstad internal policies.
  • periodic review and assess the performance of the internal audit activity, and assist in external assessments done by a qualified, independent assessor.
  • provide feedback and recommendations to responsible BR&AF team managers to improve their work in terms of effectiveness and efficiency, and communicate results of the quality assurance and improvement program to senior management and corrective action plan.
  • contribute to the development of the department's quality management system (QMS). This includes areas such as document control, change control, and risk management.
  • closely monitor industry best practices, emerging trends, and changes in professional standards, and enhance methodologies, techniques, and tools for conducting audits and other assurance engagements.


who will you work with

  • MD BR&AF, BR&AF MT
  • Business risk & audit partners
  • Partners/communities within the, IT, project management space both at randstad global as well as in our operating companies
  • BR&AF stakeholders/clients within the business including local MT's, process and control owners
  • External co-source partners


what you will bring

  • minimum 8 years of relevant experience
  • minimum master degree in business or economics, and related accreditation (e.g. CPA/CA/CIA/ACCA)


Your Business English is at level BEC B1/B2 or higher, preferably combined with proficiency in one of the following languages: Dutch, Spanish, French, Italian and /or German.


your competencies

Within Randstad we defined the following Leadership competencies:

  • delighting people: connect people (client, talent, employee, society), building strong client relationships and delivering client-centric solutions.
  • performing today: resourcefulness securing and developing resources effectively and efficiently.
  • leading change: manages ambiguity operating effectively, even when things are not certain or the way forward is not clear.
  • securing the future: drives vision and purpose painting a compelling picture of the vision and strategy that motivates others into action.
  • strategic mindset: seeing ahead to future possibilities and translating them into breakthrough strategies
  • creating clarity - showing line of sight: making sense of complex, high quantity and sometimes contradictory information to effectively solve problems and provides direction, is delegating and removing obstacles to get work done
  • courage to challenge: shows courage and confidence to speak up skillfully, challenging others even when they are confronted with resistance or unfamiliar circumstances


what’s in it for you

We put people at the heart of everything we do. Our employment conditions reflect this and are in accordance with the applicable (local) Randstad employment policies and related personnel guide.


We want our teams and talent to reflect the rich diversity of the societies we serve. We thrive for an environment of belonging, safety and confidence. So everyone can bring their whole selves to work and flourish. Learn more about equity, diversity, inclusion and belonging at randstad here.


If you recognize yourself in the profile above, we invite you to apply for this role. For more information you can reach out to our recruitment business partner: jennifer.roberts@randstadsourceright.co.uk

descriptionHtml:
Looking to grow your audit career in an international, people-driven environment?

At Baker Tilly, we’re hiring Senior Assistant Auditors who thrive on variety, enjoy client interaction, and want to make real impact through numbers. If you’re eager to develop your skills across diverse industries while working in a supportive and ambitious team — this could be your next move.


Your role: client contact, insight, and variety

As a Senior Assistant Auditor, you’ll work with both national and international clients — from local innovators to global players. Every week brings a new engagement, new insights, and new perspectives into how businesses operate. You’ll review financial statements, contribute to audits, and help clients stay ahead of regulatory and operational challenges.

You’ll work closely with your manager and team to identify improvements, provide independent advice, and ensure quality at every step. Your analytical mindset and professional curiosity will be key to turning complex data into actionable recommendations.


The team you’ll join

You’ll be part of our Audit practice — a collaborative, high-energy group of professionals who support each other and celebrate progress. We value learning, humor, and honesty, and we take pride in delivering meaningful results for our clients. Our hybrid working model gives you the freedom to manage your time while staying connected with your team.


What you bring

  • A completed degree in Accountancy or Business Economics (Bachelor’s or Master’s)
  • Started or currently completing your Chartered Accountant CA(SA) qualification
  • 2–4 years of relevant audit or assurance experience at a reputable firm
  • Strong communication skills and a client-first attitude
  • Analytical, detail-oriented, and eager to learn
  • Fluent in English; openness to learning Dutch is a plus


What we offer

  • Competitive salary (€2,908 – €4,846 gross/month + 8.33% holiday allowance)
  • Flexible (hybrid) working hours – You decide when and where you work best
  • 32 vacation days (incl. 12 customizable with your personal budget)
  • Attractive mobility options – Lease car, NS Business Card, or lease bike
  • Growth & development – Access to top training and learning programs
  • Fully equipped home office – Laptop, iPhone & everything you need to start strong


Relocating? We’ve got you.

We welcome international professionals — and if you're relocating from abroad, we offer full sponsorship and relocation support. Dutch language training is included, and your onboarding will be fully supported in English.


The application journey

  1. Submit your application
  2. Introductory call with our recruiter
  3. First interview via Teams
  4. Take a short online assessment
  5. Follow-up interview via Teams
  6. Receive your offer
  7. Welcome to Baker Tilly!


Ready to make an impact with numbers?

Apply now and take the next step in your audit career — with Baker Tilly.

descriptionHtml:
Over het bedrijf

Onze klant is een landelijk accounting en adviesbureau voor het mkb en bedient diverse sectoren, waaronder mkb, automotive, bouw en vastgoed, onderwijs en woningcorporaties. Ze investeren in innovatie en gebruiken technologische tools zoals data-analyse en procesmining.

Functieomschrijving

Als Junior Audit Manager ben je verantwoordelijk voor wettelijke, statutaire en vrijwillige controles voor klanten in diverse sectoren. Designed for scale, DataStream helps mid-sized and large organizations reduce ingestion costs, enrich security data, and ensure compliance—without vendor lock-in. Our solution is built to streamline data delivery pipelines, making it easier for IT and security teams to gain real-time insights, fast.

descriptionHtml:
Join VirtualMetric: Security Data Management 


At VirtualMetric, we are transforming how organizations manage and optimize their security data. 

Our flagship solution, DataStream, is engineered to elevate IT infrastructure and security operations by enhancing SIEM platforms like Microsoft Sentinel. 

Our platform empowers mid-to-large enterprises to collect, process, and route log and metric data with precision and scalability, delivering exceptional ROI by reducing security operations costs and boosting efficiency. 

 

Our go-to-market strategy is sales-led through trusted partners, initially focused on enterprises in the Benelux region, with planned expansion into the UK, Nordics, France, and the USA. 

 

Position Summary 

VirtualMetric is seeking a Financial Controller who will be responsible for the financial stewardship and operational efficiency of our rapidly growing SaaS business. 

You will play a critical role in managing accounting operations, financial reporting, compliance, budgeting, and forecasting, while providing actionable insights to support strategic decision-making. 

This role is ideal for a meticulous, analytical, and strategic finance professional who thrives in a fast-paced and scaling environment. 

 

Key Responsibilities


Financial Planning & Analysis (FP&A) 

  • Lead annual budgeting and periodic forecasting processes in close collaboration with relevant teams and stakeholders 
  • Perform variance analysis on budgets and forecasts, providing clear insights to leadership. 


Financial Reporting & Compliance 

  • Oversee accurate and timely preparation of monthly, quarterly, and annual financial statements. 
  • Ensure compliance with regulatory requirements and internal controls. 
  • Liaise with external auditors and manage audit processes. 


Accounting & Bookkeeping 

  • Execute day-to-day accounting and bookkeeping activities, including journal entries, reconciliations, and general ledger maintenance. 
  • Ensure accurate and timely month-end and year-end close processes. 


Customer Invoicing & Collections 

  • Prepare and issue customer invoices in alignment with contract terms. 
  • Track receivables and follow up on outstanding accounts to ensure timely collections. 


Cash Flow & Treasury Management 

  • Monitor daily cash flow and forecast future liquidity requirements. 
  • Optimize working capital and support funding activities, as well as investor reporting. 


Strategic Finance & Business Partnering 

  • Deliver data-driven financial insights to support business cases, investment decisions, and pricing strategies. 
  • Collaborate cross-functionally to align financial performance with company objectives. 


Systems & Process Optimization 

  • Implement and improve financial systems, tools, and processes for scalability and automation. 
  • Establish best practices in financial governance and cost control. 


Required Skills and Qualifications 

  • Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance, or a related field (CPA, CMA, or similar certification preferred). 
  • Minimum 3 years of progressive experience in financial control or FP&A roles. 
  • Strong hands-on experience with bookkeeping, invoicing, and collections. 
  • Prior experience in SaaS or Cybersecurity/Tech sectors strongly preferred. 
  • Proficiency in financial software (e.g., Xero, QuickBooks, NetSuite) and reporting tools (e.g., Excel, Power BI). 
  • Deep understanding of GAAP, budgeting principles, and financial compliance. 
  • Strong analytical and problem-solving abilities with a hands-on mindset. 
  • Excellent communication skills and a collaborative approach. 
  • Adaptability and drive to thrive in a fast-growing startup environment. 


What We Offer 


At VirtualMetric, we recognize that we can only be successful with a great team of people who work in a dynamic, supportive, and rewarding work environment. When you join our team, you’ll enjoy: 

  • Impactful Work – Play a key role in revolutionizing cybersecurity. Your contributions will directly improve cybersecurity operations for enterprises worldwide. 
  • Competitive Compensation – We offer attractive salaries and performance-based rewards that reflect your skills and dedication. This includes: 
  • Attractive base salary (including holiday allowance) 
  • Pension scheme 
  • Flexible Work Environment – Enjoy hybrid or remote work options that support a healthy work-life balance. 
  • Career Growth & Learning – Access continuous learning opportunities, training programs, and career development paths to help you grow professionally. 
  • Cutting-Edge Technology – Work with advanced telemetry data collection, processing, and SIEM-enhancing solutions like DataStream and Microsoft Sentinel. 
  • Collaborative & Inclusive Culture – Join a diverse team of passionate professionals who value innovation, teamwork, and excellence. 
  • Work with Industry Leaders – Collaborate with top experts in IT and cybersecurity to solve real-world challenges. 


Given the size of our company, we are looking for someone available for 3 days a week with the possibility to increase this as we grow.

 

At VirtualMetric, your talent and ambition drive our innovation. Join us and be part of a team shaping the future of IT security and efficiency. 

 

Location  

Primary Location: Amsterdam (Hybrid)  

As a globally operating startup, we are primarily a virtual company. We use tools like Slack to maintain close contact and share information. In our main office in Amsterdam, we work two days a week, currently on Monday and Wednesday.

descriptionHtml:
Position: Analyst – Quality Control


Location: Bilthoven, Netherlands

Domain: Pharmaceuticals / Biotechnology / Clinical Research

Experience: 1–2 years

Employment Type: Full-time


Citizenship / Visa: European Citizens only (already based in the Netherlands preferred)


Job Purpose: The Analyst – Quality Control will be responsible for conducting analytical testing, documentation, and compliance activities within the Quality function. The role ensures that finished product (FP) analysis meets regulatory and organisational standards, while supporting continuous improvement and operational excellence.


Key Accountabilities:


I. Analytical Testing

  • Analyse assigned samples using validated procedures and calibrated instruments.
  • Conduct instrumental analysis independently (HPLC with Chromeleon 6.8 / 7.2).
  • Perform FP release analysis as per commitment dates without errors.
  • Check, review, and evaluate test results against set specifications.
  • Ensure compliance with GLP and good documentation practices.

II. Compliance & Reporting

  • Follow all approved SOPs, specifications, and GMP requirements.
  • Prepare analytical reports, CoAs, audit reports, and method transfer documentation.
  • Ensure proper sampling, preparation, and execution of analyses in line with safety norms.

III. Documentation & Records

  • Complete all documentation online using approved templates.
  • Maintain accuracy, integrity, and authenticity of records.
  • Prepare and update documents as per GMP requirements.

IV. Laboratory Maintenance

  • Maintain a clean, compliant, and safe working environment.
  • Dispose of samples and solutions as per SOPs.
  • Support calibration, qualification, and validation activities.
  • Draft/review SOPs and maintain lab equipment.

V. Continuous Improvement

  • Provide suggestions for cost-saving, process simplification, and automation.
  • Participate in operational studies and improvement projects.


Major Challenges:

  • Ensuring the timely release of FP samples.
  • Managing deviations, non-conformances, and instrument breakdowns.
  • Balancing compliance with operational efficiency.


Key Interactions:


Internal: QA, Lab Planning, Lab Assistants.

External: Service Engineers (for instrument-related issues).


Skills & Knowledge:

  • Education: B.Sc. / M.Sc. / B.Pharm (Chemistry).
  • Experience: 1–2 years in pharmaceutical QC.
  • Preferred: 1 year in MDI / DPI / Nasal / Respiratory formulations.
  • Language: English (required).


Additional Information:

  • Reports to: Head QA/QC.
  • Interviews: 2–3 rounds.


Application Process:

To apply, please submit your CV and a cover letter detailing your relevant experience and qualifications for the role. Applications will be reviewed on a rolling basis, so early submissions are encouraged.


Equal Opportunity Employer:

We value diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive work environment. We encourage applicants from all backgrounds to apply.


Contact: Elvis Eckardt

descriptionHtml:
Your Role In Our Story

As a young and fast-evolving company, we are dedicated to continuous improvement and efficiency in our processes. This exciting period of transformation offers a unique opportunity for you to contribute to and shape our evolving financial structures. As an Accountant you become a vital member of our Group Control Finance Team. You will oversee complex accounting tasks at a group level while providing guidance and mentorship to junior staff.

Role

Our diverse, international team comprises a finance director, a finance manager, three FP&A colleagues, three financial controllers, and two accountants. Reporting directly to the finance manager, you will handle accounts payable, intercompany transactions, and a variety of group-related accounting topics and automations. You will collaborate closely with our financial controllers, accountants, and the broader operations and Business Support departments across the group. In essence, your role involves:

  • Leadership and Supervision: Oversee the work of junior accountants, providing mentorship and support. Review reconciliations, and reports prepared by junior staff.
  • Complex Accounting Tasks: Manage month-end and year-end closing processes: prepare complex balance sheet reconciliations, i.e. account 2950, and reconcile debit and credit cards
  • Financial Analysis: Conduct detailed financial analysis to support strategic decision-making: Vendor and Customer ledger analysis. Provide insights and recommendations based on financial data analysis.
  • Audit Preparation: Assist with internal and external audits by providing necessary documentation and information. Address audit findings and implement corrective actions as needed.
  • Regulatory Compliance: Ensure adherence to all relevant accounting standards and regulatory requirements. Stay updated on changes in accounting regulations and implement necessary adjustments.
  • Project Management: Lead and manage special accounting projects and initiatives. Ensure timely completion and successful implementation of projects.
  • Budgeting and Forecasting: Assist in the maintenance and development of annual budgets: updating PO Budget information for each budgeting cycle & link PO Budgets and perform analysis on gaps in linking. Contribute to budgeting and forecasting with financial insights from your experience in working with our financial accounting data.

Your Expertise

To be successful in this role, we are looking for candidates with the following qualifications and attributes:

  • Over 2 years of comprehensive experience in accounting functions within an international company in the Netherlands, with group-level accounting as a plus.
  • Proven track record of handling complex accounting tasks.
  • Advanced knowledge of accounting principles, practices, and regulations.
  • Strong leadership and supervisory skills.
  • Proficiency in accounting software and ERP systems.
  • Consistent high performance in current accounting role.
  • Demonstrated ability to manage multiple tasks and projects effectively.

If you meet these criteria and are ready to contribute your expertise to a dynamic and challenging environment, we encourage you to apply.

descriptionHtml:
About the job

Hours: 36-40 hours

Workplace: Hybrid

Location: Utrecht


Capgemini, since 2001, providing pension implementation solutions. We are expanding our current services focused on business, IT and digitalization with pension administration.

We use an innovative and tailor-made IT platform in which process automation plays an important role.


As a Financial Controller

  • Help build the set-up of our new pension implementation organization.
  • You ensure correct accounting and support the automation of all financial processes.
  • We are looking for a Financial Controller for the Finance & Control department.
  • Within the position, you are responsible, together with the team, for drawing up the annual accounts and the implementation of the planning & control cycle of various pension funds.
  • You are widely deployable; from performing analyses, monitoring them to making improvement proposals and thinking about information management within the pension funds.
  • You are critical and proactive. You provide monthly management information, reports and the quarterly and annual figures.
  • In this position you report directly to the Manager Operations.


At Capgemini

  • You will become part of a team of six financial specialists with actuarial knowledge and various levels of experience in the field.
  • Everyone is passionate about the characteristics of the pension profession and the challenge that working with financial data offers.
  • You work towards a common goal: the new pension implementation organization within Capgemini Financial Services Benelux.


Benefits

  • We start with a one-year contract.
  • Unlimited training at our Academy
  • 26 vacation days. Fancy extra free time? Then buy extra days.
  • Capgemini contributes to your pension. In addition, you also have the freedom to contribute extra to this yourself.
  • Work wherever you want with our connectivity and home working allowance.
  • A fast laptop and a SIM card that you can also use privately.
  • A lease car, NS Business Card or payment of your travel allowance.


What do you bring?

  • You are a good team player and are open to development.
  • You prepare the annual accounts and the annual statements of the fund and act as a spider in the web with both the internal organization and the external parties (including the external accountant, the custodian and other parties).
  • You coordinate, plan, organize and monitor the progress of the interim and balance sheet audit activities of the external accountant and ensure the delivery of the balance sheet file based on the prepared by client list.
  • You are responsible for the other external (DNB) reports.
  • Higher professional education (Accountancy, Business Economics or Business Administration).
  • 4 years - 6 years of relevant work experience.

descriptionHtml:
Ben jij op zoek naar een nieuwe uitdaging als assistent accountant (32uur of 40uur) voor in de controlepraktijk bij een accountantskantoor zich richt op middelgrote vennootschappen?


Dit accountantskantoor met meerdere vestigingen in het land is momenteel op zoek naar startende assistent accountant met geen of 1 tot 2 jaar ervaring voor op de controlepraktijk. Dankzij hun platte organisatie en wisselende teams heb je bij hun genoeg mogelijkheden om jezelf inhoudelijk te ontwikkelen. Ze zijn dagelijks bezig met serieuze zaken en intelligente oplossingen voor complexe uitdagingen, maar op hun kantoren heerst een informele en prettige sfeer waarin humor een belangrijke rol speelt.


Gezien hun diverse klant portfolio van middelgrote vennootschappen, werk je in een prettige omgeving waar je veel kan leren. Hiernaast lopen er meerdere RA en AA accountants rond, waar jij alles aan kan vragen zodat je veel kan leren en kan doorgroeien.


  • jouw rol in het kort:
  • Vanaf het begin wordt je betrokken bij klantbezoeken.
  • Je voert controle- en assurance-werkzaamheden uit.
  • Documenteert bevindingen en conclusies volgens richtlijnen en kwaliteitsnormen.
  • Je signaleert mogelijkheden voor advies.


  • Wat wij zoeken:
  • We zoeken iemand die houdt van inhoudelijke uitdagingen en ambitie heeft om door te groeien.
  • Je vindt een goede balans tussen eigen verantwoordelijkheid en samenwerking in een team, met ondersteuning van ervaren collega’s.
  • Je hebt een hbo/wo-diploma in accountancy of bent hiermee bezig.
  • Kritisch denkvermogen en nauwkeurigheid zijn voor ons essentieel.


  • Wat zij jou bieden:
  • Een salaris tussen de €2700 en €3300, afhankelijk van je ervaring.
  • Volledige vergoeding van studiekosten, zodat je je continu kunt ontwikkelen.
  • Bij een fulltime dienstverband heb je recht op 28 vakantiedagen + vakantiegeld, en een extra beloning van 2 vakantiedagen als je gedurende het jaar niet ziek bent geweest.
  • Een moderne laptop wordt verstrekt voor flexibiliteit en efficiëntie in je werk.
  • Zowel kilometervergoeding als een vaste vergoeding voor openbaar vervoer voor je woon-werkverkeer.
  • Een tijd-voor-tijd regeling voor een eerlijke compensatie van overuren, voor een gezonde werk-privébalans.
  • Een goede pensioenregeling voor een zekere financiële toekomst.
  • Een moderne werkomgeving met sta-zit bureaus en de nieuwste technologie voor meer productiviteit en welzijn.
  • Wekelijkse gezamenlijke lunch op verschillende locaties voor een beter teamgevoel en informele interactie tussen collega’s.
  • Verschillende uitjes en evenementen door het jaar heen voor een positieve werksfeer en sterke teamgeest.


Spreekt dit jou aan? Solliciteer dan snel, want de werving is al volop bezig! Mocht je vragen hebben bel dan naar +31 8500 490 15 (tussen 8:00 en 19:00) of neem een kijkje op de website van Brown & McCrow.

Deze vacature wordt beheerd door Rodi en Madelief van Brown & McCrow. Dit is overigens het enige recruitment consultancy van Nederland dat enkel bemiddeld in de Accountancy branche. Jij wordt dus altijd geholpen door een specialist!

(Jouw sollicitatie is volledig confidentieel. Wij informeren niemand over jouw kandidatuur zonder jouw toestemming.)

Dankzij hun platte organisatie en wisselende teams heb je bij hun genoeg mogelijkheden om jezelf inhoudelijk te ontwikkelen. Ze zijn dagelijks bezig met serieuze zaken en intelligente oplossingen voor complexe uitdagingen, maar op hun kantoren heerst een informele en prettige sfeer waarin humor een belangrijke rol speelt.Gezien hun diverse klant portfolio van middelgrote vennootschappen, werk je in een prettige omgeving waar je veel kan leren. Hiernaast lopen er meerdere RA en AA accountants rond, waar jij alles aan kan vragen zodat je veel kan leren en kan doorgroeien.jouw rol in het kort:Vanaf het begin wordt je betrokken bij klantbezoeken.Je voert controle- en assurance-werkzaamheden uit.Documenteert bevindingen en conclusies volgens richtlijnen en kwaliteitsnormen.Je signaleert mogelijkheden voor advies.Wat wij zoeken:We zoeken iemand die houdt van inhoudelijke uitdagingen en ambitie heeft om door te groeien.Je vindt een goede balans tussen eigen verantwoordelijkheid en samenwerking in een team, met ondersteuning van ervaren collega’s.Je hebt een hbo/wo-diploma in accountancy of bent hiermee bezig.Kritisch denkvermogen en nauwkeurigheid zijn voor ons essentieel.Wat zij jou bieden:Een salaris tussen de €2700 en €3300, afhankelijk van je ervaring.Volledige vergoeding van studiekosten, zodat je je continu kunt ontwikkelen.Bij een fulltime dienstverband heb je recht op 28 vakantiedagen + vakantiegeld, en een extra beloning van 2 vakantiedagen als je gedurende het jaar niet ziek bent geweest.Een moderne laptop wordt verstrekt voor flexibiliteit en efficiëntie in je werk.Zowel kilometervergoeding als een vaste vergoeding voor openbaar vervoer voor je woon-werkverkeer.Een tijd-voor-tijd regeling voor een eerlijke compensatie van overuren, voor een gezonde werk-privébalans.Een goede pensioenregeling voor een zekere financiële toekomst.Een moderne werkomgeving met sta-zit bureaus en de nieuwste technologie voor meer productiviteit en welzijn.Wekelijkse gezamenlijke lunch op verschillende locaties voor een beter teamgevoel en informele interactie tussen collega’s.Verschillende uitjes en evenementen door het jaar heen voor een positieve werksfeer en sterke teamgeest.Spreekt dit jou aan? Solliciteer dan snel, want de werving is al volop bezig! Mocht je vragen hebben bel dan naar +31 8500 490 15 (tussen 8:00 en 19:00) of neem een kijkje op de website van Brown & McCrow.Deze vacature wordt beheerd door Rodi en Madelief van Brown & McCrow. Dit is overigens het enige recruitment consultancy van Nederland dat enkel bemiddeld in de Accountancy branche. Jij wordt dus altijd geholpen door een specialist!(Jouw sollicitatie is volledig confidentieel. Wij informeren niemand over jouw kandidatuur zonder jouw toestemming.)

companyDescription:
We are a leading provider of insurance and reinsurance offering innovative risk management solutions for businesses worldwide. We partner with those who move the world forward, navigating complex risks and working across diverse industries to support and empower our clients.

Note: We are currently experiencing some technical issues with our recruitment platform which we hope to resolve shortly, please be patient with us, thank you for your interest in jobs at AXA XL.
descriptionHtml:
Amsterdam, Netherlands

Effective risk management involves a holistic view of various types of risks (e.g. insurance risk, operational risk, financial risk, etc.) and their interactions with each other. Enterprise Risk Management (ERM) enables informed strategic thinking, better decision making, and enhances the ability of the business to respond quickly in a dynamic and challenging environment.

You will be working in the Risk function, based in the Amsterdam office with a proportion of remote working possible, and be involved in a range of Risk activities for the XLICSE Benelux and Nordics branches, as the main focus, and for the wider risk function of XLICSE. The key role of the Risk Manager is the identification, management and monitoring of operational risks in the XLICSE Benelux and Nordics branches

You will report to the Deputy CRO XLICSE with a second reporting line to the local branch country manager.

What you’ll be doing

What will your essential responsibilities include?

  • Working effectively with the XLICSE Benelux and Nordics branches in the identification, management and monitoring of operational risks. This will include coordinating the submission of operational incidents, production of the annual Operational Risk Assessment, presentation at local governance forums and tracking of these risks.
  • Member of the Benelux and Nordics branch Leadership team representing the risk function which includes participation in Leadership team meetings, local governance forums and attendance at Northern Europe Leadership Team seminars.
  • Provide risk training as relevant at leadership team level and across the branch as required.
  • Production of the quarterly Benelux and Nordics branch risk dashboards and presentation at local branch governance forums and XLICSE Audit, Risk and Compliance Committee.
  • Contribute to 2nd opinion on business plan for XLICSE with identification of key local strategic risks for XLICSE Benelux and Nordics branches.
  • Support roll out of policies and procedures across the branch, including enhancement of risk culture.
  • Support roll out of internal control framework across the Benelux and Nordics branches, including testing as applicable for local controls.
  • While based in Amsterdam, you will travel to the Nordics branch (Stockholm) on a quarterly basis.
  • Working closely with the XLICSE Risk team based in Dublin in supporting XLICSE wide risk activities, e.g. project risk assessments, and collaborating with the other European Branch risk managers on emerging risks and issues.
  • Collaborating with the AXA XL Centres of Excellence, as required, for example information risk management.
  • Support any ad hoc regulatory request, locally or from the Central Bank of Ireland.
  • Engage with internal audit, as required.
  • As part of the ERM team, identify any shortfall in expected compliance with the emerging regulatory requirements and aid in developing plans to address any shortfall.

What you’ll bring

We’re looking for someone who has these abilities and skills:

Required Skills And Abilities

  • Proven experience of working in a Risk, Compliance or Internal Audit function for an insurance company is essential.
  • Enterprise Risk Management Framework experience, in particular the assessment of operational risk in a similar role.
  • Experience in assimilating significant amounts of information for problem solving.
  • Outstanding analysis skills (both quantitative and qualitative).
  • Clear communication, written and presentation, will be important for presenting results and solutions.
  • Thorough data interpretation skills and attention to detail are a must to ensure producing high quality, reliable analysis.

Desired Skills And Abilities

  • You will be working in a dynamic, geographically dispersed team so you will need to be highly adaptable and comfortable both working on your own initiative and as a keen team player.
  • Graduate degree and/or studying towards an insurance or Risk Management qualification (e.g. FRM, CFA).

Who we are

AXA XL, the P&C and specialty risk division of AXA, is known for solving complex risks. For mid-sized companies, multinationals and even some inspirational individuals we don’t just provide re/insurance, we reinvent it.

How? By combining a comprehensive and efficient capital platform, data-driven insights, leading technology, and the best talent in an agile and inclusive workspace, empowered to deliver top client service across all our lines of business − property, casualty, professional, financial lines and specialty.

With an innovative and flexible approach to risk solutions, we partner with those who move the world forward.

Learn more at axaxl.com

What we offer

Inclusion

AXA XL is committed to equal employment opportunity and will consider applicants regardless of gender, sexual orientation, age, ethnicity and origins, marital status, religion, disability, or any other protected characteristic. At AXA XL, we know that an inclusive culture and enables business growth and is critical to our success. That’s why we have made a strategic commitment to attract, develop, advance and retain the most inclusive workforce possible, and create a culture where everyone can bring their full selves to work and reach their highest potential. It’s about helping one another — and our business — to move forward and succeed.

  • Five Business Resource Groups focused on gender, LGBTQ+, ethnicity and origins, disability and inclusion with 20 Chapters around the globe.
  • Robust support for Flexible Working Arrangements
  • Enhanced family-friendly leave benefits
  • Named to the Diversity Best Practices Index
  • Signatory to the UK Women in Finance Charter

Learn more at axaxl.com/about-us/inclusion-and-diversity. AXA XL is an Equal Opportunity Employer.

Total Rewards

AXA XL’s Reward program is designed to take care of what matters most to you, covering the full picture of your health, wellbeing, lifestyle and financial security. It provides competitive compensation and personalized, inclusive benefits that evolve as you do.

We’re committed to rewarding your contribution for the long term, so you can be your best self today and look forward to the future with confidence.

Sustainability

At AXA XL, Sustainability is integral to our business strategy. In an ever-changing world, AXA XL protects what matters most for our clients and communities. We know that sustainability is at the root of a more resilient future. Our 2023-26 Sustainability strategy, called “Roots of resilience”, focuses on protecting natural ecosystems, addressing climate change, and embedding sustainable practices across our operations.

Our Pillars

  • Valuing nature: How we impact nature affects how nature impacts us. Resilient ecosystems - the foundation of a sustainable planet and society - are essential to our future. We’re committed to protecting and restoring nature - from mangrove forests to the bees in our backyard - by increasing biodiversity awareness and inspiring clients and colleagues to put nature at the heart of their plans.
  • Addressing climate change: The effects of a changing climate are far-reaching and significant. Unpredictable weather, increasing temperatures, and rising sea levels cause both social inequalities and environmental disruption. We're building a net zero strategy, developing insurance products and services, and mobilizing to advance thought leadership and investment in societal-led solutions.
  • Integrating ESG: All companies have a role to play in building a more resilient future. Incorporating ESG considerations into our internal processes and practices builds resilience from the roots of our business. We’re training our colleagues, engaging our external partners, and evolving our sustainability governance and reporting.
  • AXA Hearts in Action: We have established volunteering and charitable giving programs to help colleagues support causes that matter most to them, known as AXA XL’s “Hearts in Action” programs. These include our Matching Gifts program, Volunteering Leave, and our annual volunteering day - the Global Day of Giving.

For more information, please see axaxl.com/sustainability.

companyDescription:
LEMON is partner for financial talents and organisations that want to move forward. With LIME at our side, we are the business partner, coach and advisor for the finance community in the Netherlands: from ambitious top talents (LEMON) to experienced CFOs (LIME). Together, we encourage talent and challenge finance executives to make a real impact. 

Our network consists of ambitious financials who are ready for a challenging second or third step in their career. The salary range in which we operate varies from approximately €70k to €100k base salary. This includes positions such as: Head of Finance, (group) Business Controller, (group) Financial Controller, ESG Controller, M&A Analyst and Data Analytics & Business Intelligence positions.

www.lemonsearch.nl
+31 (0) 20 661 43 43

The world is changing
People are changing
Needs are changing
Goals are changing

Attracting talented professionals or making the right career choices is more challenging than ever. This requires a talent coaching and recruitment partner that really understands, and who goes the extra mile to identify and encourage talent, and help others realize their ambitions. 

We are LEMON, partner of financial talents and companies who want to move forward.
And, just like us, believe in the power of creating valuable connections in which everyone’s potential is maximised to grow together.

With LIME by our side, we are able to grow, learn and have fun. And we seize every opportunity to be better, do better and get better. Together. This is how we empower teams and encourage unique talents to achieve more.

Because they are the future. And we make sure they are ready for it.

LEMON: empowering the future.

descriptionHtml:
Red Bull staat al bijna vier decennia synoniem voor energie, creativiteit en grensverleggende ideeën. De lancering in Oostenrijk in 1987 betekende het ontstaan van een geheel nieuwe productcategorie: energiedranken. Sindsdien heeft Red Bull de wereld veroverd en is het uitgegroeid tot marktleider met een aanwezigheid in meer dan 175 landen. Het iconische blauw-zilveren blikje met de rode stieren dat vleugels geeft., staat vandaag voor veel meer dan alleen een drankje: het opent de deur naar een merkwereld vol sport, muziek, gaming en events die wereldwijd miljoenen mensen inspireren. Met een jaarlijkse omzet van € 270 miljoen heeft Red Bull een sterke positie in een snelgroeiende markt. Om het succes van Red Bull in Nederland verder uit te bouwen, zoeken we een Financial Controller die met scherpte en energie, structuur en balans brengt.


In het kort:

  • Betrokken bij de periodieke afsluitingen, rapportages en analyses en het coördineren van de Planning & Control-cyclus
  • Beheren van de volledige accountingcyclus, van jaarafsluiting en externe controles tot rapportages, betaalrun en liquiditeitsbeheer
  • Data quality: CPM, masterdata en contractmanagement, rapportages en compliance tijdens de maandafsluiting
  • Stakeholdermanagement: Head of Accounting & Taxes, Head of Control, Business Control Team en externe accountants


In deze rol werk je nauw samen met de Head of Accounting & Taxes en fungeer je als haar rechterhand. Jij bent het financiële geweten van Red Bull Nederland op het gebied van accounting, reporting en datakwaliteit. Daarbij bouw je mee aan een solide en toekomstbestendig financieel fundament dat verdere groei en succes mogelijk maakt.


Je neemt de lead in de maand- en jaarafsluitingen, ondersteunt bij het opstellen van de jaarrekening en coördineert interne en externe controles. Je verzorgt rapportages richting het Red Bull-hoofdkantoor, het CBS vanwege de opgaveplicht en verzekeraars. In samenwerking met Business Control vertaal je cijfers naar scherpe inzichten die het management voorzien van de juiste handvatten voor besluitvorming en efficiency. Je vervult een coördinerende rol tijdens de Planning en Control-cyclus en bent zelf op termijn verantwoordelijk voor de Balans planning. Je werkt mee aan verbeterprojecten binnen Finance en draagt zelf proactief verbeteringen aan. Daarnaast voer je de betaalruns uit, beheer je de statiegeldadministratie en bewaak je de liquiditeits- en cashpositie. Ook de fiscale aangiftes vallen onder jouw verantwoordelijkheid, waarbij je de organisatie proactief adviseert over actuele wet- en regelgeving.


Je hebt daarnaast een sleutelrol in het waarborgen van datakwaliteit. Je zorgt dat het CPM-systeem (Customer Profitability Management maakt de kosten en opbrengsten op klantniveau zichtbaar) op product- en prijsniveau aansluit op de aanwezige contracten en beheert account- en masterdata. Daarmee garandeer je dat Red Bull altijd kan sturen op accurate, betrouwbare en bruikbare informatie: een onmisbare basis voor de verdere groei van het merk.


Goed om te weten

In deze rol sta je midden in het financiële hart van Red Bull Nederland. Je zorgt voor een solide accountingbasis, van nauwkeurige afsluitingen en betrouwbare rapportages tot strakke processen en vertaalt die naar inzichten die de business verder brengen. Zo combineer je vakmanschap in accounting met de kans om impact te maken in een ambitieuze en dynamische organisatie, waar volop ruimte is om te leren en te groeien.


Wie ben jij?

Je combineert een scherp oog voor detail met een sterk analytisch vermogen en weet daarmee echt het verschil te maken. Als zelfstarter neem je initiatief, leer je snel en pak je nieuwe onderwerpen met energie op. Je legt makkelijk contact met collega’s en bent altijd nieuwsgierig naar het grotere geheel. In een dynamische omgeving houd je moeiteloos het overzicht en werk je gestructureerd en nauwkeurig, ook wanneer de druk toeneemt. Resultaten behalen en kwaliteit leveren geven jou energie, en je streeft er steeds naar jezelf verder te ontwikkelen.


“Bij Red Bull staan onze mensen centraal: zij brengen het merk écht tot leven. Red Bull Nederland is een plek waar je hard werkt, mooie resultaten boekt en waar we samen het onmogelijke mogelijk maken. Ons team bestaat uit dynamische en creatieve mensen die het merk laten groeien, unieke producten en ervaringen neerzetten en zo mensen én ideeën vleugels geven.”


  • Je hebt een afgeronde hbo-/wo-opleiding
  • > 4 jaar ervaring in audit of een financiële functie
  • Je bent sterk in Excel en hebt een natuurlijke affiniteit met cijfers.
  • Ervaring met SAP of een vergelijkbaar ERP-systeem is mooi meegenomen.
  • Je werkt nauwkeurig en analytisch, en zet deze kwaliteiten in om verbeteringen en efficiëntie te realiseren.
  • Je communiceert gemakkelijk met zowel collega’s als externe partijen en bent nieuwsgierig naar het bredere plaatje.
  • Je bent proactief, houdt het hoofd koel onder druk en werkt gestructureerd in een dynamische omgeving.
  • Je bent resultaatgericht en kwaliteitsbewust, altijd op zoek naar kansen om zaken slimmer en beter te doen.


Meer weten?

Neem contact met Nigel Pracht: 06 - 14 77 70 72 / n.pracht@lemonsearch.nl

Ons team bestaat uit dynamische en creatieve mensen die het merk laten groeien, unieke producten en ervaringen neerzetten en zo mensen én ideeën vleugels geven.”Je hebt een afgeronde hbo-/wo-opleiding > 4 jaar ervaring in audit of een financiële functieJe bent sterk in Excel en hebt een natuurlijke affiniteit met cijfers.Ervaring met SAP of een vergelijkbaar ERP-systeem is mooi meegenomen.Je werkt nauwkeurig en analytisch, en zet deze kwaliteiten in om verbeteringen en efficiëntie te realiseren.Je communiceert gemakkelijk met zowel collega’s als externe partijen en bent nieuwsgierig naar het bredere plaatje.Je bent proactief, houdt het hoofd koel onder druk en werkt gestructureerd in een dynamische omgeving.Je bent resultaatgericht en kwaliteitsbewust, altijd op zoek naar kansen om zaken slimmer en beter te doen.Meer weten?Neem contact met Nigel Pracht: 06 - 14 77 70 72 / n.pracht@lemonsearch.nl

descriptionHtml:
📍 Financial Accountant | Amsterdam (On-site)

🕒 Full-time (40 hours) | Yoursafe – Licensed Fintech | www.yoursafe.com


Join a purpose-driven fintech reshaping financial access for all.


At Yoursafe, we believe financial services should be secure, inclusive, and accessible—no matter your background, gender, or identity. As a fast-growing, DNB-licensed Electronic Money Institution, we’re looking for a Financial Accountant to strengthen our Amsterdam-based finance team.


🔍 What you’ll do

Reporting to the CFO, you'll play a key role in our Finance operations—combining precision, autonomy, and the opportunity to help shape our financial processes. Your work will touch every part of our finance function:

  • Own general ledger tasks and support monthly/yearly closing cycles
  • Prepare financial statements and assist with tax filings
  • Manage payment flows and reconciliations
  • Drive automation in finance processes
  • Liaise with auditors and external partners
  • Support financial analysis and reports for leadership


💡 What makes this role different?

You’ll operate at the heart of a fintech that handles high-risk transactions responsibly—including solutions for regulated online entertainment businesses. You’ll learn fast, contribute to DNB-aligned compliance frameworks, and help elevate finance in a tech-driven environment.


✅ What you bring

We’re looking for someone who thrives in dynamic settings and gets energized by building and improving:

  • Degree in Finance, Accounting, or Business Economics (HBO/WO)
  • 3–5 years of experience in accounting or financial control
  • Strong Excel skills; knowledge of Exact Online is a plus
  • Analytical, detail-oriented, and naturally organized
  • Proactive, collaborative, and solutions-focused
  • Comfortable working in English in an international team


🌍 Why Yoursafe?

  • A mission-driven fintech with global ambition
  • An international, inclusive culture with real impact
  • Competitive salary and benefits
  • Hybrid working & a great office with a view near central Amsterdam
  • Fresh, healthy & free lunches by our in-house chef
  • Opportunities to grow and innovate within the team


🚀 Ready to join?


Apply today and help us build a safer, smarter future for finance.


Kindly note: We do not work with recruiters or agencies for this role.

descriptionHtml:
Use your Internal Controls expertise in an Advisory role to help clients move forward and make a real impact. At Deloitte.

What impact will you make?

As a (Senior) Consultant in Controls Assurance, you operate in an advisory role. You use your knowledge of accounting, internal control, and finance maturity to advise clients on internal controls over processes that lead to financial reporting. You investigate complex issues, make recommendations and observations, and translate them into powerful and pragmatic solutions for the client. You provide valuable insights and advice to enhance the finance operating model, making day-to-day operations more efficient and effective. Your focus is not only on professional aspects; you also play a crucial role in cooperating with the client. Through your involvement in various advisory projects, you continuously expand your expertise, enabling you to offer high-quality advisory services that drive meaningful improvements for clients.

How do you do this?

  • Assist in setting up documentation and evaluation of risk- and control frameworks at entity-level and process level.
  • Review and test internal controls to support the rationalization or improvement of the control framework.
  • Provide insights and advice to enhance the finance-operating model, making day-to-day operations more efficient and effective for the organization.
  • Assist in enterprise risk management evaluation, targeting top-level strategic risks.

Working within our Assurance team in Amsterdam means being part of a young, growing and ambitious community with highly motivated colleagues. From the moment you start, you will contribute to building our integrated, technology driven Controls Assurance service offering at Deloitte Amsterdam.

What’s in it for you?

  • You’ll receive a profit-sharing bonus. On top of your fixed salary.
  • Continuous professional growth. Join our development program.
  • A work-from-home office setup allowance to make sure you have everything you need for an ergonomically designed workstation and internet allowance.
  • Work part-time (32 hours a week) or full-time (40-hours a week).
  • Flexible working hours, you are in charge of your own calendar.
  • 26 days of paid annual leave, and the opportunity to purchase additional leave.
  • The option to exchange three national holidays for three non-national holidays.
  • A good mobility scheme: the choice of various options such as a lease car, travel by public transport, a cash option or a combination of these.
  • A laptop and iPhone. The iPhone can be for personal use.
  • A time for time arrangement that creates flexibility for personal moments that matter.
  • A good pension scheme with a personal contribution of only 2%. For a comfortable future.
  • An opportunity to take part in our collective health insurance scheme.
  • An opportunity to benefit from tax-efficient facilities such as fitness, a bicycle scheme or the opportunity to lease a bicycle.
  • The opportunity to use 55 hours of babysitting service per calendar year, if your child is 12 years old or younger.
  • A flexible budget, which you can use to make choices in flexible benefits, for example: purchasing extra leave days or financing a bicycle plan.
  • Six weeks of fully paid birth leave for traditional households and rainbow families.

Be the true you

You know how to inspire others and always remain detail-oriented. Above all, you dare to be yourself—the true you—with your own strengths, worldview, and personality. Furthermore, as a (Senior) Consultant in our Controls Assurance team, you should possess the following qualifications:

  • A completed (Post) Master’s degree in Accountancy, Controlling, Finance, or Economics, with eligibility to qualify as CA (SA)/CPA, or nearing completion of RA or ACCA education.
  • At least 3 years of experience in Audit, Internal Control, or a similar role, preferably with Big 4 experience.
  • Hands-on experience in setting up, documenting, and evaluating risk and control frameworks over financial reporting at both entity-level and process-level. This includes familiarity with methods for identifying, assessing, and mitigating related risks, as well as familiarity with frameworks such as COSO.
  • Knowledge of financial systems and processes, with the ability to provide insights into improving and automating financial operations.
  • Strong analytical skills, problem identification and solving abilities, and close attention to detail.
  • Ability to effectively communicate insights and advice to stakeholders at various levels of the organization.
  • Commitment to learning and applying new skills and technologies to improve processes and operations.
  • Good knowledge of financial reporting standards (IFRS; NL GAAP and US GAAP).
  • Relevant work experience with GRC software (e.g. Workiva) with a focus on optimizing and automating business processes, software selections, and digital transformations is a plus.
  • Excellent command of English, both spoken and written; proficiency in Dutch is also beneficial.

(W)here

You share your expertise with the 1,800 colleagues of our Audit & Assurance department. Within our teams, cooperation and personal development are key. With over 7,500 people in 15 offices across the Netherlands, Deloitte is one of the largest providers of professional services. Our work areas are Audit & Assurance, Strategy, Risk & Transaction Advisory, Support, Tax & Legal, Technology & Transformation.

At Deloitte we work hybrid. This means that we combine working at the office, at the client, and at home.

Let's get down to business

We look forward to receiving your application for this position. At Deloitte, we welcome everyone who can bring quality and ambition. We'd like to know who you are.

Contact

  • Lisa van der Laan
  • Experienced Recruiter Audit & Assurance
  • lvanderlaan@deloitte.nl
  • +31650040024

descriptionHtml:
As a Supervisor Audit, you’ll take charge of engagements from start to finish. You’ll advise clients directly, guide your team, and ensure every project is completed on time and within budget. Each week, you’ll visit a different client — from a major aquaculture company to a small childcare provider — gaining broad insight into how various organizations operate and grow.


You’ll identify risks, analyze trends, and turn them into clear, practical advice. Whether you’re optimizing financial processes, supporting compliance, or fine-tuning reporting — you’ll be your client’s trusted sparring partner.


In this role, you’ll mentor junior team members, plan and coordinate audits, and report to your manager. Your leadership makes the difference in how our clients experience quality and care.


Who you are

  • Currently have your CA(SA) qualification
  • Several years of relevant experience in audit or assurance
  • Strong communicator — in English, and ideally open to learning Dutch
  • Naturally collaborative, client-focused, curious, and analytical
  • Proactive and eager to coach, solve problems, and lead by example


What we offer

  • Competitive salary (€3.624 - €5.575 gross/month + 8.33% holiday allowance)
  • Flexible (hybrid) working hours – You decide when and where you work best
  • 32 vacation days (incl. 12 customizable with your personal budget)
  • Attractive mobility options – Lease car, NS Business Card, or lease bike
  • Growth & development – Access to top training and learning programs
  • Fully equipped home office – Laptop, iPhone & everything you need to start strong


Relocating? We’ve got you.

We welcome international professionals — and if you're relocating from abroad, we offer full sponsorship and relocation support. Dutch language training is included, and your onboarding will be fully supported in English.


Your application journey

  1. Apply via this page
  2. Introductory call with our recruiter
  3. First interview via Teams
  4. Take a short e-assessment
  5. Follow-up interview to dive deeper
  6. Receive a personalized offer
  7. Welcome to Baker Tilly!


📩 Ready to take the lead in audit — and in your career?

Apply now and start your next chapter with Baker Tilly.

descriptionHtml:
Jouw carrière in Finance: Ambitie? Check. Auto? Check


Ben jij klaar om je talent in de praktijk te brengen en écht impact te maken? Bij Hermans & Partners doen we het graag nét even anders. Wij zijn een snelgroeiend MKB-kantoor waar professionaliteit en plezier hand in hand gaan. Hier krijg je de ruimte om te groeien, samen te werken met ervaren collega’s én je eigen stempel te drukken. En als kers op de taart: een super hippe bedrijfsauto staat voor je klaar!


Dit ga je doen

  • Als Junior Audit Specialist stap je midden in de dynamiek van de accountancy. Je controleert jaarrekeningen en beoordeelt complexere posten en cijferopstellingen.
  • Werkt nauw samen met accountants, fiscalisten en juristen aan uiteenlopende opdrachten.
  • Leert hoe je cijfers vertaalt naar waardevol advies voor ondernemers.
  • Kortom: jij helpt ondernemers inzicht en vooruitzicht te krijgen.


Dit ben jij

Jij hebt oog voor detail, een natuurlijke affiniteit met cijfers én het vermogen om helder en professioneel te communiceren. Of je nu je MBO4-opleiding (Junior Assistent Accountant of vergelijkbaar), HBO Finance & Control, Bedrijfseconomie, Accountancy (of vergelijkbaar) al hebt afgerond, of er bijna bent – je bent klaar voor de volgende stap. Daarnaast ben je:

  • Ambitieus, kwaliteitsgericht en proactief.
  • Leergierig en communicatief sterk.
  • Resultaatgericht, maar ook een echte teamspeler.
  • Woonachtig in (de omgeving van) Rotterdam.
  • En niet te vergeten: je brengt een gezonde dosis humor en gezelligheid mee!


Waarom Hermans & Partners?

Bij ons staat jouw ontwikkeling centraal en zorgen we voor een fijne werk-privébalans. Wat je kunt verwachten:

  • Goede arbeidsvoorwaarden: een mooi salaris passend bij jouw kennis en ervaring, een auto en telefoon van de zaak, pensioenregeling én flexdagen.
  • Werk-privé balans: overwerken is bij ons niet standaard.
  • Talentontwikkeling: wij investeren in jouw groei en persoonlijke vaardigheden.
  • Een gezellig en professioneel team: korte lijnen, veel humor en fijne collega’s.
  • Leuke cliënten en uitdagende opdrachten die jouw werkdag interessant houden.
  • Personeelsuitjes: van fanatiek tot ontspannen (en ja, dit is hét moment om je leidinggevende te verslaan 😉).


Klaar om je carrière een kickstart te geven?

Sluit je dan aan bij Hermans & Partners en bouw samen met ons aan een succesvolle toekomst.

Benieuwd naar meer? Neem contact op met Martin den Hoed van Louter Professionals:

📞 088 206 3199 | 📱 06 57398256 (ook WhatsApp!)

✉️ m.denhoed@louterprofessionals.nl

companyDescription:
Sympower accelerates Europe’s energy transition towards a climate-neutral future by providing flexibility services to support smarter, renewable energy systems. Our end-to-end services encompass BESS multi-market optimisation, demand response services, and optimisation for renewable energy providers. By managing over 2.5GW of energy assets across Europe, we’re enabling our customers and partners to generate long-term revenue.

For over a decade, we have optimised hundreds of energy assets for dozens of industries on multiple European energy markets, providing customised solutions tailored to the needs of our customers and partners.

As a route-to-market provider and optimiser, we prioritise the success of our customers and guarantee the biggest impact towards building a truly sustainable energy system.

descriptionHtml:
Job Description

The Financial Controller is responsible for accounting and control of our entities in Europe. This includes timely closure of the accounting period, internal controls, tax reporting, accounts filling and collaboration on various initiatives. Join our growing international team and help us with the energy transition!

What Is In It For You

We are committed to creating an inclusive and values-based culture where everyone feels that they belong and where everyone has the opportunity to do meaningful work.

We offer a market competitive compensation package, including but not limited to:

  • 30 Days Paid Holiday Leave
  • 1 Day Paid Wellness Leave
  • 1 Day Paid Birthday Leave
  • Paid Maternity and Partner Leave
  • Pawternity Leave
  • Mental Health and Wellbeing Support
  • Remote Office Budget
  • Internet Allowance
  • Development Plan & Budget
  • Stock Appreciation Rights
  • 2 Days Paid Volunteer Leave

Learn about all of our benefits on our careers page.

What You Will Do

  • Manage financial month end close processes including preparation and posting of journal entries, accruals and account reconciliations.
  • Manage monthly payroll checks and posting in multiple jurisdictions.
  • Take ownership of the quality of the accounting data, internal controls and financial compliance in several European subsidiaries.
  • Prepare information and coordinate filling of the VAT returns.
  • Coordinate annual income tax return preparation and filling.
  • Coordinate statutory financial statement preparation.
  • Manage subsidiaries’ liquidity.
  • Support the year-end audit for the Group.
  • Collaborate with the local teams and wider organisations on projects with any financial insights needed both for financial planning/analytics.
  • Manage relationships with local accounting and tax advisors.
  • Take initiatives in process improvements and support the implementations where needed.
  • Support Accounting & Reporting Manager and the larger team as needed and more!

Job requirements

What You Will Need

  • Bachelor’s degree or higher in Finance/Accounting and/or professional accounting qualification
  • 5+ Accounting/Controlling experience in an international environment.
  • Strong technical skills (systems as well as excel/google sheets).
  • NetSuite experience is an advantage.
  • Strong communication skills and willingness to work as a team.
  • Detailed orientated self-starter with strong focus on timely and quality deliverables.
  • Fluent in English. Other languages are nice to have.

Who We Are

Sympower is accelerating the global transition towards ‘net-zero’ by helping to build smarter, cleaner renewable energy systems. Using our proprietary software platform, we help balance the supply and demand of electricity across international energy networks.

We help businesses, grid operators, asset owners and other energy stakeholders around the world reduce their carbon emissions, integrate more distributed renewable energy resources, and generate new revenue streams by participating in demand-side response services. Learn more about us in this video.

In 2022, Sympower became a certified B Corp, which shows the company is meeting high standards of verified performance, accountability, and transparency across 5 impact areas: governance, workers, community, environment, and customers.

Sympower is an equal opportunity employer. We encourage a diverse workforce and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees.

Your personal data will be processed in accordance with our Privacy Policy.

MESSAGE TO RECRUITMENT AGENCIES: support for filling this position is not required, so proposals for recruitment services will not be reviewed or responded to.
