descriptionHtml:
Location: Rotterdam, the Netherlands


Job Overview

Responsible for comprehensive finance operation tasks and support monthly-closing and reporting, ensuring the accuracy of transactions and monitoring the process compliance internally.


Responsibilities

  • Responsible for the daily finance operation including AR, AP transactions and bank reconciliation
  • Support month closing and reporting
  • Assist with cashflow forecast and weekly payment arrangement
  • Responsible for sorting and reviewing abnormal data and entry input and guide related stakeholder to modify in system
  • Assist with formulating process control framework and further implementation.


Qualifications

  • Have 2+ years of relevant experience and in finance function
  • Above bachelor’s degree in finance, business management or related field.
  • Excellent Chinese and English in written and verbal communication skills.
  • Great teamwork and attend to details.
  • Good working mentality, serious and responsible work, be meticulous.
  • Excellent time management ability, able to stream out the priorities
  • Structured thinker and deliver assignment accordingly
  • Be able to use various office software and various financial software proficiently, and have basic network knowledge.


What we offer

  • Targeted development opportunities on a professional level;
  • Competitive compensation and benefits;
  • A pleasant and international atmosphere.

descriptionHtml:
Wil jij samen met collega’s uitdagende opdrachten voor klanten uitvoeren? Wil je jezelf verder ontwikkelen in je vak en ben je op zoek naar een werkgever die je vrijheid en verantwoordelijkheid geeft? Dan is onze vacature accountant / relatiebeheerder voor onze vestigingen in Nijmegen, Schijndel of Den Bosch wat voor jou!

Waarom kiezen voor accountant / relatiebeheerder bij BGH?

Als accountant / relatiebeheerder onderhoud je zelfstandig het klantcontact met een diverse klantengroep. Je werkt samen met collega’s aan uitdagende accountancy- en adviesopdrachten. Je bent een vakkundige sparringpartner voor je klanten en je stuurt een enthousiast team van 4 assistent accountants aan. Je motiveert je team om een goed resultaat neer te zetten en je verzorgt de planning en facturatie van jouw team. Dat zorgt ervoor dat je een dynamische baan hebt, waarbij je jezelf zowel vakinhoudelijk als op gebied van leiderschap kunt ontwikkelen.

“BGH is een platte organisatie en dat is prettig. Ongeacht je rol, ervaring en leeftijd kan iedereen zijn of haar input geven.” Nicky Terburg – Accountant

De mensen maken het bedrijf. Bij BGH zijn we blij met al onze collega’s en die waardering spreken we ook uit. Plezier in je werk vinden we namelijk het allerbelangrijkste. We verzetten samen het werk. We hebben oog voor elkaar én voor onze klanten. De sfeer is goed en je krijgt veel vrijheid om je eigen werk in te delen. We stimuleren persoonlijke ontwikkeling op basis van jouw eigen doelen en wensen. Kortom, bij BGH ben je verzekerd van leuke en behulpzame collega’s, uitdagende opdrachten en uiteenlopende klanten.

Dit ben jij

  • Je hebt minimaal een HBO opleiding accountancy, bedrijfseconomie of vergelijkbaar afgerond.
  • Je studeert voor Registeraccountant of gaat dit doen.
  • Je hebt een aantal jaar relevante werkervaring.
  • Je wilt jezelf verder ontwikkelen in de controlepraktijk bij MKB klanten en de non-profit sector.
  • Je kunt goed mondeling en schriftelijk communiceren.
  • Je bent kritisch, nauwkeurig en analytisch sterk.
  • Je vind het leuk om samen met een team te werken.


Fijne arbeidsvoorwaarden en goed salaris

  • Een team van ervaren en behulpzame collega’s
  • Uitdagende en uiteenlopende klanten
  • Goed salaris dat bij je niveau en ervaring past
  • Vast contract
  • Goede pensioenregeling
  • Telefoon en laptop
  • Leaseauto of reiskostenvergoeding
  • Thuiswerkvergoeding
  • 27 vakantiedagen
  • Sporten met een personal trainer (1 keer per week op kantoor) en massage op kantoor
  • Flexibele werktijden
  • Wij betalen de kosten voor een opleiding en de helft van je studie-uren


Interesse?

Heb je interesse in onze vacature assistent accountant audit? Solliciteer dan snel!

companyDescription:
Yokogawa provides advanced solutions in the areas of measurement, control, and information to customers across a broad range of industries, including energy, chemicals, materials, pharmaceuticals, and food. Yokogawa addresses customer issues regarding the optimization of production, assets, and the supply chain with the effective application of digital technologies, enabling the transition to autonomous operations.

Founded in Tokyo in 1915, Yokogawa continues to work toward a sustainable society through its 17,000+ employees in a global network of 126 companies spanning 60 countries.
descriptionHtml:
Yokogawa is looking for a

Junior Internal Auditor

Fulltime + Travelling

You finished your studies in Finance and have some years of experience in Finance and/or Auditing. You wish to work at a large corporation, where you can learn a lot about various parts of business processes of various companies. For our Internal Audit Department, we are looking for an Internal Auditor within Yokogawa Europe who can work independently and precise and can travel a week abroad per audit.

How does your role as Internal Auditor look like?

You will conduct financial and operational audits on topics such as Business Process reviews, Accounting, Governance and Risk Management. The audits will be performed together with your manager. After completing the audit, the feedback will be given to local management and together you formulate the suggestions for improvements. It is important that the reporting is correct. Especially as the Internal Auditing Department directly reports to Tokyo (where the global HQ is located).

These audits are not only performed remotely, but you also conduct some at Yokogawa locations throughout Europe, such as Madrid, Spain and Izmir in Turkey. As you are working with different cultures at every location, you are quickly able to adapt and keep a strong relationship with the stakeholders there, while maintaining the high standard that is expected from you. As travel is a part of your role, setting priorities and working structurally is a key aspect of the job.

Finally, you will be asked to join process-based special audits if there are any suspicions that things seem to go wrong. This can be from project reviews up to (possible) fraud assessments. For this, you have to be able to act on facts, not on emotion while also being able to make decisions quickly.

You can make a difference! How?

By being the right fit for the organization Yokogawa! We offer you the chance to learn everything there is to know, but we have to make sure that what you are looking for is represented in the culture of Yokogawa. This can be described best as informal, people-oriented and open. It does not matter who you want to talk to, because you can talk to everybody! Of course, we have a certain company structure, but we treat everybody the same. Opportunities to develop yourself and to grow within Yokogawa are very present. A development plan will be discussed and tailor-made for you.

Does this sound great? Great! All we ask for is the following:

  • A BSc/MSc in Finance, Accounting or Economics
  • 2 to 5 years of experience in finance, ideally with some experience with auditing
  • Full proficiency in English
  • Proficient in Word, Excel, PowerPoint, and Outlook
  • The ability to travel throughout Europe for work once in a while (10-15%).

It would be considered an advantage when you also have experience with SAP and knowledge of Risk Management.

What is Yokogawa offering you?

Yokogawa is offering you the chance to learn everything about auditing in a role covering all of Europe. You will travel frequently (one week per 2 months) and get to know the organization in a unique way.

Focus on a brighter future

Yokogawa Europe is a global supplier of process automation solutions and safety systems. Yokogawa will focus on achieving net-zero emissions, making a transition to a circular economy, ensuring the wellbeing of everyone to 2050, thus making the world a better place for future generations.

Interested?

Please sent your application letter and resume by clicking the "Apply Now" button.

An assessment will be part of the procedure.

Not just a job, but a career

Yokogawa, award winner for ‘Best Asset Monitoring Technology’ and ‘Best Digital Twin Technology’ at the HP Awards, is a leading provider of industrial automation, test and measurement, information systems and industrial services in several industries.

Our aim is to shape a better future for our planet through supporting the energy transition, (bio)technology, artificial intelligence, industrial cybersecurity, etc. We are committed to the United Nations sustainable development goals by utilizing our ability to measure and connect.

About The Team

Our 18,000 employees work in over 60 countries with one corporate mission, to "co-innovate tomorrow". We are looking for dynamic colleagues who share our passion for technology and care for our planet. In return, we offer you great career opportunities to grow yourself in a truly global culture where respect, value creation, collaboration, integrity, and gratitude are highly valued and exhibited in everything we do.

Diverse, Equitable & Inclusive culture

Yokogawa is an Equal Opportunity Employer. Yokogawa wants a diverse, equitable and inclusive culture. We will actively recruit, develop, and promote people from a variety of backgrounds who differ in terms of experience, knowledge, thinking styles, perspective, cultural background, and socioeconomic status. We will not discriminate based on race, skin color, age, sex, gender identity and expression, sexual orientation, religion, belief, political opinion, nationality, ethnicity, place of origin, disability, family relations or any other circumstances. Yokogawa values differences and enables everyone to belong, contribute, succeed, and demonstrate their full potential.

about our Employee Referral process!
descriptionText: Yokogawa is looking for aJunior Internal AuditorFulltime + TravellingYou finished your studies in Finance and have some years of experience in Finance and/or Auditing. You wish to work at a large corporation, where you can learn a lot about various parts of business processes of various companies. For our Internal Audit Department, we are looking for an Internal Auditor within Yokogawa Europe who can work independently and precise and can travel a week abroad per audit.How does your role as Internal Auditor look like?You will conduct financial and operational audits on topics such as Business Process reviews, Accounting, Governance and Risk Management. The audits will be performed together with your manager. After completing the audit, the feedback will be given to local management and together you formulate the suggestions for improvements. It is important that the reporting is correct. Especially as the Internal Auditing Department directly reports to Tokyo (where the global HQ is located).These audits are not only performed remotely, but you also conduct some at Yokogawa locations throughout Europe, such as Madrid, Spain and Izmir in Turkey. As you are working with different cultures at every location, you are quickly able to adapt and keep a strong relationship with the stakeholders there, while maintaining the high standard that is expected from you. As travel is a part of your role, setting priorities and working structurally is a key aspect of the job.Finally, you will be asked to join process-based special audits if there are any suspicions that things seem to go wrong. This can be from project reviews up to (possible) fraud assessments. For this, you have to be able to act on facts, not on emotion while also being able to make decisions quickly.You can make a difference! How?By being the right fit for the organization Yokogawa! We offer you the chance to learn everything there is to know, but we have to make sure that what you are looking for is represented in the culture of Yokogawa. This can be described best as informal, people-oriented and open. It does not matter who you want to talk to, because you can talk to everybody! Of course, we have a certain company structure, but we treat everybody the same. Opportunities to develop yourself and to grow within Yokogawa are very present. A development plan will be discussed and tailor-made for you.Does this sound great? Great! All we ask for is the following:A BSc/MSc in Finance, Accounting or Economics2 to 5 years of experience in finance, ideally with some experience with auditingFull proficiency in EnglishProficient in Word, Excel, PowerPoint, and OutlookThe ability to travel throughout Europe for work once in a while (10-15%).It would be considered an advantage when you also have experience with SAP and knowledge of Risk Management.What is Yokogawa offering you?Yokogawa is offering you the chance to learn everything about auditing in a role covering all of Europe. You will travel frequently (one week per 2 months) and get to know the organization in a unique way.Focus on a brighter futureYokogawa Europe is a global supplier of process automation solutions and safety systems. Yokogawa will focus on achieving net-zero emissions, making a transition to a circular economy, ensuring the wellbeing of everyone to 2050, thus making the world a better place for future generations.Interested?Please sent your application letter and resume by clicking the "Apply Now" button.An assessment will be part of the procedure.Not just a job, but a careerYokogawa, award winner for ‘Best Asset Monitoring Technology’ and ‘Best Digital Twin Technology’ at the HP Awards, is a leading provider of industrial automation, test and measurement, information systems and industrial services in several industries.Our aim is to shape a better future for our planet through supporting the energy transition, (bio)technology, artificial intelligence, industrial cybersecurity, etc. We are committed to the United Nations sustainable development goals by utilizing our ability to measure and connect.About The TeamOur 18,000 employees work in over 60 countries with one corporate mission, to "co-innovate tomorrow". We are looking for dynamic colleagues who share our passion for technology and care for our planet. In return, we offer you great career opportunities to grow yourself in a truly global culture where respect, value creation, collaboration, integrity, and gratitude are highly valued and exhibited in everything we do.Diverse, Equitable & Inclusive cultureYokogawa is an Equal Opportunity Employer. Yokogawa wants a diverse, equitable and inclusive culture. We will actively recruit, develop, and promote people from a variety of backgrounds who differ in terms of experience, knowledge, thinking styles, perspective, cultural background, and socioeconomic status. descriptionHtml:
Voor CCV zoek ik een ervaren Business Partner Risk & Compliance met focus op KYC (Know your customer). Jij hebt gevoel voor processen, kent de relevante wet- en regelgeving en weet hoe je risico’s scherp houdt zonder te verzanden in vinklijstjes. In deze rol coach je de business en werk je aan duurzame verbeteringen, niet vanaf de zijlijn maar samen met je interne klant.

Werken bij CCV betekent impact maken in een internationale financiële omgeving, gewoon vanuit Arnhem.

Klaar voor inhoud, samenwerking en verandering? Lees dan verder.



Wat deze functie anders maakt

Geen check-the-box-rol, maar impactvolle verbeterslagen

In deze rol ben je een inhoudelijke sparringpartner voor proceseigenaren binnen het KYC-domein. Je denkt mee, toetst en adviseert over risicobeheersing en compliance zonder te verzanden in administratieve vinklijstjes. Je focust je op processen, samenwerking en op duurzame verbeteringen

Je werkt bij een internationale financiële speler in Arnhem

Een omgeving met schaal, complexiteit én stabiliteit, maar dan zónder dagelijkse files richting de randstad. Vanuit het hoofdkantoor in Arnhem werk je aan processen om risico’s te minimaliseren die breed binnen Europa spelen, mét ruimte voor hybride werken en verbinding met de operatie

Je bouwt mee aan verandering, mét de business

Deze functie is bij uitstek geschikt voor kandidaten met een achtergrond in compliance, business risk of audit die zich dichter op de operatie willen positioneren, én voor strategische teamleads binnen KYC die de stap willen maken richting bredere procesverantwoordelijkheid. Je staat niet langs de zijlijn, maar werkt actief samen met je interne klant: de Head of KYC. Je bent betrokken bij verbetertrajecten, rapportages en analyses en hebt dus écht een relatie met de afdeling waarop jij impact maakt



Wat je gaat doen

• Adviseren van proceseigenaren over risicobeheersing en proceskwaliteit

• Je traint, coacht en adviseert de eerste lijn over risicomanagement, wet- en regelgeving en intern beleid.

• Bewaken en versterken van het risk control framework binnen KYC-processen (lead-to-cash)

• Root cause analyses uitvoeren bij meldingen of incidenten

• Meedenken in creatieve oplossingen die wérken in de praktijk én passen binnen wet- en regelgeving

• Je draagt de key principles uit op tactisch en operationeel niveau en zorgt voor een sterk risicobewustzijn


Whats in it for you?

  • Moderne manier van werken, hybride, flexibel, wel zo fijn
  • Salaris tussen €5.533,- en €7.905,- obv 40 uur
  • 30 vakantiedagen
  • Vitaliteit staat hoog in het vaandel, korting op sportabonnement, fietsregeling en vitaliteitsprogramma


Check check dubbel check?

  • HBO/WO werk- en denkniveau
  • Sterk in stakeholdermanagement en procesverbetering, jij voelt je comfortabel binnen alle lagen van de organisatie
  • Ervaring met compliance, risicobeheersing, of audit in een financiële omgeving
  • Kennis van relevante wetgeving (o.a. Wwft en Wft,)
  • Strategisch inzicht én praktische werkmentaliteit
  • Affiniteit met organisaties in transitie, waar niet alles vastligt maar veel mogelijk in is.


Waarom CCV?

CCV is een internationale speler in digitale betaaloplossingen en financiële processen. Binnen de KYC-afdeling is volop ruimte om te bouwen aan structuur, samenwerking en kwaliteit en werk je samen om financiële criminaliteit te bestrijden. Je komt terecht in een stabiel Risk & Compliance-team dat versterking zoekt in de verbinding met de operatie. Hier werk je niet vóór, maar mét je interne klant.



Voor meer informatie kun je bellen via 06-11344485 of je vragen sturen naar eileen@bbadviesenwerving.nl.



Meteen solliciteren? Dat kan natuurlijk ook, ik neem zo snel mogelijk contact met je op.



Door te solliciteren ga je ermee akkoord dat wij jouw persoonsgegevens verwerken volgens de geldende privacywetgeving. Wij bewaren sollicitatiegegevens maximaal 4 weken na afronding van de procedure, tenzij je expliciet toestemming geeft voor een langere bewaar termijn.

companyDescription:
A global aerospace company headquartered in Brazil, Embraer has businesses in Commercial and Executive Aviation, Defense & Security, and Agricultural Aviation. The company designs, develops, manufactures and markets aircraft and systems, providing Services and Support to customer after-sales. 

Since it was founded in 1969, Embraer has delivered more than 8,000 aircraft. On average, about every 10 seconds an aircraft manufactured by Embraer takes off somewhere in the world, transporting over 145 million passengers a year.

descriptionHtml:
For our HR team in the EMEA Headquarters in Amsterdam, we are currently seeking to recruit a new colleague with knowledge of (international) payroll and compensation & benefits for the EMEA region. You will also play an important role with annual budgeting and providing management information.

HR ANALYST (COMPENSATION & BENEFITS)


TASKS & RESPONSIBILITIES

• Act as one of the specialists related to (international) payroll, compensation and benefits for the region;

• Regional or International Payroll: Monthly regional and international payroll activities in cooperation with our external payroll provider. Reviews for accuracy and Resolve discrepancies with payroll;

• Compensation: Salary surveys, Annual compensation administration, variable compensation administration, expatriate compensation etc.

• Benefits: Benefits administration, benefits plan administration (from regional HQ perspective), compliance, reporting and vendor management.

• Work with complex and sensitive data to provide analytical insights to compensation, benefits, taxation and social security matters in the region, through varying levels of data analysis;

• Provide administrative and analytical support on a variety of topics including regional population, market research, program design, analysis, budgeting or implementation of new programs;

• Closely partner, cooperate and manage the relationship with external parties as well as with counterparts at our Corporate HQ in Brazil;

• Develop, refine and execute ad-hoc reporting requests as they occur;

• Manage annual in- and external audits related to payroll, compensation and benefits by preparing documentation, responding to queries, and ensuring compliance with audit requirements.

• Develop, refine and execute ad-hoc reporting requests as business issues arise;

• Provide dedicated support with annual budgeting, comparing budget, actual expenses and adjusting forecasts for HR operations using SAP and SAC tools;

• Generate and manage HR-related payment requests using SAP;

• Process HR related invoices for payment using SAP;

• Respond to employee queries related to payroll and benefits with professionalism and accuracy.

• Work closely and in good partnership with the Finance team for monthly payroll closing, arranging payments, and resolving queries as they arise.

• Specific (broad) HR projects will be part of the job responsibility, either as the project lead or in a supportive/member role. This can vary throughout the year and as per individual’s preference, knowledge and interest.

• Help drive and/or lead HR process improvements with a Lean mindset.



CANDIDATE PROFILE

• Higher education (Bachelors+) in Human Resources, Finance, Business Administration or another closely related discipline is highly desired, but can be compensated by additional years of experience;

• Preferably a minimum of 3-5 years in a similar position managing payroll for different countries within the EMEA region; knowledge of subjects such as hypotax, hypo social security, permanent establishment, etc.

• Experience with working with SAP is a must, knowledge of SAP SuccessFactors is a plus;

• Knowledge of accounting and financial processes is a big plus;

• Demonstrated strong analytical competencies, excellent planning skills and the ability to execute with a sense of urgency;

• naturally curious and open minded, with good interpersonal skills who enjoys learning by doing;

• Experience working cross-functional teams at all levels within an international organisation;

• Experience working in a mutli cultural environment is a big plus, cultural awareness is a must;

• Excellent communication skills and the ability to ‘connect’ at all levels;

• A true team player who is looking for- and open to constructive (feedback) within their own and other teams;

• Flexibility and ability to prioritise as necessary in an ever changing environment;

• Strong attention to detail a must;

• Strong analytical and problem solving skills;

• Independent and proactive attitude;

• Fluency in English is mandatory;

• Accurate and precise, the ideal candidate continuously seeks to improve, streamline and optimise processes and reports;

• Proficient in MS Excel (pivot tables, VLOOKUPs), Word, Co pilot.

This document in no way states or implies that these are the only duties to be performed by the employee occupying this position.

companyDescription:
Vallei Audit: gedreven door ambitie! 

Bij Vallei Audit combineren we expertise en scherpe analyses met een persoonlijke aanpak. We staan naast je, betrokken en nuchter, voor betrouwbare auditdiensten zonder verrassingen.
Onze teams hebben diepgaande sectorkennis en begrijpen de unieke uitdagingen binnen jouw branche. Zo leveren we inzichten en zekerheid die jouw organisatie verder helpen.

We zijn lid van het internationale Praxity-netwerk. We beschikken daardoor over een groot internationaal netwerk van deskundigen en kunnen optimale ondersteuning geven in binnen- en buitenland.
descriptionHtml:
Ben jij een ervaren assistent audit die verder wil groeien in een informeel, ambitieus en groeiend team? Zoek niet verder!


Wat ga je doen?

Als senior assistent audit ben je zowel uitvoerend als aansturend onmisbaar! Enerzijds houd je je bezig met het controleren en beoordelen van de (complexe) jaarrekeningposten, signaleer jij risico’s en speel jij in op adviesmogelijkheden. Anderzijds ben jij de rechterhand van de controleleider. Aan verantwoordelijkheid geen gebrek, waar jij een rol speelt in de planning, groei en operationele aansturing van het team. Je bent een belangrijke schakel in de ontwikkeling van de (beginnend) assistent audit door met hen te sparren en de werkzaamheden te reviewen. Maar ook jouw ontwikkeling staat centraal, waarbij we met jou werken aan een plan om binnen de audit te groeien. Als onderdeel van een energiek en ambitieus team dagen wij jou uit om het meeste uit jouw ontwikkeling te halen!


Wat verwachten wij van jou?

  • Minimaal 3 jaar ervaring in de controlepraktijk
  • Een (bijna) afgeronde HBO accountancy of je bent bezig met je master
  • Geloof in samenwerken en aandacht voor je teamleden
  • Liefde voor het vak en gedreven om te leren
  • Een controle gebruiken om de klant beter te maken


Wat wij bieden

  • Bruto maandsalaris tussen de € 4.250 en € 5.400 (obv fulltime)
  • Jaarlijkse prestatiebonus
  • Werknemersparticipatie (bonus obv waardestijging van bedrijf)
  • Prima pensioenregeling
  • Auto van de zaak
  • 27 vakantiedagen
  • Persoonlijk ontwikkelplan
  • Fijne werkplek op kantoor en ruimte voor thuiswerken
  • Team- en bedrijfsactiviteiten (successen vieren we graag en vooral samen)
  • Energiek, gemotiveerd en ervaren team


Wie zijn wij?

Bij Vallei Audit moedigen we mensen aan om gedreven én kritisch te zijn. Gepassioneerd en flexibel werken we samen aan een gestructureerd controleproces en geven wij zekerheid aan onze klanten. De combinatie van onze persoonlijke betrokkenheid en uitdagende nationale en internationale klantenkring maakt ons toegankelijk en aantrekkelijk.


Vallei Audit is onderdeel van Vallei Finance Group, een fullservice en snelgroeiende organisatie met een internationaal netwerk en vestigingen in Amsterdam, Aalsmeer, Barendrecht, Barneveld, Geldermalsen, Nieuwegein, Sliedrecht en Woudenberg. Met ruim 300 collega’s en een positie in de top 50 accountskantoren van Nederland willen wij elke dag met maximaal plezier het maximale resultaat behalen. Voor onze collega’s én voor onze klanten.


Jij en Vallei?

Ben jij enthousiast geworden en heb je interesse? Dan maken we graag kennis met jou. Reageer via de sollicitatiebutton of mail naar jijenvallei@vaacc.nl en binnen één werkdag nemen we contact met je op. Heb je eerst nog vragen of twijfel je ergens over? Neem dan contact op met Clemens van der Werf via 06-21979204.

companyDescription: We provide one-stop business solutions by integrating different modes of transport throughout the world.
companySlogan: We provide one-stop business solutions by integrating different modes of transport throughout the world.
descriptionHtml:
The Nippon Express Group is one of the world's leading logistics service providers, with headquarters in Tokyo and a global presence spanning over 50 countries and regions. With more than 73,000 professionals worldwide, the NX Group is renowned for its commitment to excellence and innovation in logistics, including tailored solutions for the healthcare and pharmaceutical industries.

To strengthen our Global Pharma Quality Assurance team, we are currently seeking a motivated and detail-oriented Pharma Quality Manager to join our international team. In this pivotal role, you will contribute to the implementation and maintenance of our global quality management system, ensuring the highest standards of compliance across our organization. You will operate in a multicultural environment and play a vital role in fostering a culture of continuous improvement and global quality excellence.

These are your key tasks:

  • Support the implementation and ongoing improvement of the Global Pharma Quality Management System across NX entities
  • Coordinate and manage the internal audit program, including planning, execution, reporting, and follow-up on corrective actions
  • Contribute to the creation and implementation of global quality training programs
  • Develop, implement and review global quality processes
  • Prepare and review global quality documents (e.g., SOPs)
  • Oversee the rollout and adoption of global quality processes across regional and local operations.
  • Collaborate on the execution of the Global Pharma Quality Plan and other strategic initiatives
  • Act as a quality ambassador, promoting a culture of compliance and continuous improvement throughout the organization


Requirements

  • Bachelor's or Master's degree in Life Sciences, Pharmacy, Quality Management, or a related field
  • Minimum of two to five years of relevant professional experience in the freight forwarding or logistics industry, preferably in a quality management role
  • Proven expertise in the implementation and maintenance of QMS
  • Experience in conducting internal and external audits, including planning, execution, and reporting
  • Demonstrated ability to plan and implement CAPA measures as well as continuous improvement measures
  • Familiarity with Computer System Validation is an asset
  • Prior experience within the pharmaceutical and/or healthcare logistics sector is highly advantageous
  • Strong working knowledge of international standards and regulatory guidelines such as ISO 9001, GDP, ISO 13485, and FDA 21 CFR Part 820
  • Solid understanding of regulatory requirements within the pharmaceutical industry, specifically in relation to freight forwarding and the supply chain of healthcare products
  • Sound knowledge of pharmaceutical supply chain processes
  • Highly organized, with excellent time management and multitasking abilities
  • Experience in planning and managing small to medium-scale projects independently
  • Proficiency in Microsoft Office applications, particularly Word and Excel
  • Excellent communication skills, both verbal and written, in English


Benefits

  • A manager level position within a leading and innovative logistics company
  • Engaging and varied responsibilities in a dynamic, international environment
  • Supportive and collaborative team culture with a structured onboarding process
  • A healthy work-life balance, including the option to work from home (hybrid model)
  • A competitive benefits package and much more

companyDescription:
KPMG Nederland levert hoogwaardige dienstverlening op het gebied van audit, tax en advisory. We werken voor een brede groep opdrachtgevers: grote (inter)nationale ondernemingen, middelgrote bedrijven, non-profitorganisaties en overheden. 
De ingewikkelde problematiek van onze cliënten vraagt om een multidisciplinaire aanpak die helpt orde te scheppen in de complexiteit. Onze professionals blinken uit in hun eigen specialisme, maar werken tegelijkertijd nauw samen om zo de toegevoegde waarde te bieden die onze cliënten helpt om te excelleren. Daarbij putten we uit een rijke bron van kennis en ervaring, opgedaan in uiteenlopende organisaties en markten.
companySlogan: Wij vertalen inzichten naar kansen & concrete actie, voor duurzaam vertrouwen en succes. 💙🌍✨ Audit & Advisory
descriptionHtml:
Als starter binnen Audit bij KPMG werk je aan financiële controles bij uiteenlopende klanten. Geen dag is hetzelfde: je komt wekelijks bij nieuwe organisaties over de vloer, werkt samen met verschillende teams en krijgt snel inzicht in hoe bedrijven écht functioneren. De veelzijdigheid van het werk, de dynamiek van het veld en de continue samenwerking maken Audit dé plek voor wie houdt van afwisseling en leren in de praktijk. Vanuit onze centraal gelegen kantoren in Rotterdam en Den Haag werk je in een professionele én gezellige omgeving, met volop ruimte voor ontwikkeling. Sta jij aan het begin van je Audit carrière? Zet vandaag de eerste stap!

Jouw uitdagingen

Let op: het eerst volgende startmoment is 1 september 2026.

  • Je voert gesprekken met klanten om inzicht te krijgen in hun processen.
  • Je stelt procesbeschrijvingen op en brengt daarbij de bijbehorende risico’s in kaart.
  • Je analyseert externe factoren die risico’s kunnen vormen voor de klantorganisatie.
  • Je past Data & Analytics toe om de jaarrekeningcontrole slimmer en efficiënter uit te voeren.
  • Je voert lijncontroles en steekproeven uit en beoordeelt de effectiviteit van de interne beheersingsmaatregelen.


Dit neem jij mee

  • Je hebt een hbo- of wo-opleiding afgerond in Accountancy, Bedrijfseconomie of Bedrijfskunde.
  • Je hebt de ambitie om je te ontwikkelen tot registeraccountant (RA).
  • Je spreekt en schrijft vloeiend Nederlands (C2) én Engels, en weet je taalvaardigheid effectief in te zetten in een professionele omgeving.
  • Je werkt graag samen in een team en hebt een klantgerichte instelling.


Wat ga je doen?

Bij KPMG start je je carrière met veel afwisseling en uitdaging. De ene dag ben je intensief bezig met de voorbereiding van klantopdrachten, terwijl je de volgende dag juist focust op de uitvoering en afronding ervan. Je richt je op het analyseren van de financiële gezondheid van organisaties en weet jouw bevindingen helder en overtuigend over te brengen. Zo ontwikkel je snel een brede blik én sterke communicatieve vaardigheden. Daarnaast leer je snel schakelen, werk je met verschillende teams en ontmoet wekelijks nieuwe klanten—een dynamiek die veel starters als enorm leerzaam en motiverend ervaren.

“Als Associate bij KPMG vond ik het erg leerzaam! Het was geweldig om met verschillende klanten te werken en van hen te leren. Mijn collega's waren heel vriendelijk en behulpzaam, en de sfeer op het werk was echt top. Voor zowel privé als werk gerelateerde vragen kan ik altijd terecht bij mijn collega’s. Ik kreeg vanaf dag 1 veel ondersteuning van mijn begeleiders, wat mij echt hielp om te groeien en me thuis te voelen in de organisatie.”

— Bram Gad, Senior Associate

Werken in het hart van de stad

Onze kantoren in Rotterdam en Den Haag liggen op toplocaties, waardoor je na werktijd direct kunt genieten van alles wat de stad te bieden heeft.

Jouw ontwikkeling staat centraal

Met het Audit Foundations programma krijg je toegang tot trainingen die je ondersteunen in de praktijk. Ervaren collega’s staan klaar om je te begeleiden en je te helpen groeien, zowel professioneel als persoonlijk.

Werkplezier hoort erbij

Naast uitdagend werk is er volop ruimte voor ontspanning. Denk aan borrels, teamuitjes en andere activiteiten die zorgen voor een goede balans tussen werk en plezier.

Klaar om jouw audit carrière te starten in een omgeving vol kansen en energie? Grijp dan deze kans en wie weet ben jij binnenkort onderdeel van ons Zuid-Hollandse Auditteam!

Dit bieden wij

  • Brutosalaris tussen € 3.060,- en € 3.265,-, winstuitkering, een vaste onkostenvergoeding en een thuiswerkvergoeding van € 2,40 per gewerkte dag.
  • Een flexibel pensioen zonder verplichte eigen bijdrage.
  • 30 vakantiedagen (op fulltime basis) en de mogelijkheid om meer dagen te kopen of je vakantiedagen te verkopen.
  • Bij KPMG werken we hybride, dus een combinatie van zowel thuis als op kantoor.
  • Een volledig ingerichte thuiswerkplek.
  • Een leaseauto + NS business kaart of een mobiliteitsregeling.
  • Een laptop en iPhone die je ook privé mag gebruiken.
  • Een uitgebreid aanbod van trainingen en opleidingen die bijdragen aan jouw professionele en persoonlijke ontwikkeling.
  • Diversiteitsnetwerken op het gebied van pride, gender, ability, cultural diversity en generations die regelmatig verschillende activiteiten organiseren om verschil te vieren!
  • Aandacht voor vitaliteit! Sporten op het kantoor in Amstelveen of korting op een sportschool bij jou in de buurt én toegang tot coaching, gezondheids- en vitaliteitprogramma’s.
  • ‘Together’ is één van onze kernwaarden. Je kunt dus rekenen op verschillende sociale activiteiten zoals teamuitjes, borrels en evenementen samen met je collega’s.

companyDescription: As the leading environmental solutions partner for organizations globally, ACT helps its partners meet increasing environmental action and disclosure expectations from regulators, customers, investors, suppliers, and other stakeholders with transparency and confidence. ACT’s expert-backed, end-to-end decarbonization and environmental impact solutions empower organizations to efficiently measure, reduce, mitigate, monitor, and disclose their carbon footprint to achieve their goals. ACT also offers comprehensive solutions for organizations to meet complex environmental regulations set by authorities at regional, national, and international levels on a global scale.
companySlogan: Empowering organizations globally to achieve their environmental goals—no matter how ambitious
descriptionHtml:
Your Role In Our Story

In essence, your role involves

  • Strategic Leadership: Execute pan-European and UK strategy in sustainable finance, aligning with company vision and growth goals.
  • Regulatory Compliance: Ensure full adherence to AFM, FCA, and EU regulations; maintain strong regulator relationships and meet governance standards.
  • Commercial Growth: Identify new markets and opportunities, develop innovative sustainable financial products, and grow revenue and market share.
  • Sales Leadership: Set and drive sales targets, lead a customer-centric sales culture, and implement sales excellence programs across Europe and the UK.
  • Operational Oversight: Manage daily operations with full P&L responsibility, ensuring cross-functional alignment and regulatory compliance.
  • Team Leadership: Lead and develop a 30-person team, including sales, trading, and support, fostering accountability, diversity, and professional growth.
  • Stakeholder Engagement & Innovation: Represent the company with clients, investors, and industry bodies, while driving innovation and staying ahead of market trends.

Your Expertise

To be successful in this role, we are looking for candidates with the following qualifications and attributes:

  • Educational & Industry Background: Master’s in Finance, Economics, or Business; 15+ years in financial services, including senior leadership in regulated environments.
  • Executive Leadership: Proven track record as MD or divisional head with board-level exposure, demonstrating strong governance, ethics, and accountability.
  • Commercial & Sales Acumen: Skilled in driving growth, leading sales teams, and executing commercial strategies to deliver measurable results.
  • Operational & Regulatory Expertise: In-depth experience across compliance, risk, finance, audit, and HR; meets Dutch AFM suitability standards.
  • Sustainable Finance Focus: Strong affinity for ESG-related financial products (e.g., carbon credits, ESG-linked instruments); prior exposure is a plus.
  • Regulatory & Market Knowledge: Deep understanding of EU/UK regulations with direct experience working with regulators (AFM, DNB, FCA).
  • Core Leadership Qualities: Strategic, ethical, resilient, and people-focused with strong communication, mentoring, and stakeholder management skills.
  • Cultural & International Fit: Fluent in English (Dutch/other EU languages a plus); effective in diverse, international teams with a passion for sustainability.

title: Managing Director Financial Solutions

companyDescription:
As BYD’s first overseas subsidiary, BYD Europe B.V., now located in Scorpius 112, 2132 LR Hoofddorp, the Netherlands, was founded in 1998. 

BYD Europe mainly focuses on New Energy products, including Electric Vehicles, especially passenger cars, buses, trucks and forklifts; Rechargeable batteries, Solar Panel, Energy Storage and Rail Transit.

Visit BYD Europe Auto brand new website: https://www.bydauto.eu/
descriptionHtml:
About BYD

Our Purpose is to build a zero-emission future that reconnects humanity with nature. We are looking for talent that connects with this mission and want to create positive impact by joining a diverse and dynamic team.

BYD is a leading, high-tech multinational, based in Shenzhen, China. Operates in four core fields of IT, automotive, new energy, and rail transit and employs over 300,000 people worldwide. As a global pioneer in New Energy Solutions BYD is dedicated to building a zero-emission ecosystem.

BYD Europe BV located in Schiedam, Netherlands is the marketing and sales HQ of BYD Co., Ltd based in Shenzhen, China. BYD Europe BV offers a full line of BYD electric vehicles, including all-electric cars, all-electric buses, and all-electric forklift trucks.

Job Description

Key Responsibilities

  • Adhere to the company's financial policies and procedures.
  • Collect and process documentation required for internal and external audits.
  • Correspond with and assist the external auditors and tax advisors, collect and present audit data.
  • Perform checks on financial data to ensure compliance with internal policies and accounting standards.
  • Correct bookkeeping errors related to accounts receivable (AR) and accounts payable (AP).
  • Assist in the production of financial statements and applications, preparation of spreadsheets, reports, and correspondence as required.
  • Assist with end of month and year closure preparation.
  • Answer questions and provide assistance to stakeholders and colleagues, as needed;
  • Help to develop and test new finance products, procedures and tools;
  • Suggest changes or improvements to increase accuracy, efficiency, and cost reductions.
  • Support Finance department team members with other tasks.

Requirements:

  • MBO or HBO education in Finance or Accounting.
  • 3+ years of working experience in a similar role.
  • Advanced spoken and written English language. Other languages are bonus.
  • Advanced MS Excel skills (creating spreadsheets and using financial functions);
  • SAP user experience will be an asset.
  • Excellent communication, collaboration and problem-solving skills.
  • Detail oriented and good with analyzing large data.

Location: Hoofddorp, The Netherlands.

Type of Employment: Full-time

companyDescription: Ouwersloot Kerkhoven adviseurs | accountants is an accounting and consultancy firm with offices in Schagen, Amsterdam and Oostzaan.
descriptionHtml:
Ouwersloot Kerkhoven adviseurs - accountants zoekt enthousiaste nieuwe collega’s voor de audit! En niet zomaar collega’s, we zoeken superhelden die cijfers kunnen temmen voor onze dynamische, professionele en enthousiaste kantoren in Amsterdam, Oostzaan en Schagen. Wij richten ons op middelgrote vennootschappen, met een bepaalde adviesbehoefte en een gezonde ambitie tot groei. 


Leuke extra's voor deze functie:

  • Mini / BMW: word lid van ons team en rij in te gekke stijl!
  • Balans: balans werk privé zeer goed te doen
  • #Team: word onderdeel van ons super gezellige en behulpzame team!
  • Ontwikkeling: Bij ons staat de ontwikkeling van onze medewerkers centraal. Wij ondersteunen hen hierin dan ook graag!


Wij zijn op zoek naar Audit medewerkers full time (40 uur) of part time (32 uur)


We zoeken (assistent) accountants met liefde voor het werk. Iemand die ‘s ochtends uit bed springt omdat hij of zij enthousiast is om met collega’s en klanten aan de slag te gaan. Bij ons heb je dankzij de platte organisatie en wisselende teams genoeg mogelijkheden om jezelf inhoudelijk te ontwikkelen. We zijn dagelijks bezig met serieuze zaken en intelligente oplossingen voor complexe uitdagingen, maar op onze kantoren heerst een informele en prettige sfeer waarin jouw mening gehoord wordt. Humor speelt hierbij een belangrijke rol. We zijn vastbesloten om professionaliteit en plezier te combineren en dit ook middels onze medewerkers naar onze klanten uit te stralen. Klinkt dit als een winstgevend lied in je oren en vind je de balans? Lees dan snel verder!


Onze collega is een enthousiaste teamspeler met een proactieve benadering van zijn of haar werk. Deze rol vereist vaardigheid in het oplossen van complexe financiële vraagstukken en de bereidheid om te blijven leren en verder te ontwikkelen. Uiteraard beschik je over een relevant HBO of WO diploma en heb je aantoonbare financiële - administratieve én eventueel fiscale kennis. Wij bieden jou een fantastische cliëntenportefeuille, met veel afwisselende werkzaamheden. In onze dynamische en professionele sfeer vind je continu uitdaging! 


Zin om cijfers te laten swingen? Voor meer informatie over deze functie, kun je contact opnemen met het secretariaat in Schagen, tel.: 0224 – 274 500. Wij ontvangen jouw sollicitatiebrief en CV graag per mail: info@okadviseurs.nl.

Wij richten ons op middelgrote vennootschappen, met een bepaalde adviesbehoefte en een gezonde ambitie tot groei. Leuke extra's voor deze functie:Mini / BMW: word lid van ons team en rij in te gekke stijl!Balans: balans werk privé zeer goed te doen#Team:  word onderdeel van ons super gezellige en behulpzame team!Ontwikkeling: Bij ons staat de ontwikkeling van onze medewerkers centraal. Wij ondersteunen hen hierin dan ook graag!Wij zijn op zoek naar Audit medewerkers full time (40 uur) of part time (32 uur)We zoeken (assistent) accountants met liefde voor het werk. Iemand die ‘s ochtends uit bed springt omdat hij of zij enthousiast is om met collega’s en klanten aan de slag te gaan. Bij ons heb je dankzij de platte organisatie en wisselende teams genoeg mogelijkheden om jezelf inhoudelijk te ontwikkelen. We zijn dagelijks bezig met serieuze zaken en intelligente oplossingen voor complexe uitdagingen, maar op onze kantoren heerst een informele en prettige sfeer waarin jouw mening gehoord wordt. Humor speelt hierbij een belangrijke rol. We zijn vastbesloten om professionaliteit en plezier te combineren en dit ook middels onze medewerkers naar onze klanten uit te stralen. Klinkt dit als een winstgevend lied in je oren en vind je de balans? Lees dan snel verder!Onze collega is een enthousiaste teamspeler met een proactieve benadering van zijn of haar werk. Deze rol vereist vaardigheid in het oplossen van complexe financiële vraagstukken en de bereidheid om te blijven leren en verder te ontwikkelen. Uiteraard beschik je over een relevant HBO of WO diploma en heb je aantoonbare financiële - administratieve én eventueel fiscale kennis. Wij bieden jou een fantastische cliëntenportefeuille, met veel afwisselende werkzaamheden. In onze dynamische en professionele sfeer vind je continu uitdaging! Zin om cijfers te laten swingen? Voor meer informatie over deze functie, kun je contact opnemen met het secretariaat in Schagen, tel.: 0224 – 274 500. Wij ontvangen jouw sollicitatiebrief en CV graag per mail: info@okadviseurs.nl.

companyDescription: IAC - Search was formerly known as CGS Search & Selection but has rebranded after the business was fully integrated into the Internal Audit Connections (IAC) Group in May 2015. The group continues to provides permanent and interim staffing solutions for Executive and Middle Management roles within the Internal Audit, Enterprise Risk Management, Compliance and Internal Control disciplines. IAC - Search offers an integrated search, advertised selection and database approach tailored specifically to the requirements of an individual client. IAC - Search operates across the industrial, commercial and financial services sectors, and the majority of our clients are blue-chip or mid-sized multinationals based in the UK, Continental Europe or the Middle East.
companySlogan: IAC Search is the Executive Search Division of the IAC Group.
descriptionHtml:
A Bold, Game-Changing Opportunity in Risk Assurance

The risk assurance market is undergoing a seismic transformation. With Private Equity investment reshaping the landscape and disrupting long-standing incumbents, our client is at the forefront of this change—driving innovation, scale, and efficiency in a historically static market.


This is not a typical Risk Assurance Partner role. This is a growth-led Partner role where market expansion leads the agenda, supported by delivery excellence and strong leadership. It offers a transparent, performance-linked equity pathway with multi-million-pound exit potential at five years, tied to clearly defined business-development milestones. In practical terms, the equity you build will mirror sustained success in originating opportunities, winning pursuits, and expanding key accounts—underpinned by high-quality delivery and effective team leadership.


Why This Role Stands Apart

Our client is a rapidly scaling, privately owned AI-enabled firm leveraging the infrastructure, brand, and expertise of a 450-strong Big 4 trained specialist team based in India. Our client will disrupt the market dominated by the large professional services firms with a business model that offers clients a significant cost advantage while maintaining premium delivery quality.


As a Partner, you will not only lead but own the growth of new geographical markets, with equity-linked rewards directly tied to your individual business development success. This is a unique chance to create and monetise real enterprise value while playing a leading role in the reinvention of risk assurance consulting.


Core of the Role

This role is BD first, always. Value accrues from BD-led milestones (revenue, margin quality, retention, regional EBIT). No BD → no equity value. Everything else supports that mission.

  • Focus on origination and growth: pipeline build, pursuits, proposals, pricing, and account expansion.
  • Market Disruption – Actively challenge incumbent firms by delivering a differentiated, cost-efficient service proposition underpinned by deep technical capability.

Your Responsibilities

  • Client Delivery Oversight – Manage and quality-assure complex internal audit, programme assurance, and risk transformation engagements.
  • Advisory Influence & Local Regional Support – Present to Audit Committees, advise Boards and C-suites on risk strategy, control frameworks, emerging risks, and assurance of major programmes.
  • Brand Building & GTM Strategy – Develop regional thought leadership through events, public speaking, and a visible social media presence.
  • Team Collaboration – Partner closely with the offshore delivery centre (450+ professionals) to scale high-impact consulting solutions efficiently and effectively.


What We're Looking For

  • A current Director or Partner with a proven track record in internal audit and risk consulting.
  • Deep client relationships and market credibility in your region, with a hunger to build a multimillion-pound/euro portfolio.
  • Strategic leadership experience in risk assurance across large, complex organisations.
  • Trusted boardroom advisor capable of influencing Audit Committees, CFOs, and senior executives.
  • Strong commercial acumen with a clear drive for equity value creation.
  • Expertise in delivering risk transformation, internal audit outsourcing, and assurance on major programmes.


This Is for You If...

  • You want to build, not just operate—this is about creating a business within a business.
  • You’re ready to step out of a traditional firm and join a fast-moving, entrepreneurial environment.
  • You want to tie your personal financial success directly to the value you create—with a multimillion equity exit strategy built into the journey.


Next Steps

We are currently hiring Partners in the UK, Western Europe, and the Middle East. If you are an ambitious and commercially driven senior leader ready to take on this bold, rewarding challenge—we want to hear from you.

companyDescription:
Welkom op de bedrijfspagina van Brown & McCrow! Wij zijn onderdeel van Maison Brown en het enige recruitment consultancy dat enkel werft voor Accountantskantoren, Administratiekantoren en Belastingadvieskantoren. Hierdoor zijn wij het meest gespecialiseerde kantoor van heel Nederland in deze tak.

Bij ons zijn de consultants buiten deze niche specialisatie ook nog eens gespecialiseerd in een bepaalde regio. Dit betekent dat wanneer u als kandidaat of werkgever geholpen word door een specialist in uw regio. Dit alles resulteert in een snelle, persoonlijke en kwalitatief hoogwaardige service. 

Wilt u meer informatie over Brown & McCrow en onze werkwijze?
Neem dan vandaag nog contact met ons op!
descriptionHtml:
Ben jij op zoek naar een nieuwe uitdaging als assistent accountant (32uur of 40uur) voor in de controlepraktijk bij een accountantskantoor zich richt op middelgrote vennootschappen?


Dit accountantskantoor met meerdere vestigingen in het land is momenteel op zoek naar startende assistent accountant met geen of 1 tot 2 jaar ervaring voor op de controlepraktijk. Dankzij hun platte organisatie en wisselende teams heb je bij hun genoeg mogelijkheden om jezelf inhoudelijk te ontwikkelen. Ze zijn dagelijks bezig met serieuze zaken en intelligente oplossingen voor complexe uitdagingen, maar op hun kantoren heerst een informele en prettige sfeer waarin humor een belangrijke rol speelt.


Gezien hun diverse klant portfolio van middelgrote vennootschappen, werk je in een prettige omgeving waar je veel kan leren. Hiernaast lopen er meerdere RA en AA accountants rond, waar jij alles aan kan vragen zodat je veel kan leren en kan doorgroeien.


  • jouw rol in het kort:
  • Vanaf het begin wordt je betrokken bij klantbezoeken.
  • Je voert controle- en assurance-werkzaamheden uit.
  • Documenteert bevindingen en conclusies volgens richtlijnen en kwaliteitsnormen.
  • Je signaleert mogelijkheden voor advies.


  • Wat wij zoeken:
  • We zoeken iemand die houdt van inhoudelijke uitdagingen en ambitie heeft om door te groeien.
  • Je vindt een goede balans tussen eigen verantwoordelijkheid en samenwerking in een team, met ondersteuning van ervaren collega’s.
  • Je hebt een hbo/wo-diploma in accountancy of bent hiermee bezig.
  • Kritisch denkvermogen en nauwkeurigheid zijn voor ons essentieel.


  • Wat zij jou bieden:
  • Een salaris tussen de €2700 en €3300, afhankelijk van je ervaring.
  • Volledige vergoeding van studiekosten, zodat je je continu kunt ontwikkelen.
  • Bij een fulltime dienstverband heb je recht op 28 vakantiedagen + vakantiegeld, en een extra beloning van 2 vakantiedagen als je gedurende het jaar niet ziek bent geweest.
  • Een moderne laptop wordt verstrekt voor flexibiliteit en efficiëntie in je werk.
  • Zowel kilometervergoeding als een vaste vergoeding voor openbaar vervoer voor je woon-werkverkeer.
  • Een tijd-voor-tijd regeling voor een eerlijke compensatie van overuren, voor een gezonde werk-privébalans.
  • Een goede pensioenregeling voor een zekere financiële toekomst.
  • Een moderne werkomgeving met sta-zit bureaus en de nieuwste technologie voor meer productiviteit en welzijn.
  • Wekelijkse gezamenlijke lunch op verschillende locaties voor een beter teamgevoel en informele interactie tussen collega’s.
  • Verschillende uitjes en evenementen door het jaar heen voor een positieve werksfeer en sterke teamgeest.


Spreekt dit jou aan? Solliciteer dan snel, want de werving is al volop bezig! Mocht je vragen hebben bel dan naar +31 8500 490 15 (tussen 8:00 en 19:00) of neem een kijkje op de website van Brown & McCrow.

Deze vacature wordt beheerd door Rodi en Madelief van Brown & McCrow. Dit is overigens het enige recruitment consultancy van Nederland dat enkel bemiddeld in de Accountancy branche. Jij wordt dus altijd geholpen door een specialist!

(Jouw sollicitatie is volledig confidentieel. Wij informeren niemand over jouw kandidatuur zonder jouw toestemming.)

companyDescription:
Randstad is the world’s largest talent company and a partner of choice to clients. We are committed to providing equitable opportunities to people from all backgrounds and help them remain relevant in the rapidly changing world of work. We have a deep understanding of the labor market and help our clients to create the high-quality, diverse and agile workforces they need to succeed. Our 46,000 employees around the world make a positive impact on society by helping people to realize their true potential throughout their working life. 
Randstad was founded in 1960 and is headquartered in Diemen, the Netherlands. In 2022, in our 39 markets, we helped more than 2 million people find a job that feels good and advised over 230,000 clients on their talent needs. We generated revenue of €27.6 billion. Randstad N.V. is listed on the Euronext Amsterdam. For more information, see www.randstad.com
descriptionHtml:
We are looking for an IT Risk & Audit Expert at Randstad Global based in Diemen - Netherlands, hybrid, fulltime


people at the heart of everything we do

Working at Randstad is unlike working at any organization. Because at Randstad we put people at the heart of everything we do. This goes for our clients, our talent, our employees and society. We combine our passion for people with the power of today’s technologies. This helps us support people and organizations in realizing their true potential. We call this partner for talent.

Learn more about our mission, history and values on our website: www.randstad.com


BR&AF

The business risk and audit function (BR&AF) provides assurance and recommendations to the Executive Board and the Audit Committee on Randstad Group’s (risk) management, control and governance practices. Therefore the department facilitates in setting governance standards. It provides advice on internal control improvements, it conducts audits, special investigations and assignments worldwide on request of the Executive Board and Audit Committee. BR&AF’s responsibility includes the quarterly reporting based on: the risk and internal control self assessments, audit results, discussions on internal control weaknesses and analysis of the key risks reported throughout the company's management structure. This also substantiates the Group’s in-control statements.


The global function consists of team members operating through hubs spread over 4 continents. The team is organized in a matrix consisting of control & audit focus teams on business and functional areas (e.g. digital, talent, client, back office & shared service centers and fraud & forensics) and partners that are the linking pin to operating units (business risk & audit partners) with a global view. A support group is set up for professional practice, learning & development, quality improvement & methods, analytics and PMO.


Representatives of the different groups (Focus teams, BR&A Partners and support group) form the BR&AF core management team that reports to the Managing Director BR&AF.


how you will contribute

As an independent and objective risk & audit expert, you are going to:

  • perform and assist internal audit projects and internal control evaluation related to 'digital' processes, such as IT processes, program & project management, 3rd party assurance, security, business IT controls, CIM and BCP.
  • cooperate with business/process leaders and support senior management to advise on risk assessments, audit planning and scoping.
  • travel. As the standard office locations for the BR&AF team are organized in hubs, like in the country mentioned, travelling is estimated to be about 20% to 30% of the job.


what you will be doing

  • participate in risk assessments and assist in audit planning and scoping, such as: IT general controls, automated controls, RPA and IT projects.
  • perform IT controls/process audits on scope elements.
  • for technical domains able to lead and execute audits including: physical security, ethical hacking and technical validation of controls on multiple OSI layers.
  • support combined comfort analyses on maturity of IT general controls relevant domains (combining audit insights with other sources of data such as internal control projects, management controls self-assessment and developments within the company)
  • participate and lead audit interviews, accountable to prepare audit workpapers and reporting, as well as assist in drafting audit findings and recommendations for management based on identified control weaknesses or process improvements.
  • support and monitor the follow-up implementation of corrective actions by management.
  • act as a resource for colleagues with less expertise.


who will you work with

  • Partners/communities within the IT operations and projects space
  • IT security, data protection, information security and business continuity communities within the Randstad organization.
  • Business risk & audit partners
  • IT & digital leadership
  • External co-source partners
  • BR & AF stakeholders/clients within the business including local MT's, process and control owners


what you will bring

  • minimum 8 years of relevant experience
  • minimum master degree in business or economics, and related accreditation (e.g. CISA/CISM/CISSP)
  • level BEC B1/B2 or higher, preferably combined with proficiency in one of the following languages: Dutch, Spanish, French, Italian and /or German


your competencies

Within Randstad we defined the following Leadership competencies:

  • delighting people: connect people (client, talent, employee, society), building strong client relationships and delivering client-centric solutions.
  • performing today: resourcefulness securing and developing resources effectively and efficiently.
  • leading change: manages ambiguity operating effectively, even when things are not certain or the way forward is not clear.
  • securing the future: drives vision and purpose painting a compelling picture of the vision and strategy that motivates others into action.
  • creating clarity - showing line of sight: making sense of complex, high quantity and sometimes contradictory information to effectively solve problems and provides direction, is delegating and removing obstacles to get work done
  • courage to challenge: shows courage and confidence to speak up skillfully, challenging others even when they are confronted with resistance or unfamiliar circumstances
  • collaborates, reaches out, you excel in organizational sensitivity: maneuvering comfortably through complex policy, process and people-related organizational dynamics recognizing the value that different perspectives and cultures bring to an organization


what’s in it for you

We put people at the heart of everything we do. Our employment conditions reflect this and are in accordance with the applicable (local) Randstad employment policies and related personnel guide.


We want our teams and talent to reflect the rich diversity of the societies we serve. We thrive for an environment of belonging, safety and confidence. So everyone can bring their whole selves to work and flourish. Learn more about equity, diversity, inclusion and belonging at randstad here.


If you recognize yourself in the profile above, we invite you to apply for this role. For more information you can reach out to our recruitment business partner: jennifer.roberts@randstadsourceright.co.uk

We provide dynamic contract, temporary, and permanent placement solutions across finance, accounting, technology, marketing, creative, legal, and administrative sectors.

companySlogan: Helping you find skilled candidates, in-demand jobs, and solutions you need to do your best work yet.
descriptionHtml:
📌Green Energy Sector

🏢Schiphol

🧭Hybrid (2 days from home)


Do you possess strong bookkeeping experience and a foundational understanding of financial controls? We are seeking a (Jr.) Financial Controller to join our team and play a vital role in ensuring the accuracy and integrity of our financial operations.


Responsibilities:

  • Financial Reporting: Participate in the preparation of accurate and timely financial reports, ensuring compliance with relevant accounting standards.
  • Bookkeeping: Maintain accurate and up-to-date financial records, including general ledger entries, accounts payable, and accounts receivable.
  • Financial Control: Implement and monitor internal controls to safeguard company assets and ensure the integrity of financial data.
  • Invoice Management: Prepare, process, and track invoices accurately while maintaining records for auditing purposes.
  • Payment Processing: Handle payments to vendors, ensure timely payment schedules, and reconcile payment records.
  • Expense Tracking: Record and monitor expenses while verifying compliance with company policies and budget guidelines.
  • Bank Reconciliation: Regularly reconcile bank statements with the company's financial records to ensure accuracy.
  • Support Audit Processes: Facilitate internal and external audits by providing necessary documentation and explanations.
  • Budget Monitoring Assistance: Collaborate with the Senior Business Controller to track budget performance, analyze variances, and prepare relevant reports.
  • Ad hoc Financial Analysis & Tasks: Support special projects, cost analysis, or other financial initiatives as directed by the Senior Business Controller.


Requirements:

  • Proficiency in English (written and verbal communication), Dutch is preferred.
  • Strong attention to detail and ability to manage multiple tasks simultaneously.
  • Strong knowledge of accounting principles and practices.
  • Experience with handling invoices, payments, and expenses.
  • Competent in Microsoft Office (Excel, Word, Outlook) and accounting software.
  • Excellent interpersonal and organizational skills.
  • Previous experience in a similar role is preferred.


What We Offer:

  • Up to 75K gross per year inclusive holiday pay, depending on background and experience
  • Yearly bonus and benefits package
  • A collaborative and dynamic work environment with room for growth.
  • Opportunities to work closely with senior management and develop valuable skills.
  • A chance to contribute to a thriving organization where your work makes a real impact.

companyDescription:
DISCOVER AMSTERDAM'S BEST KEPT SECRET

Tucked behind a historical façade right along the canals of the city’s charming center, The Dylan Amsterdam, Member of The Leading Hotels of the World, provides an exclusive gateway to uncovering the hidden treasures of Amsterdam’s past and present. Comprised of the Keizersgracht canal houses with a manicured courtyard nestled at its heart, the Dylan Hotel Amsterdam is continually recognized for its contemporary meets classic design, dedicated service and as a beloved location for those who appreciate quality.
companyName: The Dylan Amsterdam, Member of The Leading Hotels of the World
companySlogan:
Amsterdam's best kept secret!

Enchants, indulges and delights each guest that enters through its historical gate.
descriptionHtml:
Do you love to work at night and do you have a great sense for responsibility? Then we are looking for you!Located at the Keizersgracht, in the heart of the Nine Streets neighborhood is Amsterdam’s best kept secret: The Dylan Amsterdam. A 5-star luxury boutique hotel where we offer sincere and personal service and create memorable experiences for our guests. A historical environment with 41 unique hotel rooms, 4 private rooms for meetings & events and three restaurants: Bar Brasserie OCCO, Two Michelin star awarded Restaurant Vinkeles, and the smallest fine-dining restaurant in The Netherlands, até.The Dylan Amsterdam is looking for a full-time Night Auditor. In this position, you are responsible for the night audit activities and front office duties during the night. You are the contact person for our guests during the night and you are responsible for their safety. Besides, on request you prepare and/or serve orders for guests. Your day starts at 23.00 hrs. and ends at 07.00 hrs. Who are you?

  • You have passion for the hospitality industry.
  • You can deal with unexpected situations.
  • You have good organizational skills.
  • You work accurately.
  • You are creative and stress resistant.
  • You are quality oriented.
  • You are fluent in Dutch and English, both in speaking and in writing.

What can we offer you?

  • Travel allowance starting from 5 kilometres.
  • Upon commencement of employment, a Swap bike will be available to you. This will be fully reimbursed for one year
  • Workwear will be completely provided and cleaned by the hotel.
  • Shoe allowance of up to 100 euros gross per year based on full-time employment.
  • Delicious freshly prepared staff meals
  • Discounts on insurance and gyms.
  • Health and well-being
  • Access to workshops and personal intakes with the well-being platform EMPOWR.
  • Chair massage once a quarter.
  • Comprehensive master classes where you are taken through the expertise of the various departments.
  • Training based on your development needs.
  • Overnight hotel stay and dinner at Bar Brasserie OCCO after end of probationary period.
  • Discounts at The Dylan’s restaurants: two Michelin-starred Restaurant Vinkeles, Bar Brasserie OCCO, High Wine and Chef’s Table até.
  • Discount on your bar and restaurant bill at partner hotels in Amsterdam with the F&B Discount Card.
  • Staff Rate at Leading Hotels of the World worldwide.
  • Social events
  • Two staff parties per year
  • Various outings with your department
  • And many (spontaneous) get-togethers

companyDescription:
We are Creative Fabrica.

We craft exceptional tools, fueled by AI, and premium content for creators and crafters, guiding them from inspiration to creation. With over 7 million users globally, we empower our community to unlock their creative potential and pursue their passion for crafting.

Our marketplace supports over 30,000 global designers, helping them generate income through their designs.

Go Beyond Ordinary.
companySlogan: Offering tools and content for crafters, empowering their journey from inspiration to creation.
descriptionHtml:
We're Creative Fabrica, a fast-scaling tech start-up from Amsterdam.

Our mission

Enable creativity by giving access to everything related to the creative process: inspiration, learning, creating, and sharing. The ‘Creative Inspiration Flywheel’

This creates a self-sustaining community that lives within the Creative Fabrica ecosystem.

About the platform

We launched in 2016 as a marketplace for fonts with a subscription service. In the last 8 years, we have been through tremendous growth, and we're just getting started!

We have scaled to 50 million+ monthly page views, 8 million+ registrations, over 22 million listed products, and 22000+ active designers.

Our focus is to create a platform for designers that enables them to offer exceptional high-quality content. We make their products searchable in unique ways. This allows our customers to speed up their workflow, allowing them to focus on what they love most: Creating.

As our Junior Accountant, you will play a key role in Creative Fabrica’s day-to-day accounting operations, with primary responsibility for Accounts Payable while also contributing to month-end close, reconciliations, journal entries, VAT returns, and other accounting tasks. This role is designed for someone ready to grow into a broader Accountant position over time, gaining exposure to more complex processes and projects.

You will work daily in Oracle NetSuite ERP, liaise with vendors, and collaborate closely with colleagues across departments. We are looking for a detail-oriented, proactive professional who understands accounting fundamentals, thrives in a fast-paced environment, and is eager to develop into a well-rounded accountant.

You will be part of a small, dedicated finance team and report to the Head of Accounting.

What you'll do

Accounts Payable

  • Manage the Accounts Payable mailbox and ensure timely processing of all incoming invoices.
  • Code invoices accurately to the correct general ledger accounts.
  • Follow up on unmatched or unapproved invoices and route them for proper authorization.
  • Process and reconcile credit card transactions.
  • Resolve AP issues such as outstanding, duplicate, or incorrectly recorded invoices.
  • Prepare weekly/monthly payment runs, including SEPA and PayPal payments.
  • Maintain vendor records, manage payment schedules, and ensure compliance with agreed terms.

Bank & Reconciliations

  • Process bank and credit card transactions in NetSuite.
  • Perform monthly bank reconciliations.
  • Reconcile the AP ledger and investigate discrepancies.
  • Prepare and maintain AP-related reports.

General Accounting & Month-End Close

  • Prepare and post journal entries, including revenue recognition entries.
  • Assist with more complex accounting entries under the guidance of the Accountant.
  • Perform monthly account reconciliations.
  • Assist with quarterly VAT preparation and filings.
  • Maintain accurate and up-to-date general ledger records.

Audit & Reporting

  • Support month-end and year-end close procedures.
  • Assist in preparing audit documentation.
  • Ensure compliance with internal controls and accounting standards.

Projects & Process Improvements

  • Support the implementation of automated bank feeds and expense integrations in NetSuite.
  • Help improve expense claim and credit card processing workflows.
  • Contribute ideas to streamline AP and accounting processes for efficiency and accuracy.

What you'll need

  • 2+ years of experience in a similar accounting role, ideally combining AP with other accounting responsibilities.
  • Strong understanding of accounting and accounts payable principles.
  • Experience with ERP systems, preferably NetSuite.
  • Microsoft Excel skills and willingness to learn.
  • Familiarity with VAT regulations and processes.
  • Dutch Accounting Knowledge
  • A detail-oriented, organised, and structured work approach.
  • A degree / past experience in accounting, finance, or business administration.
  • Clear, professional, and solution-focused communication skills.
  • Strong command of English (written and spoken).
  • Ability to work independently and as part of a team.

Nice to have

  • Experience in an international B2C environment.
  • Ability to read and understand Dutch

We'd also like to offer you:

  • Lunch: On days you work in the office a warm fresh lunch will be provided for you.
  • 24 days of paid vacation per year + public holidays.
  • Travel Allowance, if you live more than 10 KM from the office.
  • Company pension plan
  • CF Library
  • Company events and overseas trips!
  • New Joiner Swag Pack!

Is this you?

Get in touch! We'd love to speak to you.

You can apply by clicking the "Apply now" button.

Creative Fabrica is an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees.
