descriptionHtml:
Are you ready to shape the future of finance within a dynamic and purpose-driven investment environment?

A leading infrastructure fund manager with approximately USD 2 billion in assets under management is seeking a talented Corporate Accountant / Financial Controller to join their growing team. This is a greenfield opportunity—you’ll be instrumental in building and in-sourcing the finance function from the ground up.

With a portfolio of critical infrastructure investments supporting nearly 20,000 employees globally, this firm offers a unique platform where your work will be highly visible, commercial, and impactful. This is more than a finance role—it’s a chance to build, influence, and lead within a respected investment platform backed by a legacy of operational excellence and long-term value creation.



What You’ll Do:

  • Develop and enhance accounting processes aligned with internal controls and business needs.
  • Manage a wide range of financial transactions, including revenue, expenses, accruals, and fixed assets.
  • Oversee accounts payable/receivable, payment processing, and invoice reconciliation.
  • Perform monthly and quarterly reconciliations across bank accounts and general ledger items.
  • Support month-end and year-end closings, including consolidation and audit preparation.
  • Collaborate on tax and regulatory reporting, and maintain core financial data for compliance.
  • Contribute to budgeting, forecasting, and management reporting.


What You Bring:

  • 6 years+ of experience in corporate accounting, financial control, or external audit.
  • Solid understanding of accounting principles, financial reporting, and internal controls.
  • Experience with group structures and intercompany accounting is a plus.
  • Proficiency in Excel and ERP systems (Business Central, AllVue and Continia experience is a bonus).
  • Strong collaboration skills and ability to thrive in a fast-paced environment.
  • Fluent in English, with a relevant academic qualification.
  • Tech-savvy with a proactive approach to systems and process improvement.


This role does not support overseas relocation or applicants who need VISA sponsorship.


Contact Rebecca Sheehan for more info, apply here or send your CV directly to RebeccaSheehan@brewermorris.com

companyDescription:
myGwork is the largest global platform for the LGBTQ+ business community. 
Our mission is to make the workplace more inclusive for all by providing individual users and partner organizations access to a wide eco-system of services, including job opportunities, training, mentoring, employer branding, and free community events. Joining the platform is free for individual members, which supports myGwork’s goal of ensuring that the platform's benefits are as accessible and as far-reaching as possible. Corporate members get a tailored service, with carefully curated product packages to help them achieve all their DE&I goals.

myGwork organizes two annual events, WorkFair – the largest virtual global career fair for the LGBTQ+ professionals, graduates and students, and WorkPride – a week-long global conference for the LGBTQ+ business community and allies during Pride Month. The company also recently launched the myGwork Academy, delivering practical and relevant LGBTQ+ training education to help create inclusive workplaces for all. 

For more information, check out www.mygwork.com.
companySlogan: The business community for LGBTQ+ professionals, graduates, inclusive employers and advocates for workplace equality.
descriptionHtml:
This job is with TomTom, an inclusive employer and a member of myGwork – the largest global platform for the LGBTQ+ business community. Please do not contact the recruiter directly.

As an Internal Audit Manager at TomTom, you will play a crucial role within the Internal Audit team, reporting directly to the Lead Manager of Internal Audit. In this capacity, you will be responsible for planning, executing, and delivering assurance and advisory audits focused on key operational, financial, and compliance processes throughout the organization, frequently integrating significant IT components and controls. Your position will require you to cultivate and maintain strong relationships with key stakeholders across various business units, as well as with the Internal Control team and external auditors. Additionally, you will actively contribute to the development of the annual audit plan and the strategic direction of the Internal Audit function within an entrepreneurial and fast-paced environment.What you'll do

  • Independently execute audits across the entire audit process to ensure the delivery of high-quality engagements, covering planning, execution, and reporting, while ensuring consistency and adhering to established procedures and standards
  • Build and maintain strong partnerships with key stakeholders, including unit leaders, the Internal Controls team, and external parties
  • Contribute to the development of the audit roadmap while supporting the overall strategy and procedures of the Internal Audit function
  • Provide guidance and support to finance graduates and guest auditors as needed, facilitating their development and ensuring their effective contributions to the audit process
  • Prepare comprehensive reports to be presented to Management, clearly communicating findings and actionable insights that are monitored and followed up on
  • Participate and assist, from an Internal Auditor perspective, in high impact corporate projects

What you'll need

  • Bachelor's degree in accounting, finance, business administration or similar, potentially completed with a recognized professional qualification e.g. RA, RO, RE or CIA
  • 4 - 6 years of relevant working experience
  • Experience from Big 4 is a plus
  • Experience in IT / operational auditing within a professional services firm, internal audit function or other relevant function
  • Strong communication and relationship building skills (be in continuous contact with the business)
  • Process, problem solving and action orientated mindset
  • Willingness to travel occasionally (up to10%)
  • Fluency in English

companyDescription:
Door de alsmaar veranderende, complexere wereld en steeds nieuwe wet- en regelgeving ontstaan er nieuwe risico’s en kansen. Audit, Risk en Compliance worden hierdoor steeds relevanter. ARC People focust al jarenlang op alléén deze vakgebieden, waardoor wij de markt en de uitdagingen kennen als geen ander.

Met ons eigen team van topexperts én een uitgebreid netwerk van de beste interim professionals voorzien wij snel in uw behoeften. Op meerdere vlakken en in verschillende vormen, zoals interim-collega’s, trainees, co- en outsourcing en werving. Onze specialisten staan garant voor expertise op het allerhoogste niveau en zijn snel ‘up to speed’ om impact te maken binnen uw organisatie. We houden de kosten laag door slim met overhead om te gaan. Zo kunnen we hoogwaardige dien﻿sten leveren tegen faire tarieven. Altijd met oog op de lange termijn.

Wilt u meer weten of gewoon een keer kennismaken? Neem gerust contact op:

companyName: ARC People: Audit, Risk en Compliance
companySlogan: Alles onder controle.
descriptionHtml:
Ben jij een IT Audit en IT Risk deskundige die graag zowel zelfstandig als in teamverband verschillende uitdagende opdrachten aangaat? Ben jij op zoek naar een betere balans tussen werk en privé waarbij je je eigen werk flexibel kunt indelen?


ARC People is inmiddels ruim 11 jaar succesvol binnen audit, risk en compliance. We concurreren met big four en big twenty, vanwege onze deskundigheid, persoonlijke aanpak en faire tarieven. Dankzij jarenlange ervaring en een sterke focus beschikken wij over een kennisvoorsprong binnen deze drie specialistische vakgebieden. Draag jij met jouw IT Audit en/of Risk expertise bij aan de verdere groei van ARC People en haar klanten?


Wat worden jouw bijdragen?

Als Senior Manager IT Audit en Risk ondersteun je onze toonaangevende klanten bij IT Audit en/of IT Risk uitdagingen. Je houdt je bezig met diverse opdrachten zoals IT Risk vraagstukken, general IT controls en maatwerk IT-audits. Bij voorkeur beperk jij je niet alleen tot alleen IT vraagstukken maar ga je ook aan de slag met operationele audits en riskopdrachten.

Je werkt projectmatig aan verschillende opdrachten en draagt (in samenwerking met andere collega’s) bij aan de positionering en groei van ARC People door jouw deskundigheid in te zetten en door lange-termijn relaties op te bouwen.

Eén van onze consultants is momenteel bij één van onze klanten aan het werk als Hoofd Internal Audit, waarbij zij onder andere verantwoordelijk is voor de auditjaarplannen en de verantwoording aan de Audit & Risk commissie.

“Bij ARC people wordt er écht gekeken naar wat de klant nodig heeft. De partners vinden het belangrijk om te kijken naar wat er nodig is om het probleem op te lossen en hoe we dat gezamenlijk met de klant kunnen doen.” – Anita van der Leeuw (Senior Manager IT Audit & Risk).


Wie ben jij?

Wat wij graag bij jou zien:

  • Een afgeronde WO-opleiding + RE-titel;
  • Minimaal 8 jaar werkervaring op het gebied van IT audit en/of IT risk;
  • Je hebt ervaring met projectmatig werken en krijgt energie van het delen van kennis;
  • Tevens neem je ervaring binnen de financiële sector mee;
  • Je bent communicatief vaardig, daadkrachtig en resultaat gedreven, en je hebt een neus voor kansen;
  • Je vindt onze kernwaarden (duurzame relaties, oprechte interesse en deskundigheid) niet meer dan logisch en handelt hiernaar.


Wie zijn wij?

Bij ARC People draait alles om deskundigheid, duurzame relaties en oprechte interesse. Gemiddeld 50 professionals vertegenwoordigen ARC People bij ruim 200 toonaangevende klanten. Je werkt samen met een vast team, nu nog bestaande uit 20 ambitieuze en gedreven interne professionals en een flinke groep interim professionals. Bij ons krijg je het vertrouwen om zelf de touwtjes in handen te nemen. We houden de lijnen kort en delen volop kennis met elkaar. Op vrijdagmiddag proosten we regelmatig met een goed glas wijn en we ontmoeten elkaar graag op kantoor. Je kunt zelfs een viervoeter tegenkomen. Bij ons staat MVO niet alleen op papier, maar het is onderdeel van ons DNA.


Ons aanbod voor jou

  • Een goed salaris (tussen 90K en 110K, afhankelijk van jouw ervaring);
  • Een prima bonusregeling van maximaal 2 maandsalarissen;
  • Een leaseauto of mobiliteitsbudget;
  • Hybride werken, zodat je genoeg tijd hebt voor jouw eigen leven;
  • Een contract tussen 32 à 40 uur, waarbij overwerken niet de norm is;
  • Volop groeikansen die we graag toelichten in een persoonlijk gesprek;
  • Een jaarcontract met de hoop dat je daarna zegt: "Ik blijf!";
  • Jaarlijks 28 vakantiedagen;
  • Opdrachten waar je echt in duikt, je verdiept in het vakgebied, en de uitdagingen van je klant(en) aanpakt. Zo maak je echte impact en draag je bij aan hun doelen;
  • Gemotiveerde collega’s die in een gezellig teamverband mooie ambities waarmaken.


Verder verkennen?

Heb je interesse, reageer dan via de onderstaande button. Je CV en motivatie mailen, kan ook: mkoevoets@arcpeople.nl. Voor meer informatie kan je contact opnemen met Mark Koevoets, Senior Executive Search Consultant, op 06 - 810 424 98. Hij licht deze mooie uitdaging graag verder toe. Ben je benieuwd naar de ervaringen van collega’s die de overstap naar ARC People hebben gemaakt? Laat het weten, dan organiseren wij een vrijblijvend en open gesprek.


Niet voor jou, wel voor een andere topper?

Misschien past de rol je niet of ben je niet (actief) op zoek. Maar je wilt wel andere goede professionals aanbevelen? Dan horen wij graag van je. Natuurlijk stellen daar wat tegenover, dat hoort bij samenwerken. Als een tip leidt tot een plaatsing, dan belonen wij dat met onze Findersfee.


Interesse in andere vacatures?

ARC People werkt voor aansprekende organisaties. Als je wilt weten voor welke posities wij nog meer werven, kijk dan bij onze vacatures. Je kan ons ook volgen op LinkedIn. Of schrijf je in, dan blijf je ook op de hoogte. Solliciteer direct via onderstaande knop.

companyDescription:
Al 65 jaar draait het bij Randstad om meer dan alleen werk. Vanaf onze oprichting geven we mensen betekenis, perspectief en welzijn door werk dat er echt toe doet.

Elke dag vinden wereldwijd ruim 500.000 talenten hun weg via Randstad. Jaarlijks realiseren we meer dan 250.000 vaste banen en bieden we 3,7 miljoen uur aan trainingen. Randstad, Partner voor Talent.
companySlogan: Randstad is wereldwijd het meest gelijkwaardige en gespecialiseerde talentbedrijf.
descriptionHtml:
Randstad, 's werelds grootste HR-dienstverlener, heeft de ambitie om het meest gelijkwaardige en gespecialiseerde talentbedrijf te zijn. Om deze missie te realiseren, zoeken ze een gedreven Senior Financial Accountant voor het Nederlandse Accounting team. Lees snel verder en solliciteer!


Functieomschrijving

Als Senior Financial Accounting Expert werk je vanuit het Nederlandse hoofdkantoor in Diemen. Je bent onderdeel van Team Finance (dat zo’n 100 Financials telt) en sluit je aan bij het hechte team van 7 Accounting Experts.


Het team werkt voor onder andere Randstad Operational, Randstad Professional | Yacht, BMC en Tempo-Team. Jullie vormen de eerste rapportagelijn naar de holding en spelen een sleutelrol: niet alleen in het beoordelen van historische performance, maar juist ook in het navigeren van de organisatie naar toekomstig succes.


Je komt binnen in een geoliede machine, maar met de ontwikkeling van een global finance programma komt er de komende jaren ook veel op de organisatie af. Aan jou de taak om cijfers tijdig en betrouwbaar te blijven rapporteren, en het team te helpen bij complexe vraagstukken en mee te nemen in procesveranderingen.


Taken en verantwoordelijkheden

  • Verantwoordelijk voor het volledige Record-to-Report proces van meer dan 50 entiteiten (> €3,5 miljard omzet), inclusief maand-, kwartaal- en jaarafsluitingen en complexe boekhoudkundige vraagstukken;
  • Overbrengen van jouw belastingexpertise op het team: VPB, BTW (NL-aangifte en procesoptimalisatie), inclusief implementatie van verbeteringen in fiscale processen;
  • Borgen van betrouwbare financiële rapportages, de optimalisatie ervan d.m.v. dashboards (Tableau) en de opzet van ESG-rapportages;
  • Verdere ontwikkeling en automatisering in processen en op datavlak. Hiervoor ben je initiatiefnemer, begeleid je veranderingen en realiseer je een intensieve samenwerking met collega’s uit andere teams.


Kandidaat-profiel

  • Afgeronde opleiding in een financiële richting;
  • Vanaf 7 jaar relevante werkervaring met sterke boekhoudkundige kennis;
  • Expertise in Excel is een must, ervaring met Tableau (of een andere BI-tool) is een pré;
  • Je gaat proactief te werk, bent nieuwsgierig, analytisch en legt complexe verbanden tussen de cijfers en activiteiten. Hierbij heb je affiniteit met de branche;
  • Je bent communicatief sterk en weet zowel finance- als niet-financecollega’s mee te nemen in je verhaal.


Arbeidsvoorwaarden

Randstad Groep Nederland kijkt graag uit naar een dienstverband voor de lange termijn. In deze rol zal je dan ook in vertrouwen starten met een vast contract. De verdere voorwaarden zijn als volgt:


  • Bruto salaris van €5.500 tot €6.500 o.b.v. 40 uur, afhankelijk van je ervaring;
  • Een ruim benefitbudget (12,3%) welke je kan inzetten voor vrije dagen, extra salaris of financiering van bijvoorbeeld sporten, een fiets of een sabbatical;
  • Een bonusregeling van rond de 12,5%, afhankelijk van bedrijfsresultaat;
  • Leaseauto of een mobiliteitsvergoeding;
  • Aantrekkelijke aandelenregeling;
  • 25 vakantiedagen;
  • Pensioenregeling;
  • Mogelijkheid tot thuiswerken en opties voor het inrichten van jouw thuiswerkplek;
  • Verder zijn er voor jou als financial ruime ontwikkelkansen en opleidingsmogelijkheden.


Bedrijfsprofiel

Randstad is de nummer één HR-dienstverlener ter wereld. Wereldwijd zorgen ze er dagelijks voor dat meer dan 600.000 mensen aan het werk kunnen. Daar zijn ze trots op, want werk geeft mensen een inkomen, sociale contacten en voldoening.


In Nederland geven ze hier invulling aan met een hecht team van zo’n 3.000 collega’s. Ze werken vanuit de ‘Partner voor Talent’ strategie, met een duidelijke focus op vier specialisaties: Operational, Professional, Digital en Enterprise Solutions. Gedreven door hun passie voor mensen, helpen ze hier dagelijks tienduizenden talenten en professionals aan werk.


Ze zijn een grote organisatie, maar geloven sterk in persoonlijke aandacht, samen leren, groeien en plezier maken. Ze verbinden talent en professionals met de mooiste kansen en helpen bedrijven met het vinden van de beste, diverse teams.


Randstad Groep Nederland is onderdeel van het beursgenoteerde Randstad N.V., dat wereldwijd actief is in 39 markten.

companyDescription:
EMEA Recruitment is a professional services recruitment specialist in Switzerland, the Netherlands, the Nordics, and the wider EMEA region.

We specialise in Finance & Accountancy, Human Resources, Procurement & Supply Chain, and Operations recruitment at the mid to senior level. 

Since we were founded by Paul and Kelly Toms in 2007, we have focused on building strong relationships with both our clients and candidates. This people-first approach has enabled us to become a strategic partner to some of the most reputable businesses in the world and a trusted advisor to the most in-demand talent across Europe.
companySlogan: Representing the best talent across Switzerland, the Netherlands, the Nordics and the wider EMEA region.
descriptionHtml:
Junior Accountant - Exclusive

€50-55k including holiday + bonus


EMEA Recruitment is delighted to be searching exclusively for a Junior Accountant on behalf of our client, an investment company in Amsterdam.


The Junior Accountant will support the department with daily operations, including bookkeeping, financial reporting, and data entry.


For the successful Junior Accountant, this is an excellent opportunity to grow their career and build on foundational accounting experience.


Responsibilities of the Junior Accountant:

  • Process journal entries and maintain the general ledger
  • Reconcile bank statements and balance sheet accounts
  • Assist with month-end and year-end close processes
  • Assist in preparing reports and audits
  • Maintain financial records in compliance with policies and procedures


Experience required:

  • Bachelor's or Master's in Accounting, Finance or similar
  • 2-3 years' experience in Accounting
  • Basic understanding of Dutch GAAP and IFRS
  • Proficiency in MS Excel and Accounting software
  • Good analytical and problem solving skills


If you are interested in applying for this Junior Accountant role, please register your interest using the link below. Alternatively, please send your CV to bradley.saywell@emearecruitment.com or for a confidential conversation please call Bradley in our Amsterdam office on: +31 20 241 7375.


Due to the high volume of applications we receive, we’re only able to contact those selected for the next stage of the process. If you haven’t heard from us within five working days, unfortunately your application has not been successful. Please continue to explore future opportunities at www.emearecruitment.com


EMEA Recruitment is committed to promoting Equity, Diversity and Inclusion in the workplace. We act as a neutral third party in the recruitment process, basing our candidate searches on skills and experience. We do not discriminate on the basis of age, gender, sex, relationship status, disability, race, religion/beliefs, or sexual orientation.

companyDescription:
Welcome to LRQA, Your Risk Management Advantage.

Risk doesn’t sleep. Neither do we. LRQA helps you manage risk, seize opportunities and secure competitive advantage through our industry-leading connected risk management solutions. From certification and cyber to sustainability and supply chains, we connect the dots to keep you one step ahead.
companySlogan: Your risk management advantage
descriptionHtml:
Job ID:42011

Location:LRQA - Rotterdam - Lotus C Offic

Position Category:Assessors

Position Type:Employee Regular

Unlock your Professional Potential with our Global Auditor Academy

A successful professional career is built by ongoing learning and development.

At LRQA we believe it is important to value and invest in our people to make them feel challenged and empowered to shape a better future together.

Our Auditor Academy is a transformative early careers programme designed to help professionals with industry experience transition into an Auditor role. Our mission is to empower and invest in your skills, knowledge, and connections to enable you to thrive in your career.

Start date for the Academy programme is 6th January 2025, we are now accepting applications for this cohort

What We Offer

Our Auditor Academy is more than just a training program -it’s a gateway to your future. We understand the challenges of career transitions and we are dedicated to providing a supportive, enriching environment where you can grow, learn, and succeed.

Through our academy you’ll gain access to training, real-world projects, and mentorship from world class experts, all tailored to help you achieve a Globally recognized qualification.

What Will You Bring

  • Experience preferably within quality assurance or HSE in automotive manufacturing / production environment
  • A degree (or equivalent qualification) in a relevant subject
  • You will work closely with other internal teams across the Globe, so collaboration, communication and problem solving will be part of your tool kit.
  • Commitment to continuous development taking ownership of your learning

Why join us

As the world's leading global assurance provider, we’re a force for good with sustainability at our core.

We operate in over 150 countries, are recognised by over 30 accreditation bodies worldwide, and defined by our world-class expertise in assessment, advisory, inspection and cyber security services.

We’re here to shape a better future together, helping our clients improve their cybersecurity maturity, source responsibly, achieve product integrity, navigate the energy transition and assure their assets and management systems.

And while we’re proud of our history of delivering game-changing markets first, we’re quick to adapt to new ideas and to the people that bring them. People who are ambitious and forward-thinking and share our passion about doing better; for our clients, the world, and each other.

We are LRQA

Diversity and Inclusion at LRQA:

companyDescription:
EMEA Recruitment is a professional services recruitment specialist in Switzerland, the Netherlands, the Nordics, and the wider EMEA region.

We specialise in Finance & Accountancy, Human Resources, Procurement & Supply Chain, and Operations recruitment at the mid to senior level. 

Since we were founded by Paul and Kelly Toms in 2007, we have focused on building strong relationships with both our clients and candidates. This people-first approach has enabled us to become a strategic partner to some of the most reputable businesses in the world and a trusted advisor to the most in-demand talent across Europe.

descriptionHtml:
EMEA Recruitment is pleased to support our client, a multinational logistics company, in their search for an Assistant Accountant in Rotterdam.



The Assistant Accountant will be responsible for accounts payable, compliance, and general accounting processes, amongst other duties.



The successful Assistant Accountant will receive opportunities for professional growth and development in a dynamic work environment.



Responsibilities of the Assistant Accountant:

  • Supporting with month-end close activities
  • Assist with the audit process
  • Responsible for accounts payable duties
  • Prepare and file VAT declarations
  • Cash reconciliation and managing cash flow



Experience required:

  • HBO degree in Finance, Accounting or similar
  • 3+ years' experience in Accounting or similar
  • Fluent in English and Dutch
  • Familiarity with Exact Online and Excel (preferred)
  • Strong communicator, accurate and proactive



If you are interested in applying for this Assistant Accountant role, please register your interest using the link below. Alternatively, please send your CV to bradley.saywell@emearecruitment.com or for a confidential conversation please call Brad in our Amsterdam office on +31 20 808 1102


Due to the high volume of applications we receive, we’re only able to contact those selected for the next stage of the process. If you haven’t heard from us within five working days, unfortunately your application has not been successful. Please continue to explore future opportunities at www.emearecruitment.com


EMEA Recruitment is committed to promoting Equity, Diversity and Inclusion in the workplace. We act as a neutral third party in the recruitment process, basing our candidate searches on skills and experience. We do not discriminate on the basis of age, gender, sex, relationship status, disability, race, religion/beliefs, or sexual orientation.

descriptionHtml:
The Head Operational Process and Quality is a senior leadership role responsible for driving operational excellence across the GET organization. This role ensures the design and execution of high-quality, standardized service delivery processes across all service areas. Apply now to see if the role is for you!

Your challenges

  • Lead the service design strategy and development for KPMGI Global Technology services that are being consumed by member firms and global function.
  • Lead the comprehensive GT&K Service Management strategy, roadmap and delivery.
  • Oversee KPMGI's Export Control Compliance operational activities in order to comply with the regulators in addition to further automate processes and add efficiencies.
  • Lead, mentor, and develop a large, diverse team to ensure effective program implementation, transparent communication, and continuous improvement, while fostering career growth and skill development across the department.
  • Oversee the quality-of-service delivery, implementing processes and standards that maintain consistency and excellence across all touchpoints. Establish key performance indicators (KPIs) and metrics to measure the effectiveness and impact of services.
  • Provide support and insight for customers which involves working with multiple internal and global teams to schedule and assess the impact of planned initiatives on the platform and set goals for enterprise use of each relevant module, process transformation requirements, and best practices for utilizing various platform solutions.


This is what you bring

  • Minimum of 15 years in senior roles involving complex decision-making, with a proven track record of adaptability, consistent performance during change, and effective communication across teams.Proficient in Excel with the ability to use functions and formula, Familiarity with SAP, Board and Coupa would be an advantage.
  • Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, or a related field (Master’s degree preferred).
  • Deep knowledge of strategic solution delivery models and organizational design, with the ability to drive innovation, continuous improvement, and align resources to emerging opportunities.
  • Strong knowledge of ITSM frameworks (e.g., ITIL) and experience overseeing service performance, incident/problem/change management, and service quality improvements.
  • Experience in designing and optimizing customer journeys, with a focus on delivering standardized, high-quality service experiences.
  • Familiarity with compliance frameworks, internal controls, and audit processes, with the ability to implement and monitor effective governance mechanisms.


What will you do?

About KPMG International

Together with more than 273,000 colleagues in 143 countries throughout our member firms, people at KPMG imagine big ideas and bring solutions to life for clients both big and small. A role with KPMG International will open a world of opportunity in your career.

KPMG International helps set the strategy and protects the reputation of this global organization of independent professional services firms providing Audit, Tax and Advisory services. We deliver value to our member firms and drive positive change in the communities we serve. By joining us you will gain a unique understanding of how a global organization operates and work on projects that impact the whole organization. From setting standards and best practices to developing innovative tech- enabled solutions for clients, you'll be part of a global team changing the way our business operates. We look forward to welcoming you to our team.

Global Technology & Knowledge

The core services provided by Global Technology & Knowledge are more crucial than ever to our future, as we enable KPMG’s digital transformation, provide trusted technology services, ensure security across the network and accelerate our Collective Strategy.

Our ways of working are based on the principles of customer-centricity, communities of expertise, an optimized delivery model, flexibility, a culture of empowerment, and fulfilling careers.

We are organized under five new ‘domains’: Technology Portfolio Delivery, Global Enterprise Technology, Technology Strategy & Blueprint, Global Information Security Group and Business Operations.

This is an exciting time for us as we continue to drive technology excellence at the heart of Collective Strategy v3.0, and our GT&K colleagues all play a pivotal role in making this a success.

About This Team // Global Enterprise Technology

Global Enterprise Technology is one of five main domains within KPMG’s Global Technology & Knowledge group. Global Enterprise Technology provides innovative components that KPMG’s business functions and member firms use to deliver client-facing solutions. Global Enterprise Technology also provides the information protection and technology infrastructure that secures KPMG’s technology environment and connects its network of member firms. Global Enterprise Technology works with the other GT&K domains to provide KPMG technology solutions that leverage world-leading partnerships, disruptive digital capabilities and access to the firm’s collective intelligence.

Key accountabilities % of time

35% Process Quality & Customer Experience:

  • Lead the service design strategy and development for KPMGI Global Technology services that are being consumed by member firms and global functions. This involves the development of service design blueprint(s), identifying the necessary governance, processes and resources for operationalizing services.
  • Oversee the quality-of-service delivery, implementing processes and standards that maintain consistency and excellence across all touchpoints. Establish key performance indicators (KPIs) and metrics to measure the effectiveness and impact of services.
  • Identify process inefficiencies and implement solutions to eliminate bottlenecks. Use data and feedback to continuously refine service journeys and improve satisfaction.
  • Enhance performance, quality, and cost-efficiency of the delivery function by defining and executing appropriate resourcing, offshoring scope, and utilization strategies, as well as increasing automation and process efficiency; report value creation and tracking to leadership
  • Accountable for Business Technology Resiliency stewardship and responsible for Service Assurance, owning the service interface with KPMG member firms on behalf of Global.


30% Service Management:

  • Lead the comprehensive GT&K Service Management strategy, roadmap and delivery; provide support and insight for customers which involves working with multiple internal and global teams to schedule and assess the impact of planned initiatives on the platform and set goals for enterprise use of each relevant module, process transformation requirements, and best practices for utilizing various platform solutions
  • Manage a proficient Service Management team to handle the operational tasks involved in guiding the development, design, implementation, operations and support of the technology vision, strategy, and roadmap for the Service Management (ITSM); stay up-to-date with industry trends and best practices to drive continuous improvement while leveraging AI and leading middle and back office business transformation; minimize technical debt and improve team and platform operations
  • Drive service performance monitoring, reporting, and continuous improvement initiatives.
  • Ensure effective incident, problem, change, and request management processes are in place and optimized.
  • Collaborate with service owners to ensure consistent and reliable service delivery.


15% Compliance Control:

  • Oversee KPMGI's Export Control Compliance operational activities in order to comply with the regulators in addition to further automate processes and add efficiencies
  • Ensure operational processes comply with internal policies, regulatory requirements, and audit standards.
  • Establish and maintain controls to monitor compliance and mitigate risks.
  • Partner with internal audit and risk teams to address findings and implement corrective actions.


20% Leadership & Team Management:

  • Lead, mentor, and develop a large, diverse team to ensure effective program implementation, transparent communication, and continuous improvement, while fostering career growth and skill development across the department
  • Manage global vendor relationships and ensure contractual obligations, including Service-Level Agreement (SLA) management are met; participate actively in forecast and annual budget planning, as well as manage the operational budget
  • Represent the Global Enterprise Technology in meetings, support groups, and vendor interactions, fostering effective working relationships, and collaboration across teams to enhance client value, as well as operational efficiency
  • Promote a culture of accountability and transparency in service operations.


This is what we offer

Welcome to We Do Group.
 
A new type of recruitment. 
 
At We Do Group we are proud to say we do things differently. 
 
We have an innovative, creative and highly engaged approach to finding solutions. We create opportunity and make things happen.  
 
Working hard, being kind and staying humble is core to our approach. 
 
We Do Group was founded by three experienced recruiters driven to build something special, something that challenges the norm and focuses on finding new ways of delivering exciting results. 
 
So, what’s the story behind the name? 
 
Success in recruitment is all about teamwork. The ‘We’ element represents our focus on connecting and working together with individuals and businesses. 
 
We have a positive, can-‘Do’, results and solutions led mindset. We are unapologetically ambitious; and we appreciate people rely on us to get things done. 
 
We offer permanent, interim and contract recruitment solutions across the following sectors:
 
•	Finance & Accounting 
•	HR 
•	Executive Search 
 
If you are looking to hire talent into your team or business, or if you are looking for support with your own career then please get in touch.  
 
Recruitment is all about storytelling. Each Individual and Company is on a different journey. Let us tell yours. 
 
How can we help?

descriptionHtml:
Greater Rotterdam – Hybrid – Permanent – Industrial Engineering


€60,000 – €70,000 + benefits


Are you an experienced Accountant looking to work in a truly international business? Do you thrive in a hands-on finance role with exposure to both transactional & reporting duties?


We Do Group are hiring an Accountant to join a long-established industrial engineering group that delivers specialist services to major energy and process industries worldwide. You’ll work closely with suppliers, colleagues, and auditors to keep the numbers accurate and the finance function running smoothly.


WISH LIST


  • Qualified Accountant with hands on experience reporting under Dutch GAAP (RJ)
  • Fluent Dutch & English speaker, resident in the Netherlands
  • Organised, accurate, and confident working to deadlines


THE ROLE


  • Verify and post purchase invoices into Oracle Accounts Payable
  • Reconcile and prepare general ledger account specifications
  • Archive paid invoices in line with audit requirements
  • Monitor accuracy of accounting data and ensure timely submissions
  • Liaise with suppliers and internal teams on invoices and settlements
  • Record scanned invoices on invoice processing software, following company procedures
  • Support month-end close and reporting cycles
  • Assist with audits and compliance reviews
  • Maintain knowledge of accounting and tax regulations
  • Contribute to continuous improvement of finance processes
  • Provide ad-hoc support to the Finance Manager as required


YOUR PROFILE


  • Qualified Accountant
  • Effective communicator – written & verbal
  • Strong MS Excel skills
  • Fluency in Dutch and English
  • Hands on experience reporting under Dutch GAAP (RJ)


SALARY & BENEFITS


  • €60,000 – €70,000 per year
  • Hybrid working (Greater Rotterdam office base)
  • Permanent contract of employment
  • Training, induction, and professional development opportunities
  • Inclusive, collaborative workplace culture

companyDescription: Bij Confirm bieden we een menselijke blik op Audit & Assurance. Met de gebundelde expertise van drie lidkantoren leveren we hoogwaardige diensten die inzicht en zekerheid bieden voor ondernemers en stakeholders. Bij ons draait alles om Audit & Assurance, met specialisaties in diverse sectoren, zowel profit als non-profit. Sluit je aan bij ons team en draag bij aan innovatieve oplossingen binnen een cultuur van verbinding en groei.
companySlogan: Voor mensen van waarde
descriptionHtml:
Heb jij al door jouw ervaring in de audit een eigen blik op het vak ontwikkeld en sta jij stevig in je schoenen? Dan ben je voor ons van waarde en zijn we op zoek naar jou.


We geven je de ruimte je ambities te verwezenlijken maar dan wél met een goede balans. We zijn in de eerste plaats van waarde voor onze klanten, anderzijds voor onze mensen. Audit is mensenwerk, en op het snijvlak van mens en audit hebben we ruimte voor ambitieuze senior assistenten die van daaruit verder willen groeien en nog meer van waarde willen zijn.


Met een aantal jaren ervaring als assistent ben jij in staat om zelfstandig te werken en wil je je door ontwikkelen om de meer complexere posten en processen te doorgronden. Ook begeleid je junior collega’s, die net starten, en stuur je hen aan. Daarin staat jouw vooruitstrevende blik centraal en zie je altijd oplossingen in plaats van problemen.


We bieden jou:

  • een salaris dat past bij je ervaring en ambities en goede secundaire voorwaarden;
  • een omgeving waarin jouw ontwikkeling en jouw studie centraal staan;
  • een middelgroot audit kantoor wat een verrassend en uitdagend klantenportefeuille bedient en waar jij je dus breed kan ontwikkelen en kan bijdragen aan verdere groei;
  • een omgeving waarin je uitgedaagd wordt om buiten de traditionele paden te denken en waar creativiteit wordt gestimuleerd;
  • een team met ca. 50 collega’s die werken heel serieus nemen, maar ook plezier maken.


Van jou verwachten we dat je:

  • een aantal jaren (2 tot 4) ervaring hebt in een controlepraktijk;
  • leergierig bent;
  • in staat bent om te denken in oplossingen;
  • plezier hebt in het begeleiden van startende collega’s;
  • goed kan luisteren
  • een dosis gezonde humor en teamspirit meeneemt.

Daarbij gaan we er van uit dat je nog studerend bent om registeraccountant te worden en dat je deze studie met succes wilt afronden. Daar helpen wij, en je toekomstige collega’s je natuurlijk graag bij.


Ben jij voor ons van waarde? Graag maken we kennis met jou om te horen wat wij voor elkaar kunnen betekenen. Wil je aanvullende informatie over de vacature of heb je een andere vraag? Dan kun je mailen naar Anne (a.vankempen@confirm.nu).


Acquisitie naar aanleiding van deze vacature wordt niet op prijs gesteld.

Daar helpen wij, en je toekomstige collega’s je natuurlijk graag bij.Ben jij voor ons van waarde? Graag maken we kennis met jou om te horen wat wij voor elkaar kunnen betekenen. Wil je aanvullende informatie over de vacature of heb je een andere vraag? Dan kun je mailen naar Anne (a.vankempen@confirm.nu).Acquisitie naar aanleiding van deze vacature wordt niet op prijs gesteld.

companyDescription:
The Kraft Heinz Company is one of the largest food and beverage companies in the world, with eight $1 billion+ brands and global sales of approximately $25 billion. We’re a globally trusted producer of high-quality, great-tasting, and nutritious foods for over 150 years. While Kraft Heinz is co-headquartered in Chicago and Pittsburgh, our brands are truly global, with products produced and marketed in over 40 countries. These beloved products include condiments and sauces, cheese and dairy, meals, meats, refreshment beverages, coffee, infant and nutrition products, and numerous other grocery products in a portfolio of more than 200 legacy and emerging brands.

We spark joy around mealtime with our iconic brands, including Kraft, Oscar Mayer, Heinz, Philadelphia, Lunchables, Velveeta, Maxwell House, Capri Sun, Ore-Ida, Kool-Aid, Jell-O, Primal Kitchen, and Classico, among others. 

No matter the brand, we’re united under one vision: To sustainably grow by delighting more consumers globally. Bringing this vision to life is our team of 37,000+ food lovers, creative thinkers, and high performers worldwide. Together, we help provide meals to those in need through our global partnership with Rise Against Hunger. We also stand committed to responsible, sustainable practices that extend to every facet of our business, our consumers, and our communities. Every day, we’re transforming the food industry with bold thinking and unprecedented results. If you share our passion – and are ready to create the future, build a legacy, and lead as a global citizen – there’s only one thing to do: join our table and let’s make life delicious!
companySlogan: Let's make life delicious!
descriptionHtml:
Job Description

Role Summary

The Head of Global Food Safety plays a crucial role in ensuring that Kraft Heinz organization adheres to relevant laws, regulations, and industry standards while maintaining a high level of quality in its products, services, and operations. This is a crucial position within Kraft Heinz organization, to ensure product and organizational compliance with applicable laws, regulations and standards.

This role is responsible for creating and standardizing risk management processes globally within FSQ to ensure practices are in place to prevent serious incidents and recalls.

The Head of Global Food Safety is responsible for timely compliance with ever changing laws and regulations as well as customer and consumer requirements; they should work with internal experts and recognized external bodies to ensure Kraft Heinz risk management methodologies remain up to date.

The Head of Global Food Safety is responsible for producing a competence framework for end to end, robust, product lifecycle risk assessment and management processes that are fit for purpose creating and implementing the company’s global compliance strategy. This position will work closely with Legal, Regulatory, R&D and other key functions to ensure compliance of our products, systems and processes.

Key Responsibilities

  • Develop and implement food safety strategies that align with the organization's goals and objectives, to ensure global food safety performance.
  • Serve as the business expert on the food safety risk assessment process, applying recognized risk assessment principles and processes to provide expert judgement and ensure course correction.
  • Establish and maintain process/procedures to ensure physical, chemical and microbiological safety of our products.
  • Lead the validation and verification of food safety risk management processes and practices covering
  • the lifecycle of product,
  • supply chains,
  • incident management,
  • change control,
  • emerging legislation.
  • Create resources and tools to support KHC employees in developing and maintaining food safety risk management processes and activities
  • Lead the implementation of digital tools to enhance risk management and compliance, enabling data-driven decisions,
  • Design and roll out comprehensive training and development programs in all aspects of risk assessment and risk management,
  • Ensure risk assessors can identify hazard and develop risk control / mitigation plans,
  • Promote best practice development and sharing, proactive decision-making and communication,
  • Develop companywide food safety culture programs to create awareness about individual responsibilities and champion ownership at all levels.
  • Develop and implement programs to audit, track and report compliance to company and legal food safety requirements; and evaluate their efficiency in risk reduction, brand protection and business support.
  • Collaborate and engage stakeholders across the business to build capabilities, alignment, and ownership of food safety risk assessment processes. Work closely with FSQ teams at zone and BU level to ensure a standardized, compliant, and effective approach to food safety.


Additional Responsibilities

GCOE budget for FSQ is approximately $1mm, this role has a contributory part to play in setting and controlling the budget.

Financial impact of role poor risk control in our sites and EMs can lead to significant supply chain disruption and brand damage. Recalls and withdrawals are a consequence of this and in the worst case could lead to consumer injury and death.

A typical cost of incidents in a 12-month period, globally, could be up to $40MM – this role is targeted to reduce the number of issues through implementing effective risk assessment processes by training and empowering our people to execute this process.

Expected Experience And Required Skills

  • Bachelor’s degree in food science, microbiology, or related field. Master’s degree preferred.
  • Minimum of 5 years of experience in a food safety and quality or regulatory leadership role, with demonstrated success in building & managing compliance programs.
  • In-depth knowledge of food industry, food safety risk and product lifecycle management.
  • Recognized as a subject matter expert in their field. Strong knowledge of food safety regulations and standards (e.g., HACCP, FSMA, GFSI, and ISO 22000) and ability to translate them into internal standards.
  • Certified in food safety systems, such as HACCP, PCQI, or FSVP.
  • Ability to think strategically and develop and execute impactful plans and programs.
  • Strong analytical skills, with the ability to interpret data, and identify trends and opportunities for improvement.
  • Proven project management skills with a results-oriented approach..
  • Digitally oriented mentality, able to assess and implement technological solutions to improve operational efficiency and enable data-driven decision-making.
  • Experienced in leading and managing teams, including coaching and mentoring employees.
  • Excellent communication and interpersonal skills.
  • Skilled at influencing key stakeholders and senior leaders across different functions and regions.


Key Performance Indicators

Recalls & withdrawals.

Quality incidents

Internal & external audit & inspection- compliance

Work Environment & Schedule

Though the role is ideally based out of our Amsterdam office, this role will involve working and coordinating with global teams and partners in different time zones. This role will involve frequent travels to our plants and offices around the world.

What We Offer You

  • An ambitious employer; we only want the best for you.
  • A fast career track like only few other companies can match.
  • A competitive salary and excellent bonus structure (above market); at KraftHeinz we strongly believe and live by ownership and meritocracy.
  • Additional benefits such as Commuting Allowance, Hybrid Work Allowance & Reimbursements, Bicycle & Gym Discount Plan, Employee Assistance Program and Supplemental Health Insurance.
  • A wide range of discounts with KraftHeinz’s partnerships on fashion, electronics, travels and many more!
  • Last and not least; there are always room for new insights If you have an idea, please let us know, and we can set it in action!


Location(s)

Amsterdam

Kraft Heinz is an Equal Opportunity Employer – Underrepresented Ethnic Minority Groups/Women/Veterans/Individuals with Disabilities/Sexual Orientation/Gender Identity and other protected classes.

Sprint Intermediair is een specialistisch werving- en selectiebureau dat zich met haar activiteiten volledig focust op het commerciële segment van de arbeidsmarkt. In het verlengde hiervan assisteren wij organisaties met een personeelsbehoefte en begeleiden wij sales- en marketingprofessionals naar een nieuwe uitdaging. Een positief onderscheidende werkwijze heeft ertoe geleid dat Sprint Intermediair, binnen haar domein, een begrip is geworden in werving- en selectieland.

Een positief onderscheidende werkwijze is een werkwijze waarbij wij investeren in kwalitatieve contacten met onze doelgroep en het vasthouden aan onze kernwaarden ons in staat stellen om werkgever en werknemer duurzaam te verbinden. Menselijk kapitaal werf en selecteer je op een menselijke manier!

descriptionHtml:
Bedrijfsomschrijving

Qander is de grootste bankonafhankelijke kredietverstrekker van Nederland. Met meer dan 25 jaar ervaring bieden wij vanuit ’s-Hertogenbosch verschillende soorten financieringen aan onze klanten. Qander is een modern en energiek bedrijf gericht op het zo efficiënt mogelijk ondersteunen van onze klanten en partners. Daarom investeren wij voortdurend in onze medewerkers en werken constant aan ontwikkeling van innovatieve financiële oplossingen. Wij vinden het belangrijk dat consumenten verstandig lenen met bestendige financiële producten die passen bij de klantsituatie. Zorgplicht voor onze klanten is een belangrijk uitgangspunt in onze producten en dienstverlening. Wij vinden het belangrijk te werken volgens de gedragscodes, richtlijnen en normen van de Vereniging van Financieringsondernemingen in Nederland. Qander staat ingeschreven bij de Autoriteit Financiële Markten en heeft een vergunning van De Nederlandsche Bank.


Functieomschrijving

Als Internal Auditor ben je een gedreven vakspecialist met oog voor detail en een passie voor kwaliteit. In deze rol ben je verantwoordelijk voor het uitvoeren van audits en werk je samen met verschillende afdelingen om processen te verbeteren, risico’s te minimaliseren en veiligheid van processen conform wet- en regelgeving te waarborgen. Je werkt hoofdzakelijk vanuit Nederland (’s-Hertogenbosch) en biedt tevens ondersteuning aan onze zusterorganisaties Buy Way Personal Finance in Brussel en Luxemburg. Je rapporteert aan de Manager Audit, Legal & Compliance en werkt samen met één andere Internal Auditor die in deze groep actief is.


Wat ga je doen:

  • Het vormgeven en uitvoeren van het afdelingsbeleid ten behoeve van internal audit in samenspraak met de Chief Executive Officer. Het opstellen van planning (audit plan) ten behoeve van de uit te voeren werkzaamheden;
  • Het opstellen, onderhouden en formaliseren van een audit methodologie in afstemming met de Chief Executive Officer die in overeenstemming is met de IIA standards;
  • Het uitvoeren van audits ten behoeve van het lokaliseren van risico’s en verbeterpunten binnen alle voorkomende activiteiten conform de methodologie;
  • Het rapporteren van de bevindingen inclusief aanbevelingen aan de auditees alsmede Executive Committee (ExCo) en Supervisory Board. Er is een onafhankelijke rapportagelijn richting de Supervisory Board;
  • Het inrichten, monitoren van follow up mechanisme naar aanleiding van de aanbevelingen in de vorm van acties;
  • Het ongevraagd en gevraagd adviseren van directie t.a.v. beleidsmatige en operationele regelgeving alsmede risico’s.
  • Het tevens ondersteunen van de auditmissies en -activiteiten van onze zusterorganisaties in Brussel en Luxemburg, met af en toe reizen naar deze locaties.


Functie-eisen

Jij hebt procesmatig inzicht (helicopterview), bent analytisch sterk en hebt risk awareness. Je hebt inzicht in organisatorische belangen en kan hierna handelen. Jij gaat discussies niet de weg en bent standvastig / vasthoudend. Je moet jezelf onpartijdig en onafhankelijk kunnen opstellen. Verder herken je jezelf in het volgende:

  • Afgeronde HBO of WO opleiding;
  • Je hebt een Audit achtergrond;
  • Basiskennis van wet- en regelgeving met in het bijzonder IIA standards;
  • Je beschikt over een diploma WFT Adviseur Consumptief Krediet of bent bereid om deze te behalen;
  • Communicatief sterk (zowel mondeling als schriftelijk) en een goede beheersing van de Nederlandse en Engelse taal. Je aanbevelingen en rapporten worden veelal gecommuniceerd in het Engels.
  • Je bent bereid om sporadisch te reizen tussen de verschillende organisaties.


Arbeidsvoorwaarden

Wij bieden een informele en dynamische werkomgeving met leuke collega's en een prettige werksfeer! Je krijgt bij ons de nodige vrijheid en alle ruimte om jezelf verder te ontwikkelen. Daarnaast kent Qander uitstekende arbeidsvoorwaarden, waaronder:

  • Bruto maandsalaris tussen €5000,- en €6100,- obv 40 uur (indicatie)
  • Vakantiegeld en een vaste 13de maand die beide jaarlijks of maandelijks uit te laten keren zijn;
  • Premievrij pensioen, je betaalt dus geen eigen bijdrage;
  • Bonus van max. 8% van het totale bruto jaarsalaris o.b.v. organisatie- en persoonlijke doelen;
  • Mogelijkheid om deels thuis te werken met een thuiswerkvergoeding van netto € 2,40 per dag;
  • Minimaal 29 vakantiedagen op fulltime basis (extra vakantiedagen vanaf 35 jaar);
  • Reiskostenvergoeding van € 0,23 per kilometer of volledige vergoeding van het openbaar vervoer;
  • Diverse collectiviteitskortingen voor verzekeringen;
  • Mogelijkheid voor het volgen van cursussen en opleidingen;
  • Je werktijden kunnen in overleg flexibel worden ingericht tussen 7.30 en 18.00 uur.

companyDescription:
Amicorp Group provides corporate management, financial markets and fund administration services, particularly across emerging markets. Over the past three decades, we have focused on developing innovative and bespoke solutions that help businesses, asset managers, institutional investors and family offices to succeed in a dynamic macro environment, building trusted, lasting relationships along the way.

We are fully committed to being a responsible corporate citizen. We do that through our internal policies and through the Amicorp Community Foundation which strives to empower local communities and create lasting structural change that build a better future for people and the planet. Our projects include the Amisewaka – Desa Les Community Center in Bali, our Tuma Mi Man daycare center in Curaçao, our AmiForest coconut tree plantation in the Philippines to offset our carbon emissions from travel, and the Limpopo Vocational Center in South Africa, a regeneration project that will provide training for marginalized youth in life skills, hospitality, conservation and veterinary care.
companySlogan: Building relationships that last
descriptionHtml:
SUMMARY

The role holder leads the client relationship through a team of (Senior) Account Managers and Legal Assistants to deliver pro-active, exceptional service to client companies. The role holder is accountable to generate revenue based on value-charging by coordinating legal, administrative and corporate activities for companies. The role holder also promotes (new) products and services and actively participates in growing the business from existing clients as well as identifying prospective clients through client referrals. As Director of various Amicorp entities and client companies, the role holder supports the management in realizing the projected turnover and returns based on the short and long term strategy of Amicorp.


PRIMARY DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES

- Responsible for managing the portfolios of clients and guide them on international structuring. Work directly with our clients in analyzing and interpreting regulations, procedures, documents relating to our clients’ legal and fiscal opportunities

- Setting up and managing corporate and private legal structures and actively participating in growing and marketing our business. Administering a portfolio of companies, limited partnerships and trusts in accordance with applicable legislation

- Monitor, manage and check the contracts (in any form whatsoever) of corporate clients. Monitor, manage, verify and approve the preparation of minutes of shareholder meetings (Ordinary or Extraordinary), and proxies, list of attendance

- Ultimately responsible for the quality of the client files. Assure that companies are capitalized, and that the management agreements are available in the clients’ files.

- Manage companies and ensure that all information in the client files is correct and up-to-date

- Monitor, manage, verify and approve the preparation of minutes of meetings of boards of directors or managers and proxies. Coordinate issues concerning taxes, administration, and banking of client companies with the Client Accounting Department

- Fully understand the client’s activities and make sure that any action taken is consistent with the company’s activities and purpose within the legal and economic framework for the company. Assure that all correspondence is signed in conformity with the signatory authority list

- Ensure adherence to and ongoing analysis of Amicorp’s 4-Pillar approach in particular compliance, legal and corporate services, transaction monitoring and tax and transparency.

- Anticipate and manage the impact of any emerging regulatory, taxation or legal changes and ensure compliance related risk addressed on behalf of the client.

- Achieve financial chargeability targets, ensure effective monitoring of transactions, handle pricing, invoicing and collection of outstanding fees for the portfolio of clients.

- Promote Amicorp in specialized round tables and prepare articles for publishing in internal and external publications

- Work closely with the Local Management Team, Client Acceptance Committee (“CAC”) and Compliance to find commercial solutions to onboarding and on-going client administration/service as appointed member of the CAC

- Ensure that “Know Your Customer” rules are adhered to and that client operates within the framework. Comply with all internal procedures regarding the correct use of automated programs for recording all required client and company data. Maintain up-to-date local office policies and procedures in alignment with local regulations and requirements.

- Act as a trusted advisor to the client and adhere to the regular client contact/ communication guidelines set by the Company, aimed at building personal and long-term relationship with the clients.


PERSONAL AND TEAM TARGETS

- Lead by example by accurately recording chargeable time and value of service using Maconomy, ensure effective monitoring of transactions, handle pricing, invoicing and collection of outstanding fees for the portfolio of clients.

- Monitor teams' productivity and chargeability/ billing on weekly basis. Solve payment problems, as well as collection of outstanding invoices. Drive teams’ chargeability by providing commercial leadership with respect to all client (chargeable) activities in order to achieve the production (chargeability) and revenue targets. Solve payment problems, as well as collection of outstanding invoices

- Monitor all client accounting and legal & company management activities to ensure quality and timeliness

- Build and maintain strategic business relationship with clients; guide the team, Senior Account Managers and Account Managers to build client relationships and ensure timely and quality delivery


OFFICE PROFITABILITY PERFORMANCE AND GROWTH

- Ensure office profitability and performance aligned with the approved business plan for the office. Review client management agreement, payment approvals and ensure customer policies, procedures and contractual agreements are consistently met.

- Review time sheet of team members and monitor their productivity and chargeability on weekly basis, plus review of productivity reporting and performance against targets. Discuss employee/ office performance reports with Group and Local Management on a monthly basis and take corrective actions for improvement.

- Finalize Goals for employees, continuously evaluate performance and take corrective actions to deal with under-performance together with Group Human Resources.


PROCESS AND CONTROLS

- Ensure that the sale of entities is in line with the target set and appraise the Sales Lead/ Marker Coordinator on adequate measures to be taken to improve sale of entities.

- Compliance with statutory authorities and audit requirements.

- Ensure contingency plans are in place for all services to include business recovery plans.

- Maximize utilization of resources and drive cost control measures across operations.


CANDIDATE PROFILE

- Bachelor or Master Degree in Law, Accounting, Tax or another business related field, additional relevant qualification would be desirable.

- At least 15 years of experience inclusive of at least 5 years in senior management positions in financial services industry (preferably with international exposure).

- Good understanding of key financial services and industry related legislation. Familiar with international requirements - e.g. CRS, FATCA, BEPS, GAAR, ESR, that affects the clients and entities in target markets.

- Strong commercial mindset, takes initiative, positive and proactive, dedicated, focused and revenue and target driven.

- Excellent leadership, organizational, interpersonal and communication skills. Successful team management experience including performance management and employee development.

- Excellent client facing skills. Well-developed spoken and written communication skills and the ability to tailor style to relevant audiences, and successfully liaise with people at different levels. Excellent English language fluency; additional languages preferred.

- Strong analytical and problem solving skills, solution driven, highly organized and detail-orientated with good decision making and time management skills. Independent, hands-on and takes accountability to deliver solutions and results.

- Ability to adapt and work in a smaller, dynamic local team environment; along with being part of a bigger matrix organization. Proven leadership and team player skills, with ambition to excel in the role.

- Demonstrated knowledge and understanding of operational efficiency issues and quality management and control matters.
descriptionText: SUMMARY The role holder leads the client relationship through a team of (Senior) Account Managers and Legal Assistants to deliver pro-active, exceptional service to client companies. The role holder is accountable to generate revenue based on value-charging by coordinating legal, administrative and corporate activities for companies. The role holder also promotes (new) products and services and actively participates in growing the business from existing clients as well as identifying prospective clients through client referrals. As Director of various Amicorp entities and client companies, the role holder supports the management in realizing the projected turnover and returns based on the short and long term strategy of Amicorp.PRIMARY DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES - Responsible for managing the portfolios of clients and guide them on international structuring. companyDescription:
We are proud to announce that from now on we – Hark/Sion & Microflor – will join forces and continue under the name Labeau. 

Breeding and propagating is in our DNA, 
Inspire, connect and continuously innovate, that’s our mission!

On the highest level of quality, driven by a worldwide ambition. 
With full passion, High-tech knowledge and boundless creativity 
we grow into our next phase. 

Together. 

We are: Labeau 

Next generation young plants, creators, people, breeders.
companySlogan: Next generation breeders
descriptionHtml:
Labeau was formed from a merger announced in February 2024 between Hark/Sion and Microflor. The company specializes in the breeding and propagation of young ornamental plants, with Phalaenopsis being its largest product. With around 950 employees and locations across Germany, Belgium, Slovakia, Turkey and the Netherlands, Labeau is the second largest breeding company for Phalaenopsis worldwide.


We are seeking an Financial Controller who will be responsible for overseeing and optimizing the group’s financial operations, reporting, and compliance processes for the Labeau group


Currently the group financial organization is going through a post-merger transition in which you will play an essential role.



Your role ?


  • You’ll support the rollout of ERP systems in different countries, helping to streamline and harmonize financial processes.
  • You’ll be responsible for specific parts of the monthly, quarterly, and annual financial reports, and translate financial data into clear strategic advice for the leadership team.
  • You’ll lead the forecasting process for the balance sheet, cash flow, and non-trade profit and loss accounts.
  • You’ll manage and optimize cash flow and working capital to ensure healthy financial operations.
  • You’ll track and analyse key performance indicators (KPIs), providing insights that help steer the business.
  • You’ll keep a close eye on overhead costs and implement controls to manage them effectively.
  • You’ll take ownership of complex financial topics such as corporate income tax (CIT), VAT, inventory valuation, and the preparation of business cases to support strategic decisions.
  • You’ll support the audit process by preparing documentation and ensuring timely and accurate financial statements.
  • You’ll act as the key contact between the finance team and our service organization in Turkey, ensuring smooth collaboration and clear communication.



What do you bring?


  • You have a Bachelor's or Master's degree in Finance & Control, Business administration, Accountancy or alike
  • You have at least 5 years experience in financial positions, partly on senior level
  • Strong skills in Microsoft Office, especially excel also strong in IT and data driven analysis
  • Excellent communication skills in both Dutch and English
  • Hands-on mentality
  • Familiar with monthly and yearly close cycles and management reporting reporting.
  • Experience with ERP implementations and/or Power BI is a pré.



What do we offer you?


A challenging and responsible role in a growing international organization

companyDescription:
Americold is a global leader in temperature-controlled logistics and real estate, with a more than 120-year legacy of innovation and reliability. With more than 230 facilities across North America, Europe, Asia-Pacific, and South America – totaling approximately 1.4 billion refrigerated cubic feet – Americold ensures the safe, efficient movement of refrigerated products worldwide.

Our facilities are an integral part of the global food supply chain, connecting producers, processors, distributors, and retailers with tailored, value-added services supported by responsive and reliable supply chains. Americold delivers world-class service that creates lasting value for our customers and the communities we serve.
companySlogan: We help our customers feed the world.
descriptionHtml:
Americold is a global provider of temperature-controlled infrastructure. We partner with farmers and food producers to ensure their perishable and frozen product reaches food suppliers, restaurants, and your local grocery store without spoilage. Our customers, which include some of the most respected brands in the food industry, rely on our expertise and commitment to safety in order to delivery on their commitments.


Food Quality & Compliance Lead


Goal

As Lead for Food Quality & Compliance you will lead the food safety and quality compliance agenda across our Dutch sites. You are the subject matter expert and regional lead for food quality systems, certifications, audits, and compliance, supporting site teams while holding functional accountability for the implementation and continuous improvement of food quality standards.


This role ensures that legal, regulatory, and customer requirements are consistently met across all sites, fostering a proactive food safety culture and enabling operational teams to succeed within a structured compliance framework.


Key responsibilities

  • Lead and coordinate the deployment of food safety and quality systems across the sites, ensuring alignment with BRC, IFS, HACCP, and legal standards.
  • Own and continuously improve the Quality Management System, including policies, processes, and document control for food safety compliance.
  • Prepare for and lead site support for internal and external audits (e.g. certifications, customer visits, regulatory inspections), ensuring readiness and consistent outcomes.
  • Act as the main point of contact for certification bodies, customers, and local authorities on all food safety and quality matters.
  • Champion a culture of food safety by delivering training, coaching, and awareness programs across all levels of the organization.
  • Lead root cause analysis and corrective/preventive action plans in response to incidents, customer complaints, or audit findings.
  • Monitor microbiological trends, pest control measures, hygiene standards, and validation of key food safety processes across sites.
  • Maintain oversight of required permits, licenses, certifications, and site documentation to ensure continued operational compliance.
  • Partner with local EHS teams to support safe working practices in food storage and handling, aligning quality and safety objectives.
  • Collaborate with operational site leaders to integrate quality compliance into day-to-day processes without assuming direct site ownership.


Requirements:

  • Bachelor’s degree or equivalent in Food Science, Food Safety, or a related field.
  • Minimum of 5 years’ experience in food safety and quality roles, ideally within cold chain logistics, food manufacturing, or distribution environments.
  • Strong working knowledge of HACCP, IFS, BRC, and regulatory food safety requirements.
  • Proven experience leading or coordinating multi-site certification audits and QMS implementation.
  • Strong influencing skills with the ability to lead without direct authority and drive performance across functions.
  • Experience engaging with external stakeholders including customers, authorities, and certification bodies.
  • Excellent communication, analytical, and problem-solving skills.
  • Proficiency in Dutch and English, both written and verbal.
  • Willingness to travel between sites (approximately 40–50%).

companyDescription:
Avail Infrastructure Solutions builds and maintains the world's infrastructure. Our businesses -- Rig-A-Lite and WSI -- engineer and build custom solutions for our customers in the power generation, oil & gas, and industrial markets.  Avail Infrastructure Solutions has over 1,100 employees and operates through a global network of strategically located manufacturing facilities.

Learn more at www.availinfra.com.

- Avail Enclosure Systems is North America’s largest, most experienced manufacturer of custom-engineered and custom-built, modular e-houses for data centers, power distribution centers, and control centers.

- Avail Switchgear Systems has delivered customized high- and medium-voltage switchgear solutions for over 65 years. Our expertise spans every breaker manufacturer, making us a trusted partner in the industry.

- With 60 years of experience in custom-designed bus duct products, Avail Bus Systems is the preferred choice for medium and high-voltage bus systems worldwide.

- Avail Critical Power Solutions provides a full range of low voltage solutions to connect, protect, control, and measure a wide range of electrical applications that increase the reliability and capability of our customers’ facilities. 

- Since 1938, Rig-A-Lite has set the standard for advanced explosion-proof lighting solutions for the world’s most challenging settings, including offshore rigs, production refineries, and chemical plants.

- WSI has a 45+ year history as a global leader of specialty welding and automated welding technologies and techniques across the industrial and energy markets. We offer unparalleled specialized installation, repair, and asset life extension services worldwide.
companySlogan: The future of industrial technology.
descriptionHtml:
Job Title: Finance Accountant, EMEAA

Department: Finance

Reporting to: Director of Finance, EMEAA

Division: EMEAA

Location: Netherlands

AIS WSI Europe is part of the AVAIL group with branches in the USA, Canada, Brazil, India, Poland and the Netherlands. For over 45 years, we have been providing solutions in the field of anti-corrosion, anti-erosion protection, hardfacing services, surfacing and engineering services to the needs of our clients around the world. The recipients of our services are clients from the Oil & Gas industry, petrochemical plants, cellulose and pulp mills, waste to energy plants and the nuclear industry.

Why Us?

  • Progress in a fast paced Private Equity environment, with opportunity to support acquisition or merger transactions.
  • Be part of a forward-thinking company that values innovation, premium customer service, and employee development.
  • Collaborate with a diverse and dynamic team, driving positive change across the organization.
  • Enjoy a vibrant and inclusive work environment where your contributions make a real impact.
  • Be part of a company where your ideas matter, and you have the autonomy to make a real difference.
  • Join a culture where proactivity and creativity are not only welcomed but celebrated.

Your responsibilities:

  • Verification and timely entry of purchase invoices into Oracle Accounts Payable (AP) module
  • Preparation and reconciliation of general ledger (GL) account specifications
  • Archiving paid invoices in accordance with established procedures and audit requirements
  • Monitoring the accuracy of accounting data and overseeing its timely submission by stakeholders
  • Ongoing contact with suppliers and internal departments on matters related to purchase invoices and settlements
  • Recording scanned invoices in the YOOZ application in compliance with applicable financial and purchasing procedures

Our expectations:

  • University degree (finance, accounting or related)
  • Minimum of 2 years of experience in the accounting field, AP/GL preferred
  • Practical knowledge of Oracle Financials or other integrated ERP system; experience with YOOZ welcome
  • Good knowledge of accounting and tax regulations and P2P/O2C processes
  • Proficiency in MS Excel and ability to work with financial data
  • Accuracy, self-reliance and ability to organize work under pressure of deadlines
  • Communicative and cooperative attitude with various departments and contractors
  • Knowledge of English at a level that allows you to work freely with documentation and correspondence (min. B2)
  • Availability to work on-site from our Hellevoetsluis office according to the company’s work schedule.

Apply now!

Take the next step in your career by joining our dynamic team. Embrace the opportunity to shape the future of our organization. Submit your application now and be part of our exciting journey!
