Adyen (AMS: ADYEN) is the financial technology platform of choice for leading companies. By providing end-to-end payments capabilities, data-driven insights, and financial products in a single global solution, Adyen helps businesses achieve their ambitions faster. With offices around the world, Adyen works with the likes of Meta, Uber, H&M, eBay, and Microsoft.

For support visit: https://support.adyen.com
companySlogan: Meet the financial technology platform helping the world’s leading businesses achieve their ambitions faster.
descriptionHtml:
This is Adyen

Adyen provides payments, data, and financial products in a single solution for customers like Meta, Uber, H&M, and Microsoft - making us the financial technology platform of choice. At Adyen, everything we do is engineered for ambition.

For our teams, we create an environment with opportunities for our people to succeed, backed by the culture and support to ensure they are enabled to truly own their careers. We are motivated individuals who tackle unique technical challenges at scale and solve them as a team. Together, we deliver innovative and ethical solutions that help businesses achieve their ambitions faster.

Financial Controller

Adyen is looking for a Financial Controller for the Group Finance team in Amsterdam. As a Financial Controller, you will be focused primarily on owning the trial balance for various APAC entities, overseeing the day-to-day of the entities and managing overarching projects. You will interact and collaborate with internal and external stakeholders in order to support our continued business growth in a scalable, controlled and compliant way. The ideal candidate takes ownership of our local financials for various Indian entities, and has a drive to understand these and is able to tie this back to where we’re heading as a Group globally.

Being part of the centralized Financial Controlling team in Amsterdam means you’ll be working in the (financial) engine room of a registered and listed financial institution; being part of a relatively small team means you’ll be (in)directly involved in a lot of very interesting topics that reach beyond the APAC borders.

What You Will Do

  • Managing the general ledger of multiple entities and branches ranging from mid - high complexity;
  • Preparing and reviewing month end close activities, reviewing the reconciliations performed for the entities managed like fixed asset reconciliations, prepayment reconciliation, accruals reconciliation; along with posting routing month-end journals;
  • Working closely with Adyen regulatory teams to support them in regulatory reportings to ensure compliance with all relevant regulatory deadlines;
  • Working closely with tax teams based in Amsterdam, for tax filings and tax provisioning, by gathering and providing necessary information and liaising with external advisors;
  • Preparation of financial statements, coordination with auditors to facilitate timely and smooth audit processes, and addressing their queries;
  • Meet ad-hoc reporting requests from multiple stakeholders.
  • Support internal control as a key stakeholder to monitor and maintain internal control frameworks for relevant entities.

Who You Are

  • Minimum of 5 years of experience in financial control / accounting alongside audit experience.
  • Indian Accounting Standards knowledge is preferred,CPA/ CA/ RA (or similar) is a plus;
  • You are detail-oriented, with an analytical mindset;
  • Ability to work on multiple projects simultaneously, ability to prioritize;
  • Proactive in projects you take on, able to work independently but never afraid to ask for help;
  • Able to think 2 steps ahead. We are looking for someone that can question the status quo, thinking critically and creatively to implement solutions to scale during Adyens rapid growth.

Our Diversity, Equity and Inclusion commitments

Our unique approach is a product of our diverse perspectives. This diversity of backgrounds and cultures is essential in helping us maintain our momentum. Our business and technical challenges are unique, and we need as many different voices as possible to join us in solving them - voices like yours. No matter who you are or where you’re from, we welcome you to be your true self at Adyen.

Studies show that women and members of underrepresented communities apply for jobs only if they meet 100% of the qualifications. Does this sound like you? If so, Adyen encourages you to reconsider and apply. We look forward to your application!

What’s next?

Ensuring a smooth and enjoyable candidate experience is critical for us. We aim to get back to you regarding your application within 5 business days. Our interview process tends to take about 4 weeks to complete, but may fluctuate depending on the role. Learn more about our hiring process here. Don’t be afraid to let us know if you need more flexibility.

This role is based out of our Amsterdam office. We are an office-first company and value in-person collaboration; we do not offer remote-only roles.

descriptionHtml:
Ben jij een Assistent Accountant met de ambitie om door te groeien? Wil jij jezelf blijven ontwikkelen bij een top10 accountantskantoor? Lees dan snel verder!


Het kantoor heeft meerdere vestigingen over heel Nederland en is nu specifiek voor de vestiging in Ede op zoek naar een Assistent Accountant die de samenstelpraktijk komt versterken. In het team waar je terecht komt zitten meerdere Relatiebeheerders en ervaren Assistent Accountants die jou graag in het team verwelkomen en de fijne kneepjes van het vak willen leren.


Binnen het kantoor hangt er een gemoedelijke en informele sfeer waar er zeker ruimte is voor een babbeltje onder elkaar en doorgaans worden er leuke uitjes gehouden waarbij iedereen inspraak heeft over wat er gedaan wordt. Het team zelf is verder erg divers wat betreft culturen en leeftijden, wat niet geheel onbelangrijk is.


Als Assistent Accountant in deze samenstelpraktijk zullen jouw werkzaamheden voornamelijk bestaan uit het samenstellen van jaarrekeningen voor de MKB-klanten waar jouw team over gaat, het bespreken van de jaarrekeningen met deze klanten en verder help je de klant bij de financiële administratie.


Wat ga je doen?

  • Opstellen van jaarrekeningen voor MKB-klanten
  • Starten met het opbouwen van een eigen klantenportefeuille
  • De financiële administratie van je klanten bijhouden;
  • Opleidingen volgen aan de hand van jouw ontwikkelingsdoelen


Wat neem je mee?

  • HBO werk- en denkniveau
  • Minimaal 2 jaar werkervaring als (junior) Assistent Accountant
  • De drive om te willen doorgroeien en jezelf te ontplooien


Wat krijg je allemaal?

  • Een salaris tussen de €3000,- en €3.800,-
  • 25 vakantiedagen o.b.v. full time dienstverband
  • 8% vakantiegeld
  • Leaseauto of reiskostenvergoeding
  • Goede pensioenregeling
  • Gelijk een vast contract
  • Thuiswerkmogelijkheid en een thuiswerkbudget t.w.v. €500,-
  • Ontwikkelmogelijkheden in de richting waarin jij wilt!
  • Voldoende parkeerplaatsen voor als je met de auto komt
  • Goede bereikbaarheid met het openbaar vervoer
  • En nog veel meer!


Ben jij klaar voor deze uitdaging? Solliciteer dan snel, want de werving is in volle gang! Voor vragen bel je naar [+31 (0)85 0049 010] (tussen 8:00 en 19:00) of neem een kijkje op onze website.


Deze vacature wordt beheerd door Jeffrey van Brown & McCrow , het enige recruitment consultancy van Nederland dat zich exclusief richt op de Accountancy branche. Jouw sollicitatie is volledig vertrouwelijk; wij delen geen informatie zonder jouw toestemming.

companyDescription:
EMEA Recruitment is a professional services recruitment specialist in Switzerland, the Netherlands, the Nordics, and the wider EMEA region.

We specialise in Finance & Accountancy, Human Resources, Procurement & Supply Chain, and Operations recruitment at the mid to senior level. 

Since we were founded by Paul and Kelly Toms in 2007, we have focused on building strong relationships with both our clients and candidates. This people-first approach has enabled us to become a strategic partner to some of the most reputable businesses in the world and a trusted advisor to the most in-demand talent across Europe.
companySlogan: Representing the best talent across Switzerland, the Netherlands, the Nordics and the wider EMEA region.
descriptionHtml:
Accounting & Reporting Lead – EMEA

Location: Amsterdam


We are seeking an experienced Regional Accounting & Reporting Lead to oversee financial operations, reporting, and compliance across multiple regions.


This role involves managing accounting processes, ensuring regulatory adherence, and driving process improvements.


Key Responsibilities:


Support the preparation and review of financial statements

Oversee month-end, quarter-end, and year-end close processes

Collaborate with internal and external auditors, maintaining financial controls and compliance.

Supervise accounting activities, including general ledger management and financial transactions.

Lead and develop a team, fostering a culture of continuous improvement.

Identify opportunities for process optimization and contribute to system enhancements.


Qualifications & Skills:


Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance, or a related field

Financial qualification (CPA, ACCA) would be advantageous

5+ years of accounting experience, with at least 2 years in a leadership role (audit experience also considered).

Strong knowledge of US GAAP and IFRS

Proficiency in ERP systems and financial tools

Analytical mindset, proactive approach, and strong problem-solving skills.

Fluency in English


This role offers an opportunity to contribute to a dynamic finance team while shaping financial processes in a global organization.


If you do not hear from us within two weeks, unfortunately, you have been unsuccessful. To be assured of a response, please apply via emearecruitment.com


EMEA Recruitment is committed to promoting Equity, Diversity and Inclusion in the workplace. We act as a neutral third party in the recruitment process, basing our candidate searches on skills and experience. We do not discriminate on the basis of age, gender, sex, relationship status, disability, race, religion/beliefs, or sexual orientation.

Ben jij op zoek naar een nieuwe uitdaging als Assistent Accountant in de Audit? Wil jij werken met een breed klantenpakket en een goede work-life balance behouden? Dan is dit de perfecte kans voor jou!


Een gezellig accountantskantoor in Nijmegen is op zoek naar een Assistent Accountant met enige ervaring om het super hechte team van 120 collega’s te versterken. Het kantoor werkt samen met een internationaal georiënteerde organisatie, waardoor jij een brede klantenportefeuille binnen het MKB krijgt. Bovendien biedt het kantoor interne cursusprogramma’s en individuele opleidingstrajecten aan om jouw kennis te verbreden en door te groeien.


Als Assistent Accountant zal je jaarrekening posten controleren en het klant contact onderhouden met hen. Om hiervoor in aanmerking te komen, dien je een hbo-opleiding in finance and control of vergelijkbaar te hebben afgerond en minstens 2 jaar werkervaring in een soortgelijke functie te hebben.


Naast een mooi salaris tussen €3200,- en €3800,- (afhankelijk van ervaring), biedt het kantoor 26 vakantiedagen, een studieregeling, doorgroeimogelijkheden, een goede work-life balance, een fijne werksfeer en leuke uitjes met het team.


Solliciteer nu en sluit je aan bij het enige recruitment consultancy in Nederland dat enkel bemiddelt in de Accountancy branche! companyDescription:
Aegon Asset Management 

We are active global investors and organize our capabilities around four global investment platforms where we have deep asset-class expertise: Fixed Income, Real Assets, Equities, and Multi-Asset & Solutions. 

We serve a global client-base of pension plans, public funds, insurance companies, banks, wealth managers, family offices and foundations.
descriptionHtml:
Job Description Summary

Job Description

Purpose of the role

As Director, you will lead the global audit portfolio for asset management operations and technology, while owning the strategic and operational planning activities for the AM internal audit function. This includes oversight of audit planning, resource management and manpower planning, performance tracking, methodology, and transformation initiatives.

You will serve as a senior leader in the audit leadership team, act as delegate for the Chief Auditor when required, and represent internal audit in interactions with senior business leaders and external stakeholders.

Core Tasks And Responsibilities – Audit Director

Audit Execution – Global Ops & Technology

Provide oversight of the annual plan for assigned area of responsibility (Global Operations, Information Technology).

  • Lead audits of global and regional asset management operations, including middle and back-office processes, third-party administration, fund accounting, and outsourcing.
  • Lead cross-border reviews of global shared services, operating models, and outsourcing frameworks, ensuring compliance with applicable regulations.
  • Oversee audits of IT risks, including IT general controls (ITGC), cybersecurity, cloud infrastructure, data governance, and change management.
  • Build strong stakeholder relationships with global COO, Heads of IT, relevant subject matter experts, and other assurance providers like risk, compliance and external audit

Strategic Planning & Operations

  • Support the Chief Auditor in developing and maintaining the annual risk assessment, audit universe, and multi-year audit plan.
  • Support the Chief Auditor with all staffing matters including (but not limited to) recruitment and retention, resource management and manpower planning, training and development.
  • Track key performance indicators (KPIs), audit delivery metrics, issue aging, and resource utilisation.
  • Prepare and coordinate inputs for senior governance forums, including the Audit Committee and Group Internal Audit reporting.
  • Lead internal quality assurance activities, support methodology updates, and manage alignment with Group audit standards.
  • Be an advocate for innovation driving the adoption of audit technology, continuous monitoring, artificial intelligence, data analytics and other emerging technologies within the function.
  • Foster and maintain relationships with Group Audit and other affiliate internal audit teams to the benefit of the function,

Essential

Skills, Qualifications & Other Requirements – Audit Director

  • 15+ years of internal audit experience in asset management or financial services, with increasing management responsibilities
  • Strong understanding of global asset management business models, including front-to-back investment processes, fund administration, and regulatory expectations.
  • Demonstrated ability to manage complex, cross-functional audits and influence senior stakeholders across jurisdictions.
  • Strong executive presence to interact effectively with top level senior management
  • Experience in managing or supporting audit planning, reporting, or methodology enhancement in a leadership capacity.

Desirable

  • Familiarity with audit analytics tools (e.g., Tableau, SQL, Python, Power BI) and continuous assurance concepts.
  • Exposure to strategic audit initiatives such as automation, resourcing models, or regulatory inspections.
  • Experience working in a federated or matrixed audit environment.

Professional Qualifications

  • Bachelor’s degree in accounting, finance, business, or management information systems, or equivalent experience.
  • Preferred additional qualifications: Certified Information Systems Auditor (CISA), Certified Public Accountant (CPA), Chartered Financial Analyst (CFA) or related certification

Behaviours & Competencies

  • Strategic thinker with strong execution skills and attention to detail
  • High integrity, sound judgement, and an independent mindset
  • Able to work collaboratively in a geographically distributed, culturally diverse team
  • Strong written and verbal communication skills with a focus on audit quality

Aegon Asset Management

Aegon Asset Management (Aegon AM) is a leading global investor. Our 385 investment professionals manage and advise on assets of US $337 billion for a global client base of pension plans, public funds, insurance companies, banks, wealth managers, family offices and foundations.

We organize our firm around four investment platforms: fixed income, real assets, equities and multi-asset & solutions, which includes a fiduciary and multi-manager business. Each investment platform has dedicated teams with deep asset-class expertise, organized globally and committed to maximizing client benefit from their specialist areas.

We are an international business: Our 1,160 employees work from 13 locations across Europe, the Americas and Asia. We share a common belief in fundamental, research-driven active management, underpinned by effective risk management and a commitment to responsible investment.

What will you be getting?

  • Compensation level depend upon an applicant’s qualifications and will be determined by hiring supervisors/managers and HR;
  • Plenty of scope and budget for your personal and professional development;
  • A good pension scheme.

Interested? Apply!

Are you interested in joining our Team? We look forward to receiving your resume and motivation letter. Then make sure you apply immediately! Do you have some more questions? Send an email to Talent Acquisition via people@aegon.com.

Diversity & Inclusion

At Aegon Asset Management, we are committed to fostering a diverse workforce and an inclusive culture. We are more than a global Asset Management firm – we are a people business, and we recognize that delivering excellent client outcomes comes from harnessing diverse perspectives. We value diversity in the full range of human uniqueness without limitation including race, gender, ability, language, culture, beliefs, age, origin, background, perspectives and experiences.

Equal Opportunity Employer

Aegon Asset Management aims to be an employer of choice in the financial services industry. As a global company, we offer excellent terms of employment, a very diverse and inspiring work environment, and development opportunities.

companyDescription:
RoomRaccoon is the leading Hotel Management System for independent hotels and accommodation businesses. We serve thousands of hotels globally in over 50 countries with our technology. 

Every day the 100+  ‘Raccoon’ team works hard to empower hoteliers with technology that drives better-performing hotels and guest experiences. We have headquarters in two vibrant cities on opposite sides of the world, Breda in the Netherlands and Cape Town in South Africa. Plus offices in Valencia, Spain and Lisbon, Portugal.
descriptionHtml:
RoomRaccoon


RoomRaccoon was founded by Nadja Buckenberger and Tymen Van Dyl in 2017 with a mission to redefine hotel operations through our all-in-one hotel management system.


Our diverse team of 100 professionals, spread across 5 offices worldwide, serves 2000+ clients across Europe, USA and Africa. Our benefits include equity, formal training budget allowance, annual hotel allowance, as well as birthday and Xmas vouchers.


Interested? Keep reading on!


Job title: Senior Financial Controller


Location: Only considering applications-based in Breda, Netherlands


Language: English Fluent


Work-type: On-sight


Terms: Contractor (6 month contract > 12 month contract > Permanent)


Tasks

Your North Star


The Financial Controller’s primary objective is to maintain the financial health and integrity of the company through rigorous accounting practices, internal controls, and compliance with relevant standards. In a SaaS context, this means ensuring accurate revenue recognition, managing audits, overseeing tax filings, and delivering reliable financial statements on time. The Controller safeguards the company’s fiscal operations while also building scalable processes as the business grows. Their North Star metrics revolve around accuracy, speed, and control — such as the number of days to close the books, audit readiness (including clean audit reports), and precision in revenue recognition for subscription revenue. By anchoring their work in these metrics, the Controller ensures that financial reporting is trustworthy, audit processes are smooth, and regulatory risks are minimized.


Day in the life of a Senior Financial Controller



  • Lead all accounting operations: general ledger, AR/AP, payroll, and fixed assets

  • Manage the monthly, quarterly, and annual financial close process

  • Ensure compliance with relevant finance and taxation laws and manage internal controls

  • Oversee subscription revenue recognition, billing accuracy, and deferred revenue

  • Coordinate with external auditors and manage audit readiness

  • Implement and maintain financial policies, controls, and procedures

  • Oversee tax filings, compliance reports, and liaise with external advisors

  • Partner with the Head of Finance on cash flow management, treasury, and strategic finance

  • Mentor and develop the accounting team; contribute to finance systems improvements


Requirements

  • Bachelor’s degree in Accounting or Finance; CPA preferred

  • 7+ years of experience in accounting/finance, including 2+ years in SaaS

  • Deep understanding of revenue recognition

  • Experience managing audits and implementing financial controls

  • Strong working knowledge of ERP and billing platforms


Benefits

  • Holiday allowance (8%)

  • Equity

  • Mac environment - Equipment provided

  • Incentive based bonus

  • Formal Training budget

  • Annual Hotel experience

  • Friday Lunches


RoomRaccoon Values:



  • Accountability

  • Beat Yesterday

  • Clock Speed

  • Deals a Deal

  • Empower Another


Disclaimer: Thanks so much for your excitement about joining RoomRaccoon! We’re lucky to have so many talented people eager to be part of our team, so we’re reviewing applications as they come in. If you don’t hear from us within three weeks, it means we’ve decided to move forward with other candidates this time—but don’t be discouraged! Keep an eye on our careers page, and we’d love for you to apply for roles that fit your talents in the future!

companyDescription: Five One Recruitment Ltd was started by Michael Rose with the objective to operate differently to the market. With typical performance measurements in the industry only being linked to fees, Five One Recruitment has built its processes to be based on adding value to it's clients rather than focussing on quantity based models. Specialising in the Internal Audit profession, it can offer tailored services to clients with a focus on their environment and culture as well as technical needs to ensure the best talent is found almost anywhere in the world.

descriptionHtml:
Excellent opportunity to join one of the world's top employers (voted one of the top employers for over 10 consecutive years!). The world leading FMCG company is one of the best when it comes to employee experience, having an environment where you can truly be yourself and this is welcomed and bringing ideas and being heard is paramount to the business.


Internal Audit serves as an independent monitoring function which reports to the Chairman of the Board. Through the completion of the annual audit plan, Internal Audit aims to:


  • Support the Company mitigate its main strategic, operational, compliance and financial risks,
  • Contribute to the continuous improvement of processes and controls, and identify and share best practices


The role involves up to approx 30% of travelling time depending on the audit assignment.

A strong interest in data analytics, data visualization or coding. Being internationally mobile for career opportunities.


Responsibilities:


Contribute to the Internal Audit Planning Memorandum which sets out the objectives and scope of the audit assignment:


  • Gather and review background information on the business or process to be reviewed, including through the use of data analytics
  • Assist in the identification of key risks and challenges
  • Derive audit scope and approach from the assessment of key risks and challenges


Deliver audit assignments in whole or in part under the supervision and guidance of the Internal Audit Lead Manager and/or Director, ensuring that the objectives and scope are fully covered within the allotted timeframe:


  • Gather, analyse, and interpret data
  • Organise meetings and conduct interviews with auditees
  • Perform testing of controls (focus on technology controls)
  • Identify draft audit findings and recommendations
  • Discuss draft audit findings and recommendations with auditees
  • Complete working papers and documentation to Internal Audit quality standards.


Draft assigned elements of the audit report for review by the Internal Audit Team Manager and Director and assess management actions within the allotted timeframes:


  • Draft audit findings and recommendations, ensuring that they are factually correct and written in clear and concise English
  • Discuss and incorporate changes recommended by the Director


Contribute to the continuous improvement of the activities of the Internal Audit Department:


  • Identify and make suggestions for enhancing audit processes and programmes and / or the effectiveness and efficiency of the Internal Audit function
  • Continue the development of specialist technical knowledge around IT auditing; keeping up to date as technology use in the group develops (e.g. use of new technologies such as AI, increasing use of Cloud, changes from upgrades to existing infrastructure)
  • Support the department in implementing enhancements to the effectiveness and efficiency of the Internal Audit function


Experience:


  • Relevant university degree or professional qualification such as IT, IS, Analytics, accounting, finance, business economics, CPA, ACCA, CIA, CISA, CISM.
  • Technical accreditations a strong plus (e.g. MCSE, MCSD, SAP Hana).
  • Minimum of 8 years work experience in a relevant operational IT role, external audit, or internal audit. As part of this 8 years, 4 years or more in a managerial role (line management and/or large team assignments).
  • Familiarity with accounting principles and standards, audit techniques (esp. IT), risk management principles, and/or IT processes (incl. Project Management).
  • In depth understanding of one or more of the key technologies used in the group (e.g. Microsoft platforms (including Azure and SQL DBs), SAP Hana, and Service Now).
  • Familiarity with data analysis tools (e.g. SQL, Power BI).
  • Hands-on operational and/or extensive audit experience IT processes and platforms.

companyDescription:
Avail Infrastructure Solutions builds and maintains the world's infrastructure. Our businesses -- Rig-A-Lite and WSI -- engineer and build custom solutions for our customers in the power generation, oil & gas, and industrial markets.  Avail Infrastructure Solutions has over 1,100 employees and operates through a global network of strategically located manufacturing facilities.

Learn more at www.availinfra.com.

- Avail Enclosure Systems is North America’s largest, most experienced manufacturer of custom-engineered and custom-built, modular e-houses for data centers, power distribution centers, and control centers.

- Avail Switchgear Systems has delivered customized high- and medium-voltage switchgear solutions for over 65 years. Our expertise spans every breaker manufacturer, making us a trusted partner in the industry.

- With 60 years of experience in custom-designed bus duct products, Avail Bus Systems is the preferred choice for medium and high-voltage bus systems worldwide.

- Avail Critical Power Solutions provides a full range of low voltage solutions to connect, protect, control, and measure a wide range of electrical applications that increase the reliability and capability of our customers’ facilities. 

- Since 1938, Rig-A-Lite has set the standard for advanced explosion-proof lighting solutions for the world’s most challenging settings, including offshore rigs, production refineries, and chemical plants.

- WSI has a 45+ year history as a global leader of specialty welding and automated welding technologies and techniques across the industrial and energy markets. We offer unparalleled specialized installation, repair, and asset life extension services worldwide.
companySlogan: The future of industrial technology.
descriptionHtml:
Join Our Team as Accountant, EMEA!

  • Applicants must be fluent in Dutch and currently reside in the Netherlands*

AIS WSI Europe is part of the AVAIL group with branches in the USA, Canada, Brazil, India, Poland and the Netherlands. For over 45 years, we have been providing solutions in the field of anti-corrosion, anti-erosion protection, hardfacing services, surfacing and engineering services to the needs of our clients around the world. The recipients of our services are clients from the Oil & Gas industry, petrochemical plants, cellulose and pulp mills, waste to energy plants and the nuclear industry.

Why Us?

  • Be part of a forward-thinking company that values innovation, premium customer service, and employee development.
  • Collaborate with a diverse and dynamic team, driving positive change across the organization.
  • Enjoy a vibrant and inclusive work environment where your contributions make a real impact.
  • Be part of a company where your ideas matter, and you have the autonomy to make a real difference.
  • Join a culture where proactivity and creativity are not only welcomed but celebrated.

Your responsibilities?

  • Verification and timely entry of purchase invoices into Oracle Accounts Payable (AP) module
  • Preparation and reconciliation of general ledger (GL) account specifications
  • Archiving paid invoices in accordance with established procedures and audit requirements
  • Monitoring the accuracy of accounting data and overseeing its timely submission by stakeholders
  • Ongoing contact with suppliers and internal departments on matters related to purchase invoices and settlements
  • Recording scanned invoices in the YOOZ application in compliance with applicable financial and purchasing procedures

Our expectations:

  • University degree (finance, accounting or related)
  • Minimum of 2 years of experience in the accounting field, AP/GL preferred
  • Practical knowledge of Oracle Financials or other integrated ERP system; experience with YOOZ welcome
  • Good knowledge of accounting and tax regulations and P2P/O2C processes
  • Proficiency in MS Excel and ability to work with financial data
  • Accuracy, self-reliance and ability to organize work under pressure of deadlines
  • Communicative and cooperative attitude with various departments and contractors
  • Knowledge of English at a level that allows you to work freely with documentation and correspondence (min. B2)
  • Availability to work on-site from our Hellevoetsluis office according to the company’s work schedule.

What We Offer

  • Training & Induction: World-class induction program to get your started.
  • Growth Opportunities: Continuous learning, mentorship, and professional development.
  • Inclusive Culture: Diverse and dynamic workplace that values creativity and innovation.
  • Competitive Compensation: Contract of employment and competitive salary.
  • Recognition: Your hard work is recognized and rewarded.

Apply now!

Take the next step in your career by joining our dynamic team. Embrace the opportunity to shape the future of our organization. Submit your application now and be part of our exciting journey!

  • on the basis of Article 6(1)(c) RODO, the processing is necessary for the fulfillment of a legal obligation incumbent on the controller;
  • on the basis of Article 6(1)(f) RODO as necessary for purposes arising from the legitimate interests pursued by the controller
  • Your personal data will be stored for a period of: 12 months from the date of receipt of application documents
  • The recipients of your personal data will be:
  • for internal administrative purposes your personal data may be shared with other entities, i.e. AIS WSI Europe B.V. Boyleweg 6, 3208 KA Spijkenisse The Netherlands and AIS WSI USA 560 Horizon Dr. Suite 100 , Suwanee, GA 30024, United States
  • Authorities authorized by law to receive your personal data,
  • You have the right to:
  • access to the content of your data - by exercising this right you have the opportunity to obtain information on what data, how and for what purpose are processed,
  • to rectify your data - using this right you can report to us the need to correct incorrect data or complete data resulting from an error in data collection or processing,
  • to restrict the processing of your data - by exercising this right you can submit a request for restriction of data processing, in case you question the correctness of the processed data. If the request is justified, we can only store the data,
  • erasure of your data - using this right, you can make a request for erasure of your data. If the request is legitimate, we will delete the data immediately. However, this right does not apply if the personal data is processed for purposes related to the fulfillment of the controller's legal obligations or for the performance of a task carried out in the public interest or in the exercise of public authority entrusted to the controller,
  • object - by exercising this right, you can object at any time to the processing of your data if it is processed on the basis of Article 6(1)(e) or (f) (legitimate interest or public interest). Upon acceptance of a request to this effect, we are obliged to stop processing your data for this purpose. In such a situation, once we have considered your request, we will no longer be able to process the personal data subject to the objection on this basis, unless we demonstrate that there are valid legitimate grounds for processing that are deemed by law to override your interests, rights and freedoms, or grounds for establishing, asserting or defending claims,

descriptionHtml:
Position: Property Accountant (Based in Amsterdam, Netherlands)


This position will be responsible for the Property Accountant function in the Amsterdam office and liaison with the HQ finance team based in Singapore.

Job Responsibilities

  • Responsible to review of accounts prepared by property managers / operators / outsourced agents for the portfolio of assets and management company.
  • Be involved in the preparation of financial statements for audit, handling tax matters, budgeting and forecast, perform credit reviews for potential tenants and suppliers.
  • Other duties include involvement in implementing MRI for the European entities.

Job Requirements

  • Qualified accountant with a Degree in Accounting or its equivalent in Finance / Economics.
  • Minimum 6 to 8 years’ of working experience in Real Estate finance operations..
  • You should also be someone who is hands-on with good eyes for details, able to communicate effectively to various parties and work independently in a fast-paced environment.
  • You should be able to communicate in English to liaise with Headquarters in Singapore.
  • Advanced knowledge of spreadsheet and word processing software is a pre-requisite and experience with an SAP system would be advantageous.


Mapletree is a leading, Singapore-headquartered real estate developer and investor, specializing in commercial, industrial, logistics, and residential properties worldwide. The company combines local entrepreneurship with a global strategy and serves a broad range of investors and tenants. With a growing presence across Europe and a strong focus on sustainability, innovation, and operational excellence, Mapletree is a forward-looking employer that provides professionals with the space and support to grow.

companyDescription: Cargill is a family company committed to nourishing the world in a safe, responsible and sustainable way. With over 158 years of experience, we sit at the heart of the supply chain, partnering with producers and customers to source, make and deliver products that are vital for living. Whether it’s making alternative proteins for food manufacturers or helping farmers raise healthier animals with AI, we put as much care into how things are sourced as what we provide. We put people first. We do the right thing—today and for generations to come.
descriptionHtml:
Job Purpose and Impact

The Senior Auditor, Corporate Audit executes complex internal audit programs for high priority risks aligned with Cargill's strategies, operational objectives and compliance requirements. This job performs complex project-level risk assessments, defines and executes audit test procedures, formalizes and tracks audited issues, and supports audit reporting. This position is designed to provide a 2-4 year experience in the Corporate Audit department, with the intent for the employee to then rotate out of Corporate Audit back into a business or functional position at Cargill. The role is open to other Cargill office locations in EMEA or APAC regions.

Key Accountabilities

  • AUDITS: Performs end to end audit processes and procedures across a variety of risk domains within the organization and participates in audit engagement planning and priotizing activities.
  • RISK MITIGATION: Identifies, assesses, communicates and prioritizes relevant risks in context of Cargill's broader risk environment.
  • COMPLIANCE REVIEW: Reviews and evaluates the effectiveness of internal processes and provides recommendations for improving efficiency and mitigating risks.
  • INVESTIGATIONS: Conducts investigations of alleged financial mismanagement, loss of assets or other irregularities, and prepares complex audit related documentation and reports to maintain compliance with organization and regulatory standards.
  • CONTINUOUS IMPROVEMENT: Stay aware of Corporate Audit strategy and objectives to identify and implement continuous improvement opportunities in our end-to-end audit processes.
  • REPORT DEVELOPMENT: Supports the preparation of clear, concise and well documented audit reports outlining issues, risks and recommendations and communicates audit results to management and partners, providing insights and actionable recommendations.
  • ISSUE RESOLUTION: Leads the process of issue identification, analyses root cause, provides recommendations for issue resolution, monitors management action plans, and performs testing to validate issue closure.
  • CONSULTATION: Responds to questions from audit teams and business leaders. Works collaboratively in a cross-functional audit team to ensure effective coordination of audit activities and full leverage of all audit tools and skills, such as leveraging Audit's data analytics experts.
  • STAKEHOLDER PARTNERSHIP: Works with management and staff to acquire necessary information, supports audit activities and provides advisory services to management regarding the design and implementation of internal controls.

Qualifications

MINIMUM & TYPICAL YEARS OF WORK EXPERIENCE

  • Minimum requirement of 4 years of relevant work experience, Typically reflects 5 years or more of relevant experience.
  • Proven experience in commodity risk management processes (e.g position reporting, trading P&L, forex exposure reporting, etc.).
  • Fluent Mandarin and English.
  • Intent to rotate back into business or function after a 2-4 years experience in Corporate Audit.
  • Ability to travel internationally up to 25% of time, including periodic trips spanning 2 full working weeks.

Preferred Qualifications

  • Previous audit experience
  • SAP experience

Protect yourself against recruitment fraud.

Cargill will not ask for money, processing fees, or bank information as a pre-condition of employment.

We are aware that unauthorized individuals may have posed as Cargill recruiters, made contact about job opportunities, and extended job offers via text message, instant message or chat rooms.

To ensure a job posting is legitimate, it must be listed on the Cargill.com/Careers website. Learn how to protect yourself from recruitment fraud.

companyDescription:
The Kraft Heinz Company is one of the largest food and beverage companies in the world, with eight $1 billion+ brands and global sales of approximately $25 billion. We’re a globally trusted producer of high-quality, great-tasting, and nutritious foods for over 150 years. While Kraft Heinz is co-headquartered in Chicago and Pittsburgh, our brands are truly global, with products produced and marketed in over 40 countries. These beloved products include condiments and sauces, cheese and dairy, meals, meats, refreshment beverages, coffee, infant and nutrition products, and numerous other grocery products in a portfolio of more than 200 legacy and emerging brands.

We spark joy around mealtime with our iconic brands, including Kraft, Oscar Mayer, Heinz, Philadelphia, Lunchables, Velveeta, Maxwell House, Capri Sun, Ore-Ida, Kool-Aid, Jell-O, Primal Kitchen, and Classico, among others. 

No matter the brand, we’re united under one vision: To sustainably grow by delighting more consumers globally. Bringing this vision to life is our team of 37,000+ food lovers, creative thinkers, and high performers worldwide. Together, we help provide meals to those in need through our global partnership with Rise Against Hunger. We also stand committed to responsible, sustainable practices that extend to every facet of our business, our consumers, and our communities. Every day, we’re transforming the food industry with bold thinking and unprecedented results. If you share our passion – and are ready to create the future, build a legacy, and lead as a global citizen – there’s only one thing to do: join our table and let’s make life delicious!

descriptionHtml:
Job Description

This role is set to drive complex service experience initiatives across the Globe to optimize experience for Kraft Heinz’s consumers, customers, suppliers and employees across multiple delivery locations (US, EU, India, Mexico) and languages.

What's on the Menu?

  • Identify and lead service experience driven transformational initiatives
  • End-to-end ownership of the service experience roadmap for the consumer facing vertical
  • Leading identified platform and tooling improvements and - where necessary - discovery, implementation, testing and full rollout to the fulfillers
  • Establish WFM for Tier 1 volumes across the support footprint, including identification and implementation of any necessary tooling
  • Establish and rollout quality audit practices for Tier 1 volumes across the support footprint, including identification and implementation of any necessary tooling


Recipe for Success Apply now if this sounds like you!

  • Master’s degree
  • 10+ years of experience in customer experience, consumer operations or general operations
  • Proven track record of leading complex global cross-functional projects
  • Hands-on, skilled at managing multiple stakeholders and building relationship across the Globe, ability to work under pressure and tight timelines
  • Strong analytical acumen and ability to tell a story with data
  • Continuous improvement mindset and strong advocacy for the customer (Lean Six Sigma knowledge is a plus)
  • Ability and willingness to travel


We hope to find you a seat at our table!

Location(s)

Amsterdam

descriptionHtml:
This is an interesting opportunity for a Risk Professional who is ready for the next step in his/her career, and is ready to take on more responsibility and create real impact.


Organisation

Norinchukin Bank Europe N.V. (NBE) established its office in Amsterdam in 2019 as a subsidiary of one of Japan's largest and oldest financial institutions. With its origins rooted in agriculture, fishery, and forestry, Norinchukin Bank was founded in 1923 as a cooperative bank. They form a dedicated team and are actively expanding their presence in Europe, placing a strong emphasis on sustainability. They foster an open and informal culture by continuously demonstrating their values of openness, sensitivity, engagement and cooperation.


Position

As a Non-Financial Risk Manager at NBE, you will be responsible for coordinating and enhancing the non-financial risk management. In this position you will work closely with senior management and various lines of defense to effectively manage risks and increase risk awareness across the organization.


You will collaborate with three General Risk Managers to ensure an integrated risk management approach, and you ensure compliance with relevant regulatory and legislative requirements while managing stakeholder interests effectively. You take initiative to resolve cross-divisional issues and implement improvements, and you naturally mentor and train junior team members.


Your main responsibilities are:


  • Strengthening Risk Management – Improve risk taxonomy, implement tools, and develop a company-wide risk grid;
  • Risk Framework & Compliance – Update policies and ensure regulatory adherence;
  • Strategic Risk Analysis – Support long-term risk planning and forecasting;
  • Training & Advisory – Guide first-line management on risk policies;
  • Stakeholder Collaboration – Work with compliance, audit, and UK teams on outsourced risks;
  • RCSA Implementation – Design and execute a new risk assessment methodology;
  • Monitoring & Reporting – Oversee key risk controls and prepare reports for senior management.


Profile

To be succesful in this role we are looking for someone who:


  • Has 7+ years of experience in risk management, with a strong focus on operational risk in banking;
  • Is proactive, results-driven, and able to execute effectively;
  • Shows strong leadership, planning, and stakeholder management skills;
  • Has in-depth knowledge of banking regulations, RCSA, and risk monitoring;
  • Is analytical yet hands-on;
  • Is culturally sensitive and internationally minded;
  • Shows passion for growth—both personal and team development.

descriptionHtml:
• Location: Amsterdam, Netherlands
  • Job type: Permanent, Full time

About The Job

At Sanofi we chase miracles of science to improve people’s lives. We are dedicated to making positive impact on the lives of the patients and families we serve, and we accomplish our goals through world-class research and with the compassion and commitment of our employees.

As we continue to transform the practice of medicine, to facilitate this transformation, Global Finance Services within Business Operations organization, is organized to drive to the next chapter of Sanofi’s strategy in centralizing our fragmented activities, modernizing our operations and focusing on performance & value delivery.

A2R (Accounts to Report) is an end-to-end process organization that consolidates the following activities: Fixed assets, Intercompany Accounting, Bank Accounting, General Ledger Accounting and closing, Cost Accounting, Tax accounting, Financial and Statutory Reporting and Master Data.

As the Head of A2R & Tax Compliance for Northern Europe you will be accountable for the end-to-end A2R operations, leading and delivering the best in class service and partnering with the CFO as well as relevant Finance and non-Finance stakeholders to ensure sustainable processes in Northern Europe (Belgium, Netherlands) and Nordics (Denmark, Sweden, Norway and Finland)) as per Core Model guidelines.

This role is located in Amsterdam Sanofi offices and is part of the A2R organization within the Global Finance Services (GFS) organization.

Main Responsibilities

  • Ensure a true and fair view for the financial statements for all Legal Entities under mentioned perimeter (in total 6 countries, 11 Legal entities). This includes oversight of the accounts managed by the Other Service Towers such as Source-to-Pay (S2P), Order-to-Cash (O2C) & Controlling (ConOps)
  • Accountable for the efficient, high-quality, and compliant delivery of an end-to-end A2R service, ensuring the achievement of KPIs and continuous performance improvement
  • Responsible for submission of all legal entity reports, for all given entities, to support release of all financial information to the general public
  • Prepare, advise and manage inventory related activities (i.e. inventory divestment, split valuation, yearly price change, stock equation, inventory provision review,..) with coordination of the Regional Hub (Budapest)
  • Ensures all accounts in given scope are stated in accordance with international (IFRS, IAS) and local Financial Reporting Standards and Sanofi Group Standards (SAGA)
  • Collaborate closely with GPO team and Project Managers to support the design and implementation of key A2R projects and standard processes within operations ensuring delivery for the expected benefits in terms of efficiency, cost, and quality
  • Create, understand and apply best practices from within GFS or external best-in-class service organizations
  • Ensure that Compliance and Internal Control/ SOX standards are met and ensure the local regulation adherence
  • Coordinates external audits since fees negotiation till completion of the audit with final audit memo ensuring promptly answer to Corporate and Auditors enquiries in coordination with Northern Europe Team members
  • Partner with CFO on MCO head level to support business development activities (strategic transactions) by providing expert technical accounting evaluation of potential acquisitions, strategic planning or other alliance transactions
  • Partner with HUBs (Budapest and Hyderabad) to support IFRS/ IAS/ SAGA A2R activities (transactional accounting and company reporting) by providing documents and information to the service center
  • Create a culture of cooperation, collaboration and teamwork across A2R teams (in country or in Hubs) within given scope and ensure a smooth interface to the relevant stakeholders inside and outside of GFS
  • Ensure efficient, on time, high-quality HUBs transition management
  • Coordinate and Lead LE restructuring projects to ensure End-to-End implementation
  • Accounts Committee preparation, coordination and formalization

The position also includes the relevant transactional and managerial tax activities:

  • Ensure timely and accurate quarterly reporting of tax balance and risks to the Sanofi group (IFRS)
  • Manage and/or co-ordinate the preparation of the statutory accounts all Legal Entities in their perimeter (Nordics managed by Hub in Budapest) and external statutory audit process including tax figures review for inclusion in the NE statutory accounts and NE corporation tax returns for each country
  • Ensure timeliness and accuracy for Tax Returns to the authorities
  • Review required SOX controls for tax and transfer pricing issues, ensuring they are up to date with changes in the operating business and proper fluent communication with Nordics Head of Tax and Belgium & Netherlands Tax Head
  • Review true ups to current and deferred tax balances following submission of CIT returns to tax authorities
  • Validate tax payments on account for Northern Europe
  • Liaise with the Central Transfer Pricing team to ensure Northern Europe transfer prices are aligned with group policy
  • Support BENL and Nordics team on transfer pricing calculations and documentation preparation for the authorities, including data reconciliations to final statutory accounts for the local transfer pricing file

Stakeholders’ Management

  • Cultivates strategic partnerships: MCO CFO, Head of Tax UKIE and Nordics, and Head Tax BENL, Treasury, Finance Business Partners, Procurement, Finance-Planning and Analysis, Corporate Finance, Corporate Tax, Other Interco, IPFF or ad hoc demands from Country Lead, P&C, Legal, Sales Leadership, ITS, Supply Chain, Customer Service, Corporate Compliance, Industrial Affairs and R&D Finance to support integration and implementation of efficient process and policies
  • Sets up close relationships with regional and local stakeholders when needed to allow smooth communication, efficient change management and issue resolution (ie. Regional HUBs in Budapest or Global HUB in Hyderabad) or external partners (Tax Administration,...)
  • Manage and co-ordinate statutory accounts audit with external auditors Mazars for the assigned companies in the BENL and support co-ordination Nordics with the Hub leader for Nordics

People Management

  • Lead the A2R services organization with direct responsibility for A2R teams
  • Oversee 6 direct reports (2 in Belgium, 1 in Sweden, 3 in Netherlands)

Travel expectations

  • Travel as needed based on business and team requirements.
  • May include travel to Gelt, Geel offices

About You

  • Education:
    • Professional Accounting qualification
    • Degree level education or equivalent preferred

  • Experience:
    • Experience of working in the Pharmaceutical industry
    • 10 + years Finance experience
    • Experience in Tax topics
    • International background with exposure to multicultural environment.
    • Experience of working within a HUB / BPO model (transitions)
    • Experience of working with a professional team of tax professionals and accountants.
    • Team Management experience
    • Track record of having effectively managed large efficiency improvement programs with a multitude of individual projects and managed transformation with change management

  • Skills and behaviors:
    • Very good understanding of relevant Finance process including IFRS (ie IAS 12)
    • Very good program and project management skills (ability to identify problems and define solutions) incl. implementation plans in a structured manner)
    • Inventory management knowledge
    • Ability to think strategically and take a helicopter view, but also be able to dive deep into operational problems when required
    • Strong delivery orientation including development of business cases, planning, execution and measuring ROI
    • Problem solving and ability to exercise good business judgment in a fast-paced working environment with prioritizing capacity (complex tax matters)
    • Very good communicator with an ability to discuss on executive level within Sanofi and externally
    • Fluency in English, written and spoken
    • Leadership skills: ability to shape culture, shape GFS sense of belonging and motivate a large group of people to work towards a common set of goals
    • Good knowledge of the BE, NL tax legislation including WHT, VAT
    • Excellent IT skills (Excel including VLookups, Pivot Tables etc) or SAP
    • Ability to set ambitious goals and priorities, motivating the team towards established objectives
    • Ability to work independently with minimal supervision
Why choose us?

  • At Sanofi, we believe in continuous learning and development. We encourage innovative thinking and welcome fresh ideas for process improvements and system optimizations.
  • An international work environment, in which you can develop your talent and realize ideas and innovations within a competent team
  • An individual and well-structured introduction and training when you onboard
  • You can create your own career path within Sanofi. Your professional and personal development will be supported purposefully
  • As a globally successful and constantly growing company, Sanofi provides international career paths as well
  • We offer a dynamic work environment where your contributions will have a direct impact.
  • Discover endless opportunities to grow your talent and drive your career, whether it’s through a promotion or lateral move, at home or internationally.
  • Enjoy a thoughtful, well-crafted rewards package that recognizes your contribution and amplifies your impact
  • Drive implementation science, powerful crowdsourcing and open innovation, discovering the ways to bring science to live faster, contributing to a patient first mindset
  • Take good care of yourself and your family, with a wide range of health and wellbeing benefits including high-quality healthcare, prevention and wellness programs and at least 14 weeks’ gender-neutral parental leave

Pursue Progress. Discover Extraordinary.

Progress doesn’t happen without people – people from different backgrounds, in different locations, doing different roles, all united by one thing: a desire to make miracles happen. You can be one of those people. Chasing change, embracing new ideas and exploring all the opportunities we have to offer. Let’s pursue progress. And let’s discover extraordinary together.

At Sanofi, we provide equal opportunities to all regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, or gender identity.

We are proud to be a Disability Confident Employer, committed to offering interviews to candidates who request consideration under the Scheme and meet the minimum requirements for this role.

If you have a disability and require adjustments for the interview process, please email us at diversity.recruitmentUKIE@sanofi.com. We are dedicated to ensuring an inclusive and supportive experience for all applicants.

Watch our ALL IN video and check out our Diversity Equity and Inclusion actions at sanofi.com!

You’ll help push boundaries, challenge convention, and build smarter solutions that reach the communities we serve. Ready to chase the miracles of science and improve people’s lives? Let’s Pursue Progress and Discover Extraordinary – together.

companyDescription:
HIZKIA is a global provider of fine art logistics, transport, and collection management services.

When it comes to art, seeing is believing. You can think you’ve seen a painting after looking at a photo in a book, but there is no comparison to standing in front of that same painting and seeing the artist’s brush strokes on the canvas.

Art strengthens the mind: it inspires, intrigues, and connects us to new cultures, practices, and ideas. At HIZKIA, we believe this should be a universal experience, and aim to use our expertise to make art accessible to everyone, everywhere.

Operating from offices throughout Europe, we aim to facilitate this by offering museums, galleries, auction houses and individual collectors a wide range of specialist services. This includes art handling, transport, installation, project management and coordination, storage, and collection care management.

We stand for delivering the highest quality when it comes to managing and preserving our shared cultural heritage. This common ambition is reinforced by the diversity of our ten offices, each with their own areas of expertise, expanding our collective knowledge base.

You can find us in Amsterdam (NL), The Hague (NL), Willebroek (BE), Liège (BE), Paris (FR), Copenhagen (DK), Stockholm (SE), Cologne (DE), Berlin (DE), and Munich (DE).
companySlogan: Inspired by art, driven by service
descriptionHtml:
(Senior) Financial Accountant bij HIZKIA


HIZKIA biedt wereldwijd topkwaliteit in logistiek, kunsttransport en collectiebeheer aan musea, galeries, veilinghuizen en particuliere kunstverzamelaars. HIZKIA is de afgelopen 35 jaar uitgegroeid tot een onbetwist marktleider in Nederland en is een van de belangrijkste partners in het internationale kunsttransport. HIZKIA biedt uiteenlopende gespecialiseerde diensten: kunstverpakkingen, transport van antiek en kunst, verhuur van de kunsttransportkist Turtle, installatie van tentoonstellingen, restauratie en deskundig (advies over) beheer en behoud van collecties en de coördinatie van internationaal bruikleenverkeer en kunstopslag in onze depots in Europa.

Onze belangrijkste kernwaarde is: 'Inspired by art, driven by service'.


Wat doe je als (Senior) Financial Accountant bij HIZKIA?

De afdeling Finance administreert en verwerkt financiële en operationele gegevens zodat zij op tijd, volledige en betrouwbare informatie/sturingsgegevens kan aanleveren aan het management/directie, zodat de organisatie optimaal aangestuurd kan worden. Daarnaast faciliteren de ondersteunende functies de organisatie en personeel in het optimaal uitvoeren van zijn taken.


De (Senior) Financial Account heeft de verantwoordelijkheid om de financiële administratie te controleren en te muteren zodat de administratie juist, actueel en compleet is. Tevens heeft de (Senior) Financial Account een ondersteunende en uitvoerende rol in verbeterprocessen t.b.v. de financiële administratieve organisatie en de interne beheersing.


Taken en verantwoordelijkheden:

·      Het beheersen van alle voorkomende werkzaamheden binnen de financiële administratie;

·      Debiteurenbeheer;

·      Zorgdragen voor uitzoeken en aansluiten van W&V- en balansrekeningen;

·      Het controleren van de administratie;

·      Verantwoordelijk voor de vaste activa staat;

·      Het maken, controleren en verzenden van aangiftes inzake belastingdienst, enz.

·      Beantwoorden van financiële vragen m.b.t. debiteuren/crediteuren aan derden, zowel intern en extern;

·      Meewerken aan maandafsluiting en maandrapportage;

·      Intercompany aansluitingen;

·      Betalingsproces;

·      Controleren en goedkeuren van memoriaalboekingen;

·      Back-up voor het beheren, muteren en controleren van de schade en boete administratie;

·      Verantwoordelijk voor het declaratiesysteem; en zorgdragen verwerking en instrueren van gebruikers;

·      Verantwoordelijk voor het vastleggen van elk proces, werkinstructies en verzorgen van trainingen;

·      Back-up voor alle voorkomende werkzaamheden die nog niet benoemd of beschreven zijn.


Dit neem jij mee:

·      Je hebt relevante werkervaring in een soortgelijke functie;

·      MBO+ werk en denkniveau;

·      Je hebt sterke communicatieve vaardigheden in het Nederlands, Engels is een pre;

·      Je bent proactief, stressbestendig en kunt zowel zelfstandig als in teamverband goed functioneren;

·      Ruime kennis van Exact Globe en Microsoft Excel.


Wat bieden wij:

·      Een afwisselende en uitdagende functie voor 32-40 uur per week;

·      Veel ruimte voor eigen initiatief en de mogelijkheid om werkprocessen te optimaliseren;

·      Een marktconform salaris;

·      Een dynamische werkomgeving waarin geen dag hetzelfde is;

·      24 vakantiedagen en 3,5 ATV-dagen o.b.v. een fulltime dienstverband;

·      Een jaarcontract met de mogelijkheid tot een vast dienstverband;

·      Vitaliteitsbudget;

·      Geluksdag.


Herken jij jezelf?

Dan willen wij je graag ontmoeten! Solliciteer met je CV en motivatie via wouter.kanselaar@hizkia.com.

Voor aanvullende informatie kun je contact opnemen met Wouter Kanselaar, 06-83694299.

companyDescription:
Nebius builds infrastructure for the age of AI.
 
Built on large-scale GPU clusters deployed across Europe and the US, Nebius’s full-stack AI cloud platform combines the scale, flexibility and reliability of a hyperscaler with the power and performance of a supercomputer.

We serve a fast-growing ecosystem of AI innovators of all sizes — from startups to research institutes to enterprises — across sectors including healthcare and life sciences, robotics, finance and entertainment, as well as national AI programs.

Led by Arkady Volozh, the visionary entrepreneur and founder of Yandex, Nebius is built around the talents of hundreds of engineers with deep expertise in building world-class tech infrastructure, as well as an in-house AI R&D team. 

Headquartered in Amsterdam and listed on Nasdaq (NBIS), Nebius has a global footprint, with hubs across Europe, North America and the Middle East.

As well as our core AI infrastructure business, Nebius Group includes other companies growing under distinctive individual brands:
🚖 Avride develops autonomous cars and delivery robots for sectors such as ride-hailing, logistics, e-commerce, and food and grocery delivery.
📚 TripleTen is a leading edtech platform, specializing in reskilling and upskilling individuals for successful careers in tech.

We also own equity stakes in companies including ClickHouse and Toloka.
descriptionHtml:
Why work at Nebius

Nebius is leading a new era in cloud computing to serve the global AI economy. We create the tools and resources our customers need to solve real-world challenges and transform industries, without massive infrastructure costs or the need to build large in-house AI/ML teams. Our employees work at the cutting edge of AI cloud infrastructure alongside some of the most experienced and innovative leaders and engineers in the field.


Where we work

Headquartered in Amsterdam and listed on Nasdaq, Nebius has a global footprint with R&D hubs across Europe, North America, and Israel. The team of over 800 employees includes more than 400 highly skilled engineers with deep expertise across hardware and software engineering, as well as an in-house AI R&D team.


The Role

The Internal Audit Manager will support and maintain an effective and SOX-compliant internal control framework for a large international business. This role will contribute to risk governance and compliance efforts, working closely with key stakeholders to ensure operational efficiencies, adherence to company policies, and reduction of financial reporting misstatements. Internal Audit Manager supports the Internal Audit Department in executing the annual (SOX) audit plan. The Internal Audit Manager will be responsible for executing process walkthroughs, control testing, and reporting of audit observations within the audit team and to business management. Support the company in mitigating its main strategic, operational, compliance, and financial risks. Contribute to the continuous improvements of processes and controls (including SOX), and identify and share best practices


Your responsibilities will include

• Execute audits, ensuring proper coverage and consideration of auditing principles, risk assessment, audit timelines.

• Support the documentation of business objectives, key controls, and SOX test strategies to ensure that audit objectives align with broader business needs.

• Assess control design, execute testing, and identify control gaps.

• Work closely with both Internal Control and External auditors to ensure compliance and continuous improvement.

• In this role, you will play a hands-on key role in the annual SOx program including walkthroughs, testing and reporting to management. Furthermore, you will perform and report on operational audit engagements as part of the annual audit plan.

• Take active role in delivering the yearly Audit plan;

• Conduct interviews with employees and review documentation to gather information and evidence. Identify control deficiencies and make recommendations to improve controls and mitigate risks. Prepare clear and concise audit reports to communicate findings and recommendations to management.

Support the assessment of control design and execute testing of controls and identify gaps.



We expect you to have

• Master’s degree in Accounting or Economics.

• 6+ years of professional experience, with a minimum of 2 years in a SOX-compliant audit environment.

• Extensive knowledge of SOX, IFRS, US GAAP, Dutch corporate governance, and internal controls evaluation.

• Advanced understanding of internal audit methodologies and ability to assess risks, interpret complex issues, and provide recommendations to Senior Management.

• Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, with the ability to evaluate complex business processes and identify key controls.

• Excellent interpersonal and communication skills to collaborate effectively across teams.

• Fluency in English, both written and verbal.

• Strong analytical skills with a data-driven approach.

• Proactive problem-solving and ownership mindset.

• Ability to collaborate and influence senior stakeholders.

• Highly motivated and adaptable in a dynamic environment.

• Strong communication and networking abilities.


What we offer

  • Competitive salary and comprehensive benefits package.
  • Opportunities for professional growth within Nebius.
  • Hybrid working arrangements.
  • A dynamic and collaborative work environment that values initiative and innovation.


We’re growing and expanding our products every day. If you’re up to the challenge and are excited about AI and ML as much as we are, join us!

companyDescription:
Infosys Consulting is a global management consulting firm helping some of the world’s most recognizable brands transform and innovate. Our consultants are industry experts that lead complex change agendas driven by disruptive technology.  With offices in 20 countries and backed by the power of the global Infosys brand, our teams help the C-suite navigate today’s digital landscape to win market share and create shareholder value for lasting competitive advantage.  

To see our ideas in action, or to join a new type of consulting firm, visit us at www.InfosysConsultingInsights.com.
descriptionHtml:
About Us

At Infosys, we are committed to be an equal opportunity employer, and to provide a work environment free of discrimination and harassment.

Infosys is recognized as one of the UK's top firms by the Financial Times and Forbes due to our client innovations, our cultural diversity and dedicated training and career paths. Management Consulting Magazine named us on their list of Best Firms to Work for. Furthermore, Infosys has been recognized by the Top Employers Institute, a global certification company, for its exceptional standards in employee conditions across Europe for five years in a row.

The Role:

We are seeking a highly experienced Senior Principle/Principle with a strong background in Communication, Media and Technology and business consulting to lead strategic projects within our CMT division. The ideal candidate will bring a blend of industry expertise, leadership skills, and a consulting mindset to drive Regulatory and Compliance initiatives, optimize operations, and guide cross-functional teams toward achieving operational excellence.

Key Responsibilities:

  • Establishment of Compliance Office:
  • Develop and implement the structure of the compliance office, outlining reporting lines, key responsibilities, and workflow processes
  • Collaborate with cross-functional teams to integrate compliance into daily operations seamlessly
  • Compliance Framework:
  • Identify and define the scope of compliance areas, including finance, legal, regulatory, SOC (Service Organization Control), security, and entertainment and connectivity regulations
  • Design and implement policies and procedures to ensure adherence to entertainment and connectivity regulatory compliance requirements
  • Entertainment and Connectivity Regulatory Compliance:
  • Stay informed about industry-specific regulations governing entertainment content delivery and connectivity services
  • Develop strategies and processes to ensure compliance with entertainment and connectivity regulations applicable to our platform
  • Security Compliance:
  • Stay informed about industry-specific security standards and regulations
  • Develop and implement security compliance programs to safeguard our Entertainment Platform against potential security threats
  • Audit Management:
  • Conduct regular audits to assess compliance with internal policies, legal requirements, industry standards, security protocols, and entertainment and connectivity regulations
  • Analyze audit findings and work closely with relevant stakeholders to address and rectify any non-compliance issues
  • Certification Programs:
  • Drive external certification processes, ensuring the platform meets industry-specific standards, security, and entertainment and connectivity regulations
  • Stay informed about changes in regulations and industry standards, adjusting certification strategies accordingly
  • Team Leadership:
  • Build, lead, and mentor a high-performing compliance team
  • Provide guidance and training to team members on compliance, security, and entertainment and connectivity regulatory matters, fostering a culture of accountability and awareness
  • Collaboration and Communication:
  • Collaborate with internal departments, legal teams, and external consultants to stay abreast of regulatory changes, potential security threats, and their impacts on the business
  • Communicate compliance, security, and entertainment and connectivity regulatory requirements and updates effectively to all relevant stakeholders

Qualifications:

  • Education:
    • Bachelor's or Master's degree in Engineering, Infomation Technology, or a related field.
  • Experience:
    • 10+ years of experience in setting up and managing compliance functions, with a focus on security, entertainment, and connectivity regulatory compliance, preferably within the cable or telecommunications industry
    • Strong understanding of finance, legal, regulatory, SOC, security, and entertainment and connectivity compliance requirements
    • Experience in leading and motivating cross-functional teams
    • Excellent communication and interpersonal skills
    • Familiarity with industry-specific security certifications and audit processes

What We Offer:

  • Competitive salary and performance-based bonuses
  • Opportunities for professional growth and career advancement
  • Flexible work hours and the possibility of hybrid work
  • A collaborative and innovative work environment with a team of industry experts

Why Join Us?

  • At Infosys, we believe in empowering all individuals, regardless of gender, background, or experience level. Women represented 22.2% of our Board and 39.3% of our workforce, that spans over 156 different nationalities and more than 56 countries worldwide.
  • We make investments in key initiatives that support our mission of driving gender equality across our business through custom programs for our consultants such as Advance and Everywoman, offering skill-building workshops, and cross-company network events connecting women, businesses, and organizations all over the world
  • We foster a supportive environment with employee resource groups dedicated to various aspects of diversity and inclusion, including multicultural ERG (employee resource group) focusing on; Family Matters, Health and Wellness, Disabilities, and LGBTQ, ensuring everyone feels valued and supported
  • Be part of a diverse and inclusive team that thrives on innovation and collaboration. Discover our DE&I values further here
